jobs.title: Marketing Automation Senior Specialist (HubSpot)
jobs.description:
**About Smile Source**

Smile Source helps independent dental practices reduce overhead and increase profitability. By pairing purchasing power with business coaching, education, and peer support, we help independent dentists grow confidently without selling, partnering, or giving up control.

**Position Overview**

The HubSpot CRM Marketing Manager will lead the strategy, optimization, and day\-to\-day management of HubSpot across the Smile Source portfolio. This role is responsible for CRM architecture, marketing automation, campaign execution, lead nurturing, reporting, and aligning marketing with sales initiatives.

This position combines marketing systems management, data analytics, and hands\-on execution to drive measurable results in member acquisition, retention, and partner engagement.

**Key Responsibilities**

**HubSpot CRM Strategy \& Management**

* Own and manage the HubSpot CRM marketing platform, ensuring data integrity, workflow automation, website page, form, and campaign management
* Design and implement automated marketing workflows, email nurture sequences, and segmentation strategies.
* Develop CRM architecture to support lead nurturing funnels, onboarding processes, and partner communications.
* Maintain dashboards and reporting to track pipeline performance, campaign ROI, and growth metrics.

**Marketing Automation \& Campaign Execution**

* Organize and execute integrated marketing campaigns using HubSpot tools (email, landing pages, forms, automation, reporting).
* Manage lead scoring models and lifecycle stages to improve marketing\-to\-sales alignment.
* Optimize conversion rates across digital channels and landing pages.
* Support paid media, SEO/SEM, and content initiatives through CRM integration and tracking.

**Data \& Analytics**

* Define KPIs for marketing and growth initiatives.
* Analyze CRM and campaign data to provide actionable insights and recommendations.
* Deliver weekly \+ monthly performance reports to leadership.

**Cross\-Functional Collaboration**

* Partner with marketing strategy, sales, and operations teams to align CRM processes and growth objectives.
* Train internal teams and partners on CRM best practices and usage.
* Serve as the internal HubSpot subject matter expert for marketing and sales

**Vendor \& Budget Management**

* Coordinate with external marketing agencies or HubSpot consultants when needed.
* Manage assigned budget and technology investments to maximize ROI.

**Qualifications**

**Required**

* Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Data Analytics, Business, or related IT field.
* 3\+ years of marketing experience with direct HubSpot CRM management.
* 5\+ years of strong experience in marketing automation, segmentation, and email campaign strategy.
* Proficiency in CRM reporting, dashboards, and analytics.
* Excellent project management and communication skills.
* This is a mostly remote position, with minimal travel required for team events.

**Preferred**

* HubSpot certifications (Marketing Hub, Sales Hub, or CRM Implementation).
* Experience in dental, healthcare, or service, or subscription\-based marketing or sales
* Knowledge of paid media integration and lead attribution modeling.
* Experience aligning CRM systems with sales or franchise/member networks.

Pay: $80,000\.00 \- $90,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* Dental insurance
* Employee discount
* Flexible schedule
* Flexible spending account
* Health insurance
* Health savings account
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Parental leave
* Retirement plan
* Vision insurance

Work Location: Remote

jobs.title: CRM & Marketing Data Specialist
jobs.description:
Description:
**Position Overview:**


The CRM \& Marketing Data Specialist manages the organization’s CRM database and supports member onboarding, account administration, and dues processing. This role ensures data accuracy, maintains compliance with CRM best practices, and coordinates closely with Membership, Accounting, and the Senior Leadership Team to keep member records current and properly documented. The position also supports onboarding processes, permissions management, and ongoing process improvements to enhance operational efficiency and the overall member experience.

**Duties \& Responsibilities:**

**CRM \& Database Management**

* Maintain and update the CRM member and partner database in accordance with organizational standards and established CRM best practices.
* Enter and manage data as outlined by CRM department processes, ensuring complete and accurate account and contact records.
* Establish new member accounts, including listing creation/activation, contact management, and permissions.
* Update, audit, deactivate, or eliminate outdated accounts to ensure database accuracy.
* Use CRM “traces” to document and track completed and outstand tasks (e.g., onboarding, paperwork, follow\-ups).
* Monitor CRM inquiries, troubleshoot issues via Simpleview ticketing, and support staff with CRM/CMS needs.
* Track web metrics using Google Analytics and maintain working knowledge of Simpleview functionality.

**Member Onboarding \& Accounting Administration**

* Streamline and follow established processes for new member onboarding and orientation.
* Onboard member contacts, manage permissions, and ensure accurate representation across all channels.
* Facilitate or coordinate Extranet and member benefit trainings.
* Provide Accounting with new member documentation and coordinate updates related to payments and membership status.
* Send internal notifications regarding new or updated member accounts and follow up on missing data as needed.
* Collaborate with Membership to ensure member records remain current throughout the year.

**Content \& Member Engagement**

* Review membership dues processes including invoicing, follow\-up statements, suspension notices, and termination letters.
* Collaborate with the Vice President of Membership and Community Relations and committees regarding removals, reinstatements, and due waivers.
* Maintain accurate documentation of all dues\-related communications and actions.

**Process Improvement \& Benefits Administration**

* Collaborate annually to update member benefits sheets.
* Adhere to CRM best practices, maintain thorough documentation, and identify opportunities to improve workflows and onboarding processes.

**Accounting**

* Accurately enter and manage invoices in Certify AP, including those received electronically or via mail, ensuring proper coding and timely processing.
* Support the annual membership billing process by preparing and issuing invoices to members.
* Record all incoming payments received through the mail in the check log, scan checks, and deliver them to the Accounting department for further processing.
* Maintain and organize physical and electronic filing systems by accurately filing documents and scanning records for electronic storage as required.
* Professionally communicate with members, customers, and vendors regarding payment collections, W\-9 requests, or other inquiries, ensuring clear and timely responses.


Perform other duties as assigned.


Requirements:
**Job Skills \& Qualifications:**

* Proficient in CRM/CMS platforms (e.g. Simpleview) and content management.
* Proficient in Microsoft Suite.
* Strong organizational skills with attention to detail for managing listings, inventory, and office operations.
* Excellent customer service and professional communication skills with members, vendors, and staff members.
* Experience leading trainings, troubleshooting issues, and supporting multiple departments collaboratively.
* Knowledge with web analytics (Google Analytics), content calendars, and marketing promotions.
* Ability to multitask, prioritize, and maintain accuracy in a fast\-paced environment.
* High school diploma required, Associates or Bachelor’s in Business, Marketing, Accounting, or related field preferred.

**Work Environment \& Schedule:**

* Office based, professional, and fast\-paced with interaction across teams, members, and vendors.
* Frequent use of computers, phones, and office equipment; occasional handling of mail, documents, and inventory.
* Part\-time, standard business hours, occasional flexibility for evening events and weekends
* Administrative, CRM, and data entry tasks throughout the day.
* Full\-Time, Monday\-Friday, 9:00 am \- 5:00 pm

jobs.title: Marketing Operations Specialist
jobs.description:
**Benefits:**
* Competitive salary
* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Vision insurance
  

**MARKETING OPERATIONS SPECIALIST**

  

Full\-Time \| Brentwood, TN \| FourPoint Business, LLC  

  

  

**Department:** Marketing  


  

**Reports To:** Marketing Department Head  


  

**Employment Type:** Full\-Time  


  

**Location:** Brentwood, TN (on\-site) \& Remote  

  

  

**ABOUT FOURPOINT BUSINESS**  

  

  

FourPoint Business is an integrated back\-office services company serving small and mid\-sized businesses throughout Middle Tennessee and beyond. Our service lines include Marketing, Bookkeeping, Consulting, and Managed IT, all delivered under one roof. We target businesses in the $1 to $5M revenue range that need enterprise\-level systems without the overhead of building them internally.  

  

  

Our marketing team handles everything from website design and paid advertising to SEO, content marketing, and client reporting. We work with a portfolio of active clients while also regularly engaging new prospects through structured discovery and analysis processes.  

  

  

**ROLE OVERVIEW**  

  

  

The Marketing Operations Specialist is a hands\-on execution role responsible for managing day\-to\-day marketing deliverables across our client portfolio and internal brand. You will own the production and delivery of client work, including website projects, paid ad campaigns, SEO, content, email, and reporting. This role requires a generalist who is comfortable moving between platforms and project types without losing attention to quality or deadlines.  

  

  

You will work closely with the Marketing Department Head and coordinate with the sales team to support the full client lifecycle, from pre\-sale analysis to onboarding to ongoing monthly services.  



  

  

**CORE RESPONSIBILITIES**  

  

  

**Website Design and Development**  

* Build and manage client websites on WordPress
* Execute website redesigns from kickoff through launch, following established project timelines
* Handle on\-page SEO setup, including meta tags, schema, and page structure
* Manage ongoing website maintenance, updates, and hosting for active clients

  

  

  

**Search Engine Optimization**  

* Execute monthly local SEO services including on\-page optimization, citation building, and keyword tracking
* Coordinate with content writers to ensure blog posts are properly formatted, SEO\-optimized, and published correctly
* Monitor and report on keyword rankings and organic traffic performance for client accounts
* Manage and optimize Google Business Profiles for clients, including updates, posts, and review monitoring

  

  

  

**Paid Advertising**  

* Build, manage, and optimize Google Ads campaigns for client accounts
* Build, manage, and optimize Meta (Facebook and Instagram) ad campaigns
* Set up proper UTM tracking and ensure campaign data flows correctly into Google Analytics 4
* Prepare monthly performance summaries and flag optimization opportunities

  

  

  

**Email Marketing and Content**  

* Build and send client email campaigns, newsletters, and outreach sequences
* Format blog posts in HTML using the established three\-box structure (title and slug, meta details, content)
* Assist in writing or editing marketing copy for landing pages, ads, emails, and social posts
* Develop and maintain content calendars for active clients

  

  

  

**Reporting and Analysis**  

* Produce formatted marketing analysis reports for prospective clients, covering website audits, SEO performance, competitive landscape, and Google Business Profile assessment
* Compile monthly client performance reports drawing from GA4, Google Ads, Meta Ads Manager, and search ranking data
* Support the discovery process by pulling organic traffic data, competitor research, and website analysis ahead of prospect meetings

  

  

  

**Client and Project Coordination**  

* Manage active project timelines and communicate status updates to internal stakeholders
* Support client onboarding using the established 30\-minute kickoff framework and intake process
* Draft Scopes of Work, proposal line item descriptions, and follow\-up emails using GoHighLevel
* Coordinate with the sales team to ensure proposals are accurate and delivered on time

  

  

  

**TOOLS AND PLATFORMS**  

  

  

Proficiency in the following tools is required or strongly preferred:  


  

* GoHighLevel (CRM, proposals, invoicing, email)
* WordPress (Elementor, WPBakery, or similar page builders)
* RankMath or Yoast SEO
* Google Analytics 4 and Google Tag Manager
* Google Ads Manager
* Meta Ads Manager (Facebook and Instagram)
* Adobe Programs including Photoshop, Premiere Pro, and Lightroom
* Canva or equivalent design tool
* Google Search Console
* Microsoft Office or Google Workspace

  

  

  

**QUALIFICATIONS**  

  

  

**Required:**  

* 2 or more years of hands\-on digital marketing experience covering multiple service areas (SEO, paid ads, web, or email)
* Demonstrated experience managing client work in an agency or multi\-client environment
* Working knowledge of WordPress website management and basic HTML
* Familiarity with Google Ads and Meta Ads campaign setup and optimization
* Ability to produce professional written deliverables including reports, emails, and SOWs
* Strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple active projects simultaneously

  

  

  

**Preferred:**  

* Experience with GoHighLevel or a comparable CRM and proposal platform
* Familiarity with local SEO best practices including citation management and Google Business Profile optimization
* Experience using GA4 for campaign tracking and reporting
* Exposure to the EOS (Entrepreneurial Operating System) framework

  

  

  

**WHAT WE OFFER**  


  

* A direct role with real ownership. You will manage active client accounts, not just support tasks.
* Variety. No two weeks look exactly the same across our client portfolio.
* A team that values systems, clarity, and doing the work well. We run on EOS.
* Competitive compensation based on experience.
* Room to grow within the marketing department as FourPoint continues to scale.

jobs.title: Marketing Operations Senior Associate
jobs.description:
We’re building a world of health around every individual — shaping a more connected, convenient and compassionate health experience. At CVS Health®, you’ll be surrounded by passionate colleagues who care deeply, innovate with purpose, hold ourselves accountable and prioritize safety and quality in everything we do. Join us and be part of something bigger – helping to simplify health care one person, one family and one community at a time.

**Position Summary**

**This role is required be based in our New York City office, generally \~3 days a week on‐site.**


The Marketing Operations Senior Associate is the center of project management workstreams in the NYC\-based "Behavior Change Pods" Agile marketing organization. The focus of this role will be delivering Aetna and Caremark member communications aimed at helping members get healthier while reducing their medical costs. Using advanced analytics and cutting\-edge marketing tools, the Behavior Change Pods help members manage their health and navigate the complex care system in a friendly way.


This team works to deliver highly valued programs for the enterprise. The colleague in this role will collaborate with a cross\-functional team of marketers, martech specialists, data scientists, data engineers, and product owners to ensure the timely launch of campaigns.


Responsibilities include, but are not limited to:

* Support multiple enterprise programs each with their own processes and requirements.
* Partner with different operations managers and product teams to build project plans and coordinate between on timelines, budgets, and requirements.
* Traffic campaigns through approvals and legal / regulatory reviews through different processes for different business units.
* Manage vendor interactions for direct mail, phone calls, and innovation tech partnerships.
* Execute Quality Assurance / User Acceptance Testing for campaigns, in partnership with a Salesforce Marketing Cloud Architect and data partners.
* Conduct ongoing monitoring for active campaigns, maintaining deliverability reports and escalating to leadership as needed.
* Support general project management and co\-develop key artifacts and processes internally to ensure the Pod is a “well\-oiled machine.”
* Manage Pod finances and other reporting / dashboards.

 **Required Qualifications**

* 3\+ years in a marketing operations role in a large corporation (ideally working with some combination of email, direct mail, SMS, IVR/phone, paid media, web).
* 2\+ years of project management experience, with desire to stretch into a marketing environment or 1\+ years of general marketing strategy/operations experience or consulting experience.

 **Preferred Qualifications**

* Experience / familiarity with Workfront workflow management tool is strongly preferred!
* Some professional experience with PowerPoint (building deliverables / communication materials) and Excel (basic manipulation, pivots, reporting, etc.).
* Strong attention to detail, flexibility, and ability to learn quickly and prioritize are all critical for this role.
* Experience working in an Agile methodology and/or rapid test and learn environment (either directly with marketing campaigns or in a product development environment) is preferred, but not required.
* Experience for healthcare industry is preferred, but not required – but a passion for healthcare is necessary!

 **Education**


Bachelor's Degree in marketing preferred or equivalent (HS Diploma \+ 4 years of relevant experience)

**Anticipated Weekly Hours**


40**Time Type**


Full time**Pay Range**


The typical pay range for this role is:


$50,188\.00 \- $122,400\.00
This pay range represents the base hourly rate or base annual full\-time salary for all positions in the job grade within which this position falls. The actual base salary offer will depend on a variety of factors including experience, education, geography and other relevant factors. This position is eligible for a CVS Health bonus, commission or short\-term incentive program in addition to the base pay range listed above.

  

Our people fuel our future. Our teams reflect the customers, patients, members and communities we serve and we are committed to fostering a workplace where every colleague feels valued and that they belong.

**Great benefits for great people**


We take pride in our comprehensive and competitive mix of pay and benefits – investing in the physical, emotional and financial wellness of our colleagues and their families to help them be the healthiest they can be. In addition to our competitive wages, our great benefits include:

* **Affordable medical plan options,** a **401(k) plan** (including matching company contributions), and an **employee stock purchase plan**.
* **No\-cost programs for all colleagues** including wellness screenings, tobacco cessation and weight management programs, confidential counseling and financial coaching.
* **Benefit solutions that address the different needs and preferences of our colleagues** including paid time off, flexible work schedules, family leave, dependent care resources, colleague assistance programs, tuition assistance, retiree medical access and many other benefits depending on eligibility.


For more information, visit https://jobs.cvshealth.com/us/en/benefits


We anticipate the application window for this opening will close on: 04/01/2026
Qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with all federal, state and local laws.

jobs.title: REMOTE: Senior Marketing Operations Lead (Lifecycle & Automation)
jobs.description:
**THIS IS A REMOTE POSITION**



Marrina Decisions is seeking an experienced **Senior Marketing Operations Lead** to support one of our premium enterprise clients. This role is responsible for designing and optimizing marketing automation, lifecycle engagement programs, and data\-driven marketing operations that drive measurable business outcomes.



The ideal candidate combines **deep marketing automation expertise, strong analytical skills, and cross\-functional collaboration** to deliver scalable marketing programs that support client growth.



This role will work closely with **marketing, product, sales, and data teams** to improve lead management, lifecycle engagement, campaign automation, and reporting infrastructure.



Candidates must be **U.S. Citizens or authorized to work in the United States without corporate sponsorship**.



Responsibilities



Marketing Automation \& Lifecycle Programs


* Design and manage **lifecycle marketing programs** that support lead generation, activation, and engagement
* Build and optimize automated marketing campaigns across multiple channels
* Develop lifecycle workflows that improve **lead conversion, customer engagement, and retention**
* Implement segmentation and personalization strategies to improve campaign performance


Marketing Operations \& CRM Integration


* Manage marketing automation platforms such as **Marketo, HubSpot, or other MAP tools**
* Ensure proper **Salesforce CRM integration and data flow**
* Maintain and optimize **lead management, lead routing, and lifecycle models**
* Implement automation processes that improve marketing efficiency and scalability


Data, Reporting \& Performance Insights


* Build and maintain **marketing dashboards and reporting frameworks**
* Monitor campaign performance and key engagement metrics
* Partner with marketing and leadership teams to provide **data\-driven recommendations**
* Ensure strong data quality and governance across marketing systems


Collaboration \& Client Support


* Work closely with cross\-functional teams including **marketing, sales, product, and analytics**
* Translate business requirements into scalable marketing automation solutions
* Support enterprise client marketing initiatives and operational programs
* Provide strategic input to improve marketing processes and campaign performance


Required Qualifications


* **7–10 years of experience** in marketing operations, marketing automation, or lifecycle marketing
* Strong expertise with **marketing automation platforms (HubSpot, or similar)**
* Experience working with **Salesforce CRM and marketing system integrations**
* Experience building **lead management and lifecycle marketing frameworks**
* Strong analytical skills and experience with marketing performance reporting
* Ability to manage complex marketing programs in a **fast\-paced environment**
* Excellent communication and collaboration skills


Preferred Qualifications


* Experience supporting **enterprise marketing teams or agency clients**
* Experience working in **SaaS, technology, or subscription\-based businesses**
* Familiarity with **AI\-driven marketing tools and personalization platforms**
* Experience designing scalable marketing automation frameworks


Compensation Range


**$100,000 – $120,000 annually depends on location and experience**



This posted range represents Marrina Decisions’ good\-faith estimate for this remote role at the time of posting.



Final compensation will depend on:


* candidate experience
* geographic location
* qualifications
* alignment with internal compensation equity

  



Benefits



Marrina Decisions offers:


* Health Benefits
* Paid Vacation
* Paid Holidays
* Paid Sick Time
* Opportunity to work with innovative enterprise clients and leading technologies in the **MarTech ecosystem**


Marrina Decisions is an **Equal Opportunity Employer**.

jobs.title: Account Acquisition - AVP, Marketing Operations III
jobs.description:
### **Position Summary**


The Assistant Vice President of Marketing Operations and Supply Chain is responsible for leading the end‑to‑end operations that supports card manufacturing, direct mail production, procurement, and inventory management for all cardmember physical materials. This role oversees a cross\-functional team responsible for supply continuity, vendor performance, production accuracy, and creative workflow management. The AVP directs a multi‑million‑dollar spend, manages a complex vendor network, and ensures all operational processes meet regulatory standards. Success in this role requires strong operational leadership, cross‑functional alignment, risk mitigation, and the ability to maintain uninterrupted production across a multi‑source supply chain.
### **Summary of Essential Job Functions**

* Lead end‑to‑end operations for card manufacturing, direct mail production, procurement, and inventory management to ensure continuity, accuracy, and error‑free execution of all prospect and cardmember materials.
* Oversee supply and demand alignment for cards, paper, envelopes, and other inventory items to maintain uninterrupted production and supply continuity.
* Manage multi‑million‑dollar spend across Direct Mail and Supply Chain, including forecasting, accruals, invoice approvals, and spend tracking.
* Direct vendor management activities, including performance reviews, contract negotiations, SLA oversight, and issue escalation across a multi‑source supplier network.
* Ensure compliance with regulatory and audit requirements for card production, sensitive materials, and operational controls.
* Oversee production timelines, quality control, and vendor coordination to ensure all programs meet accuracy, delivery, and performance standards.
* Maintain and optimize postal discounts through USPS promotions and strategic mail delivery planning.
* Lead cross‑functional alignment with Compliance, Risk, Product, IT, and Marketing to ensure operational readiness for new initiatives and enterprise programs.
* Oversee the Traffic team responsible for routing, proofing, and managing creative and production workflows.
* Ensure all creative assets move through review, approval, and production processes on time and without errors.
* Manage multi‑source supply strategies to reduce operational risk and strengthen business continuity.
* Identify, assess, and resolve operational risks before they impact production, cardmember experience, or regulatory compliance.
* Partner with Product, Marketing, and Operations to forecast material needs for new product launches, reissues, and enterprise initiatives.
* Lead allocation management and prioritization during supply constraints, including vendor escalation and contingency planning.
* Provide executive‑level reporting on supply risk, vendor performance, operational stability, and budget performance.
* Maintain accurate documentation, SOPs, and process maps for all operational functions.


### **Position Requirements**

* 7–10\+ years of experience in Supply Chain, Direct Mail, Procurement, or Operations (financial services preferred).
* Proven experience managing teams and large vendor ecosystems.
* Strong analytical skills with the ability to manage complex budgets and forecasting models.
* Exceptional organizational skills and attention to detail.
* Ability to manage multiple high‑stakes priorities in a fast‑moving, highly regulated environment.
* Strong communication and relationship‑building skills across all levels of the organization.
* Experience with operational risk management and process optimization.

  

Credit One Bank, N.A. is a data\-driven financial services company based in Las Vegas. Founded in 1984, Credit One Bank offers a spectrum of credit card products for people in all stages of financial life. Credit One Bank is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion and does not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of age, race, religion, color, disability, sex, sexual orientation, or national origin. Reasonable accommodations can be made for those who require them, including access to job applications and workplace accommodations. Employment at Credit One Bank is based on mutual consent (also known as at\-will). This means that employees and the Bank may terminate the employment relationship at any time, with or without cause and with or without notice. Please contact the recruiter for this position to learn more. Credit One Bank does not accept unsolicited resumes from agencies and is not responsible for related fees.

jobs.title: Consulting: Manager, Marketing Data & Analytics
jobs.description:
**Who We Are**


Marketbridge is a leading integrated growth consulting and marketing services firm that accelerates performance from strategy through execution. We combine management consultancy with marketing agency expertise, all backed by marketing science, creative problem\-solving, and technological know\-how. Our integrated approach brings together teams, technology, data, strategies, processes, and plans to fuel sustainable revenue growth and build deeper, more resonant customer relationships. With a team of 310 professionals across global locations including Boston, D.C., San Francisco, Seattle, London, and Canada, Marketbridge partners with over 150 clients worldwide, including Amazon Web Services, AMD, MetLife, Flex and CERN.

 **Who We’re Looking For**


Marketbridge is looking for Marketing Analytics Manager to oversee client projects and deliver insights based on complex analysis. These projects will leverage diverse parts of your toolkit including marketing analytics, predictive modeling, and marketing framework development. The Marketing Analytics Manager will have ownership over challenging and multifaceted projects, while still retaining executional responsibilities.  



  

**Responsibilities**

* **Analytics Expertise:** Serve as a subject matter expert across our solutions offerings such as database marketing, marketing campaign reporting, investigative descriptive analytics, and propensity modeling while leading and individually contributing to analysis tasks including Excel, R, SQL, Python, and SAS
* **Storytelling \& Strategy:** Synthesize analyses, develop solutions and strategic frameworks, and conduct research to construct compelling, detailed, and actionable recommendations for our clients
* **Project and Client Management:** Primary point person responsible for engagement delivery, managing project timelines, workstreams, deliverables, and team members while maintaining client relationships across multiple key stakeholders (internal and external)
* **Mentorship \& Training:** Help develop and mentor junior staff through on\-the\-job, project\-based training

  

**Qualifications**


This position is for those with deep analytics experience who are looking for an opportunity to use data, research, and best practices to help improve clients’ sales and marketing strategies while increasing their project management responsibility. We are looking for someone who:

* Has 4\-5 years of experience in the fields of data\-driven consulting, market research, or other role where you analyzed data daily
* Prior consulting experience
* Can successfully manage challenging marketing and sales enablement projects
* Can use data to build a narrative with actionable strategic recommendations
* Possesses a strong quantitative mindset
* Demonstrates stellar account management, going above and beyond to surprise and delight clients

**Note:** This position requires the ability to work in the United States without visa sponsorship. Marketbridge is an Equal Opportunity Employer.  

  

**The ideal candidate will:**

* Have exceptional project management skills
* Be able to effectively manage and mentor a diverse project team
* Be a persuasive communicator and storyteller, in person and in writing
* Be extremely conscientious and organized
* Be proactive and start projects with little prodding

**Our Culture**


At Marketbridge, you’ll join a vibrant community of collaborative minds dedicated to reinventing growth. We celebrate curiosity, reward bold thinking, and empower you to take the lead in shaping innovative strategies. From day one, you’ll gain hands\-on experience, learn from supportive mentors, and engage in ongoing professional development through targeted training, skill\-building workshops, and leadership guidance. Our inclusive environment values each individual’s voice, fostering genuine connections and a sense of belonging. Here, you won’t just watch the industry evolve—you’ll help drive it, turning fresh ideas into measurable outcomes and building a career defined by meaningful impact and continuous growth.

**BENEFITS:**At Marketbridge, we support our team with benefits that prioritize well\-being, flexibility, and growth. Here’s what you can look forward to:


* ️ Time Off \& Flexibility – Flexible PTO, summer Fridays, and paid parental leave (up to 16 weeks for birthing parents). Plus, we’re closed from Christmas to New Year’s so you can fully unplug.
* Health \& Wellness – Comprehensive medical benefits and free premium access to the Calm app for relaxation and mindfulness.
* Financial Security – 401(k) with a 3% company contribution\* (US Only), life insurance, long\-term disability, and AD\&D coverage for extra peace of mind.
* Learning \& Growth – A professional development fund ($500\) and continuous learning programs to invest in your career.
* Tech \& Perks – Company\-provided laptop \& accessories, monthly Wi\-Fi \& cell stipend, and exclusive discounts through Perkopolis.

  

*The salary range for this role is $125,000 \- $175,000 with an eligible annual bonus of up to 20%.*

jobs.title: Senior HubSpot Administrator
jobs.description:
Collectors is the leading creator of innovative technology that provides value\-added services for collectors worldwide. We grade, authenticate, vault, and sell millions of record\-setting collectibles, all while modernizing and digitalizing the process to further our mission of helping collectors pursue their passions. We’re always on the lookout for talented people to join our growing team.
Our services span collectible trading cards, autographs, comic books, coins, video games, event tickets, and memorabilia. Our subsidiaries include PSA, PCGS, Beckett, SGC, and Card Ladder.
Since our founding in 1986, we have graded and authenticated millions of items. We employ more than 3000 people across our headquarters in Santa Ana, California and offices in New Jersey, Texas, Florida, Japan, Shanghai, Hong Kong, Canada, Mexico, Germany, and France.
We’re looking for a Senior HubSpot Administrator to join our Beckett team. You will serve as the primary leader of HubSpot across the organization. This role is responsible for overseeing system configuration, managing custom objects, leading integrations, and building automation and reporting. Acting as both a system owner and strategic partner, the administrator ensures HubSpot evolves with organizational needs and remains the unified CRM system of record for all brands and business units.  

You’ll report to the Director of Growth and Collector Experience and work from our Plano, TX office. Remote or hybrid candidates will also be considered. We believe that there is significant value in in\-person collaboration. If you live within a 1 hour commuting distance to one of our offices, you will be required to be onsite most of the time. This will be discussed further as part of the recruiting process.  

What You’ll Do:* System Administration \& Configuration: Own HubSpot setup, including managing users, permissions, custom objects, properties, and workflows.
* Integrations \& Data Management: Lead integrations with platforms such as DocuSign, Cornerstone, Business Central, and other business systems to maintain data integrity and cross\-functional visibility.
* Automation \& Optimization: Design, build, and optimize automation, pipelines, and reporting dashboards to streamline workflows and improve operational efficiency.
* Cross\-Functional Enablement: Partner with departmental leaders to define CRM requirements, implement best practices, and ensure consistent adoption of HubSpot across business units.
* Governance \& Standards: Develop and enforce CRM governance, ensuring data quality, standardized processes, and platform scalability.
* Onboarding \& Transitions: Play a key role in transitioning new brands and business units onto HubSpot, ensuring smooth onboarding and alignment with existing processes.
* Reporting \& Insights: Deliver accurate and timely reporting to leadership, empowering data\-driven decision\-making and performance tracking.
* Continuous Improvement: Stay up to date with HubSpot releases and CRM best practices, proactively identifying opportunities for platform improvement and new functionality.

  

Who You Are:* Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, Information Systems, or related field (or equivalent experience)
* 5 \- 8 years of experience administering HubSpot (or comparable CRM platform) in a multi\-department or multi\-brand environment
* Hands\-on experience with system configuration, workflow automation, custom objects, and reporting dashboards
* Proven track record of leading integrations (e.g., DocuSign, Cornerstone, ERP/Finance tools like Business Central)
* Strong understanding of CRM governance, data management, and automation best practices
* Ability to translate business needs into technical CRM solutions.
* Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to train and influence cross\-functional teams
* HubSpot certifications (preferred but not required)

  

Salary Range:*The salary range for this position is $100,000 \- $110,000\. Actual compensation on this range varies based on a variety of non\-discriminatory factors, including location, job level, experience, and skill set. This role may be eligible for bonuses, commissions, or other forms of compensation, please ask your recruiter for details.*
Collectors uses e\-Verify to validate your ability to work legally in the United States.
We are aware that there are instances where individuals are receiving job offers that fraudulently allege to be from Collectors or one of our business units. This type of fraud can be carried out through false websites, through fake e\-mails claiming to be from the company or through social media. We never ask for personal information such as your bank account, Social Security numbers or National IDs, nor do we send or request payments for the purchase of business\-related equipment. If you suspect fraud, please reach out to jobs@collectors.com.*We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ethnicity, ancestry, religion, national origin, gender, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital or parental status, disability, veteran status, or other class protected by applicable law. We believe that a team that represents a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and skills will better service the diverse community of collectors we support.**If you require an accommodation to apply or interview with us due to a disability or special need, please email* *people@collectors.com**.**U.S. residents: for disclosures relating to personal information we collect during the employment application and recruitment process, please see our**Privacy Notice for U.S. Applicants**.**If you are based in California, you can read information for California residents* *here**.*

jobs.title: Marketing Operations & Experience Intern
jobs.description:
**JOB DESCRIPTION**

**MARKETING OPERATIONS \& EXPERIENCE INTERN**

**CLASSIFICATION** Seasonal; Full\-time Summer Internship


(Approximately 40 hours per week for \~12 weeks, May–July with flexibility into August)

**FLSA** Nonexempt

**REPORTS TO** Program Experience Manager. Intern will work closely with New Strategic Business Department along with Marketing, Creative, \& Events Teams.

**LOCATION** Park City, Utah (Hybrid work schedule).


Interns will be responsible for their own housing and transportation arrangements

**SALARY $20 / hour**

 **SUMMARY / OBJECTIVE**


The Marketing Operations \& Experience Intern will support initiatives that enhance member engagement, marketing effectiveness, and customer experience at U.S. Ski \& Snowboard. Over the course of the summer, the intern will contribute to projects across email marketing, CRM segmentation, and member communications. Interns participate in a structured summer program that includes mentorship from department leaders, exposure to multiple teams across the organization, and professional development opportunities. The experience culminates in a final presentation highlighting project outcomes, insights, and recommendations.

 **About Us**:


U.S. Ski \& Snowboard is committed to lead, encourage, and support athletes in achieving excellence and making the United States of America the Best in the World in Olympic skiing and snowboarding. We are committed to fostering, cultivating and maintaining a culture of diversity, equity, and inclusion by creating an environment in which diversity is valued, equity is striven for, and inclusion is embraced.

 **ROLES \& RESPONSIBILITIES**

* Assist with member welcome kit development and production, including copywriting, project coordination, vendor communication, and fulfillment planning.
* Support email marketing initiatives including drafting content, building templates, assisting with campaign execution, and tracking campaign performance.
* Contribute to CRM and data projects within Salesforce, including customer segmentation and improving customer journey workflows.
* Provide customer service and engagement support, responding to inquiries and identifying trends that may improve the member experience.
* Assist with consumer\-facing initiatives and marketing projects, including occasional support for events or engagement campaigns.
* Support cross\-functional marketing and engagement initiatives as needed.

 **OTHER DUTIES**


Please note this job description is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities and activities may change at any time with or without notice.

 **ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS**


Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.


This job generally operates in a professional office environment. This role routinely requires effective communication with others using common telecommunications equipment and computer equipment. Must be able to read, examine, and organize documents written in English. May require the ability to sit, stand, and walk around for extended periods of time. May require work on weekends, nights, holidays, and on\-call basis.

 **COMPETENCIES \& EXPERIENCE**

* Current junior, senior, or recent graduate pursuing a degree in Marketing, Business, Communications, Data Analytics, Information Systems, or a related field
* Strong interest in marketing technology, CRM platforms and email marketing tools.
* Strong written communication skills and ability to create clear, engaging content.
* Demonstrated analytical thinking and interest in segmentation and data analysis.
* Strong attention to detail.
* Familiarity with Excel or Google Sheets for basic data analysis (sequel or python a plus).
* Experience with or interest in design tools such as Canva is preferred.
* Working knowledge of AI tools a plus.
* Ability to manage multiple projects and work effectively in a collaborative, fast\-paced environment.

 **U.S. SKI AND SNOWBOARD EEO STATEMENT**


U.S. Ski \& Snowboard is an Equal Opportunity Employer that does not discriminate on the basis of actual or perceived race, color, creed, religion, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, sex or gender (including pregnancy, childbirth and pregnancy\-related conditions), gender identity or expression (including transgender status), sexual orientation, marital status, military service and veteran status, physical or mental disability, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by applicable federal, state or local laws and ordinances. U.S. Ski \& Snowboard's management team is dedicated to this policy with respect to recruitment, hiring, placement, promotion, transfer, training, compensation, benefits, employee activities, access to facilities and programs and general treatment during employment.

jobs.title: Marketing Automation & Data Strategist
jobs.description:
With a strong reputation for integrity, innovation, and member focus, Oregon State Credit Union is one of the most respected credit unions in the Northwest. Guided by our vision to create financial solutions that make lives better, we are seeking a Marketing Automation \& Data Strategist to lead the development, execution, and optimization of data driven, automated marketing programs that enhance the member experience and drive growth.
  
  

Reporting to the Assistant Director of Marketing, the Marketing Automation \& Data Strategist plays a pivotal role in advancing member acquisition, onboarding, engagement, retention, and product adoption across owned marketing channels. This role serves as both the administrator of the marketing automation platform and the primary data analyst for the marketing department, translating complex data into actionable insights that inform strategy and decision making.
  
  

**What you will need to be successful:**  

**Marketing Automation Strategy \& Execution:**  

Proven ability to design, develop, and optimize highly segmented and personalized marketing automation campaigns and lifecycle journeys. Strong experience building workflows, triggers, dynamic content, audience rules, and frequency caps to drive measurable results across the member lifecycle.
  
  

**Audience Segmentation \& Personalization:**  

Demonstrated expertise building and maintaining targeted audience segments using behavioral, demographic, transactional, and product data. Strong focus on data hygiene, list accuracy, deliverability, and preference management best practices.
  
  

**Data Analytics \& Performance Measurement:**  

Advanced analytical skills with experience collecting, analyzing, and interpreting data to evaluate marketing performance. Ability to develop dashboards, reports, and KPIs to track engagement, conversions, ROI, attribution, and trends—and translate insights into strategic recommendations.
  
  

**Marketing Database \& Systems Management:**  

Experience administering a marketing database or CRM (MCIF or similar), ensuring data accuracy and completeness. Ability to partner with technology, data, and digital banking teams to strengthen integrations and expand automation capabilities across platforms.
  
  

**Cross Functional Collaboration \& Communication:**  

Strong written and verbal communication skills with the ability to translate data and technical concepts for non technical audiences. Proven ability to collaborate with brand, content, digital, design, technology, and leadership teams to deliver integrated marketing initiatives.
  
  

**Experience \& Education:**  

Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, e Commerce, or a related field, or equivalent experience. Three or more years of experience developing and managing marketing automation programs. Three or more years of experience in data analytics and reporting (may be concurrent). Advanced proficiency in Excel and business intelligence tools such as Tableau or Power BI.
  
  

**Ways we'll appreciate you:**  

* A collaborative and positive workplace culture
* Initial and on\-going support, training and mentorship with promotion opportunities
* Competitive wages, bonuses, paid holidays and paid time off
* 100% credit\-union\-paid employee coverage for medical, dental, vision, life and disability insurance; dependent coverage also available
* 401k Plan with an amazingly generous credit union match!
* Tax\-saving Flexible Spending and Health Savings Plans
* Premier membership perks, company clothing, wellness and recognition programs


Since it began in 1954, Oregon State Credit Union has been a member\-owned, not\-for\-profit financial services institution. We exist for the benefit of our member\-owners, and not to profit from them. That's what makes us different from banks and how we're able to create financial solutions that make lives better.
  
  

**Location:** 4800 SW Research Way, Corvallis
  
**Work type:** Full\-time, hybrid and Exempt
  
**Compensation:** $96,716k year DOE \+ bonus
  
  

Oregon State Credit Union is an equal opportunity employer, including veterans and individuals with disabilities.
  
  

The information above provides the general nature of work performed in this position and outlines primary qualifications. The successful candidate must be able to demonstrate that they have the legal right to work in the US and pass a background screening to the satisfaction of Oregon State Credit Union.

jobs.title: Marketing Operations Governance Manager, Vice President
jobs.description:
**JOB DESCRIPTION**  



On the Marketing Operations Team, you will collaborate extensively with cross\-functional teams including CMOs, Marketing Automation, Payments Operations, Controls, Legal, Compliance, and Finance to define and implement robust processes that enhance our marketing operations.




As a Marketing Operations Governance Manager on the Marketing Operations Team , you will establish and oversee risk management and compliance frameworks for Corporate and Investment Banking marketing. This strategic role ensures regulatory adherence, operational integrity, and effective execution of marketing processes, collaborating extensively with cross\-functional teams.


Job Responsibilities

* Provide strategic oversight of compliance for all marketing communications and enterprise\-wide initiatives.
* Govern marketing standards to maintain regulatory adherence across all channels and platforms.
* Partner with marketing teams to interpret and implement procedures for regulations such as CAN\-SPAM and list validation.
* Standardize and ensure compliance with accessibility requirements, including asset distribution.
* Analyze and address differences in legal and compliance processes across business lines.
* Manage regulatory compliance frameworks, including legal reviews and data privacy requirements.
* Develop and maintain standardized operational frameworks for marketing activities.
* Consolidate and harmonize procedures across the integrated CIB Marketing organization.
* Ensure consistent application and annual review of disclaimers and claims in all materials.
* Collaborate on compliance attestations and operational efficiency initiatives (e.g., SharePoint, RBAC).
* Oversee audit management, acting as liaison with Audit and coordinating audit requests.




**Required Qualifications, Skills and Capabilities**

* Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Business Administration, or related field
* 7\+ years of experience in Marketing operations experience
* Experience in marketing controls, compliance, or audit functions.
* Strong understanding of legal and compliance regulations affecting marketing practices
* Proven ability to manage multiple complex projects simultaneously while working collaboratively across diverse teams
* Excellent analytical, problem\-solving, and communication skills with demonstrated ability to influence stakeholders at all organizational levels


**ABOUT US**  


JPMorganChase, one of the oldest financial institutions, offers innovative financial solutions to millions of consumers, small businesses and many of the world’s most prominent corporate, institutional and government clients under the J.P. Morgan and Chase brands. Our history spans over 200 years and today we are a leader in investment banking, consumer and small business banking, commercial banking, financial transaction processing and asset management.
We offer a competitive total rewards package including base salary determined based on the role, experience, skill set and location. Those in eligible roles may receive commission\-based pay and/or discretionary incentive compensation, paid in the form of cash and/or forfeitable equity, awarded in recognition of individual achievements and contributions. We also offer a range of benefits and programs to meet employee needs, based on eligibility. These benefits include comprehensive health care coverage, on\-site health and wellness centers, a retirement savings plan, backup childcare, tuition reimbursement, mental health support, financial coaching and more. Additional details about total compensation and benefits will be provided during the hiring process.


We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants’ and employees’ religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation.


JPMorgan Chase \& Co. is an Equal Opportunity Employer, including Disability/Veterans

  

  

  

**ABOUT THE TEAM**  


J.P. Morgan's Global Banking business is one of the largest wholesale banking client franchises in the world. We serve clients, including corporations, governments, states, municipalities, healthcare organizations, education institutions, banks and investors.  

  

Commercial Banking provides credit and financing, treasury and payment services, international banking and real estate services to clients—including corporations, municipalities, institutions, real estate investors and owners, and not\-for\-profit organizations.

jobs.title: Director, Marketing Systems & Operations
jobs.description:
**Department:** Marketing and Communications  

  

**Reports To:** Chief Marketing Officer  

  

**FLSA Classification:** Exempt **FTE:** 1  

  

**Supervises:** None  

  

**Hybrid Schedule (subject to change):** In office two days a week, Tuesday and Thursday. Remote Monday, Wednesday, and Friday. This schedule may change based on department’s needs and meetings.  

  

**Position Budgeted:** $120,000\.00 to $150,000\.00  

**Position Summary:**  

  

The Director, Marketing Systems \& Operations is responsible for building, maintaining, and continuously improving the systems that support The Boston Foundation’s marketing and communications work. Reporting to the Chief Marketing Officer, this role is accountable for ensuring that TBF’s marketing technology, data, and workflows operate as a coordinated, reliable system in support of audience growth, donor engagement, and campaign readiness.  

  

This role focuses on the systems, data, infrastructure, and metrics tracking that enable effective marketing and communications across the organization. It emphasizes designing scalable, well\-governed operations—spanning Salesforce, Pardot, WordPress, and related platforms—so that teams have the tools, processes, and insights they need to execute their work efficiently and effectively. Success in this role requires strong technical fluency, systems thinking, and the ability to translate strategy into durable, organization\-wide marketing operations.


**Essential Functions:**  

  

Marketing Systems \& Infrastructure


* Manages day\-to\-day operation of TBF’s marketing operations ecosystem, including Salesforce (marketing use cases), Pardot, WordPress, and related integrations;
* Designs and maintains marketing systems that support audience growth, segmentation, personalization, and performance measurement; and
* Ensures marketing technology is used consistently and intentionally across teams.


Website Management \& Platform Migration


* Leads the migration of TBF.org from Sitecore to WordPress, in partnership with internal stakeholders and external vendors;
* Oversees ongoing website operations post\-migration, including regular updates to content on TBF.org and other affiliated sites, paired with governance, workflows, integrations, and sustainability; and
* Ensures the website platform supports frequent updates, campaign storytelling, accessibility, and integration with CRM (Customer Relationship Management platform), email and necessary third\-party web tools.


CRM, Email \& Data Architecture


* Responsible for marketing\-related data structures within Salesforce, including audience definitions, attributes, data governance, segmentation logic, and email templates;
* Oversee the production and distribution of multimedia content through Pardot for the Foundation and its key initiatives;
* Strengthens and maintains Salesforce–Pardot integration to support targeted, relevant communications; and
* Establishes and documents data governance practices, including field ownership, quality control, and maintenance standards.


Reporting, Measurement \& Optimization


* Develops and maintains dashboards and reports that track marketing performance across web (experience with Google Analytics), email, and social channels;
* Ensures leadership and teams have access to clear, trusted data to support decision\-making and continuous improvement; and
* Identifies opportunities to reduce manual work and improve efficiency through better system design.


Cross\-Functional Enablement


* Supports the cross\-functional Editorial Team by translating strategy into operational systems and technical requirements;
* Expertise with leveraging SEO (Search Engine Optimization) and AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) that optimizes and drives strategic content creation across web and overall online presence;
* Partners with Communications, Philanthropy Operations, IT (Information Technology), Public Affairs (and other departments as needed) to align tools, data, and workflows; and
* Provides guidance, standards, and support to teams using marketing systems, helping establish consistent practices over time.

  

  

*Other Duties and Responsibilities:*


* Individuals assigned to this position may perform other duties as assigned.

  

  

**Qualifications**  

  

*Preparation, Knowledge, Previous Experience:*


* Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience; and
* At least 5–8 years of experience in marketing operations, CRM management, or digital marketing systems.


*Skills, Abilities, Competencies:*


* Strong experience with Salesforce; marketing automation experience with Pardot strongly preferred;
* Experience with Google Analytics preferred;
* Experience managing websites and CMS (Content Management Systems); WordPress experience strongly preferred;
* Strong understanding of how CRM, email, and web platforms work together as a marketing system;
* Experience designing data structures, segmentation models, and reporting frameworks;
* Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills;
* Ability to translate strategy into scalable systems and workflows;
* Collaborative work style and comfort working cross\-functionally;
* Strong organizational judgment and attention to detail;
* Commitment to contributing to an organizational culture of learning and wellbeing; and
* Preference for awareness, understanding, and connection to the Boston community.

  

  

**Working Conditions \& Physical Demands:**


* Ability to work for long periods of time at a workstation;
* Ability to use a computer monitor and keyboard for long periods of time; and
* Ability to work onsite and remotely, as required.

  

  

*The content is intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by persons assigned to this job. It is not intended to constitute an exhaustive list of all responsibilities and duties required.*  

  

*External and internal applicants, as well as position incumbents, who are or become disabled as defined under the Americans with Disabilities Act or applicable state law, must be able to perform the essential functions of the job (including those listed above) either with or without reasonable accommodation. Reasonable accommodation, if any, will be determined by management in consultation with the employee on a case\-by\-case basis.*  

  

*This job description is intended to be general and may be revised from time to time. At management’s discretion, the employee may be assigned different or additional duties from time to time.*

The Director, Marketing Systems \& Op

jobs.title: Marketing Operations Manager
jobs.description:
Notre Dame Federal Credit Union humbly began in 1941 as a conversation between two professors from the University of Notre Dame. Now, over 75 years later, we have grown into a national, award\-winning financial institution serving over 56,000 members worldwide. We consider our partners (employees) to be one of our greatest assets to this success. We strive to make great things happen for our members and partners by changing one life every day. If you share that same passion for making an impact on someone’s life, you should apply to join the Notre Dame FCU family.


  
**PRIMARY FUNCTIONS:**

The Marketing Operations Manager will work interdependently with executives, directors, and managers to implement and execute local and national marketing plans, projects, and campaigns that bring brand and service awareness to current and potential members of the organization. This role serves as a key execution partner to the SVP of Marketing, with the authority to manage and execute a high volume of marketing initiatives independently. The Marketing Operations Manager translates strategic direction into actionable plans and ensures initiatives move from concept to completion with minimal executive oversight.  



**SPECIFIC DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:**

This position must effectively carry out the essential duties of this position in a manner that consistently demonstrates the core values of Notre Dame Federal Credit Union in a positive manner. This position requires thought and innovation, and one to think critically and creatively. This position also requires a high aptitude for learning. The duties identified below are the essential functions of the position. Other duties may be assigned.  



**Marketing Operations**

* Works in conjunction with the VP of Marketing to propose, develop, and implement marketing plans designed to meet the short and long\-term strategic goals of the credit union.
* Independently manage and execute high\-priority initiatives delegated by the SVP \& VP of Marketing.
* Translate executive\-level strategy into tactical project plans with clear timelines, resource allocation, and measurable outcomes.
* Develop, implement, and enforce standardized campaign intake, prioritization, and launch processes.
* Manage department\-wide campaign calendar and capacity planning to ensure alignment with strategic objectives.
* Rapidly reprioritize projects as organizational needs shift while maintaining clarity and accountability across the team.
Serve as the operational hub between Marketing, IT, Compliance, Branch Operations, and executive leadership.  
* 

**Marketing Technology**

* Maintain governance of marketing automation platforms (HubSpot experience strongly preferred).
* Own and administer the department’s project management system (Asana), including workflow architecture, templates, and task governance.
* Ensure proper tagging, segmentation, and UTM tracking discipline across digital campaigns.
* Partner with Business Intelligence and IT to ensure data accuracy and reporting integrity.
Identify opportunities to improve automation, efficiency, and reporting through technology.  
* 

**Campaign Execution Oversight**

* Oversee operational execution of campaigns across digital, print, email, social, and branch channels.
* Monitor campaign timelines, budgets, and resource allocation.
* Ensure campaign assets move efficiently through review, compliance, and launch processes.
Oversee vendor coordination related to print, promotional materials, and digital execution.  
* 

**Reporting \& Performance Measurement**

* Own campaign performance reporting and build standardized dashboards to track KPIs and ROI.
* Present clear, concise performance summaries to marketing leadership.
* Monitor marketing budget allocation and vendor spend pacing.
* Identify operational bottlenecks and implement process improvements.

  
**EDUCATION and/or EXPERIENCE:**

* A Bachelor’s Degree in Marketing, Business, Communications, or a related field is required.
* Candidates should have a minimum of 5 years of professional experience managing complex projects, operational workflows, or cross\-functional initiatives in a fast\-paced environment.
* At least 3 years of experience using project management software is required, with strong preference for advanced proficiency in Asana.
* Experience with marketing, particularly HubSpot, is strongly preferred. Prior experience in marketing or financial services is preferred but not required.
The ideal candidate will demonstrate success managing multiple concurrent initiatives, building efficient systems, and analyzing performance data to inform decision\-making.  
* 

**SUPERVISORY RESPONSIBILITIES:**

Manage partners directly and indirectly related to specific and overall projects in the efforts, growth needs, and work flow of the Marketing Department. Carry out supervisor responsibilities in accordance with the organization’s policies and applicable laws. Responsibilities include interviewing, hiring, and training partners; planning, assigning, and directly work; appraising performance; rewarding and disciplining partners; addressing complaints and resolving problems.  



**PERFORMANCE STANDARDS:**

To perform this job successfully, an individual must have the ability to solve problems and handle complex details. He/she must be able to handle multiple projects simultaneously and must be a team player. He/she must be adept at quickly learning and understanding technology; and have a deep understanding of how technology can be applied to solve business challenges. The position requires the individual to possess excellent communication skills, both written and verbal, along with the ability to present information effectively. Error\-free work is expected. The chosen candidate must pay close attention to detail and must be able to meet all production and distribution schedules and deadlines, and must be creative, self\-motivated, organized, dependable and professional, with a demonstrated record as a self\-starter. Individual must use diplomacy, tact, and professionalism in actions and appearance.  



**QUALIFICATION REQUIREMENTS**

To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.  



**LANGUAGE SKILLS:**

Must be able to read and comprehend instructions, correspondence, and memos. This position will require the ability to develop reports that are easy to comprehend, as well as professional. The ability to communicate effectively with others by using appropriate communication tools/methods is required. Exceptional knowledge of the English language is particularly important. The ability to professionally write correspondence, create reports, and use software to produce presentations for both large and small groups is preferred.  



**MATHEMATICAL SKILLS:**

Ability to add, subtract, multiply and divide in all units of measurement using whole numbers, common fractions, and decimals. Ability to compute rate, ratio, and percent; and to draw (using software) and interpret graphs.  



**REASONING ABILITY:**

Ability to apply common sense understanding to carry out detailed but uninvolved written or oral instructions. Ability to deal with problems involving a few concrete variables in standardized situations. Ability to collect data, establish facts, and draw valid conclusions.  



**CERTIFICATES, LICENSES, REGISTRATIONS REQUIRED:**

A valid Driver’s License is needed.  



**OTHER SKILLS AND ABILITIES:**

Ability to operate a personal computer and software. Ability to effectively utilize software including Excel, PowerPoint, PhotoShop, and customer relationship management systems. Keyboard proficiency and accuracy is necessary. This position must have an ability to effectively communicate through all mediums.  



Must be able to operate all standard office machinery such as: digital camera; telephone; laser printers; scanners; fax machine; photocopier; typewriter; adding machine; and occasionally VCR, TV, overhead projector, or camcorder.  



**PHYSICAL DEMANDS:**

The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an partner to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.  



While performing the duties of this job, the partner is regularly required to sit, talk and hear. The partner is frequently required to use hands or fingers, handle or feel objects, tools, or controls. The partner is often required to stand, walk, and reach with hands or arms.  



The partner must regularly lift and/or move up to 50 pounds. Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, color vision, peripheral vision, and depth perception.  



**WORK ENVIRONMENT:**

The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those of a partner encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. The noise level in the work environment is usually moderate, at times high.  



**MENTAL DEMANDS:**

The mental characteristics necessary to competently perform this job include the continuous need to be alert, resourceful, precise, and accurate in performing all job functions, as well as the need to continuously use a high degree of concentration, patience, imagination, and initiative. While performing the duties of this job, the partner is continuously required to remember a significant amount of details, concentrate, exercise judgment, read, write, reason, and take initiative to start and complete tasks, and exercise patience. Partner must apply common sense to resolve issues.  



**REGULATORY COMPLIANCE \& CONFIDENTIALITY:**

Follows regulatory and policy compliance requirements, which would include those efforts in compliance with Bank Secrecy Act (BSA), Office of Foreign Asset Control (OFAC) and Anti\-Money Laundering (AML) Regulations, and other compliance related Policies. Also, this position requires maintaining confidentiality with regard to non\-public information about our members and Notre Dame Federal Credit Union.


*This job description does not imply that these are the only duties, including essential functions, to be performed by the individuals occupying this position. In addition, this job description is not an employment contract. The employment relationship with the individuals occupying this position remains “at will”.*
