jobs.description:
Hybrid, US\-based\* Ideal Candidate lives in or around Denver, CO or San Diego, CA


* **Hybrid Requirements:** Employees within 40 miles of a physical office location must be present in office 2 days a week.


**Office Locations:** San Diego, CA \& Denver, CO


**Overview:**



For over 15 years, 85SIXTY has been helping clients transform the way they reach, acquire and engage with their customers through an integrated combination of data, technology, cross\-channel strategies, and creative thinking. Some might call us a full\-service digital consultancy, but we like to think of ourselves as a partner that can both drive strategy and orchestrate delivery. Founded in 2010 by a veteran in retail, eCommerce, and digital, 85SIXTY was built around a consultative, high\-touch approach that allows us to work across a broad spectrum of areas to increase our value to our customers. We are supporting today’s forward\-thinking brands in their journey towards growth, whether that be financially, operationally and/or strategically. Solving whatever is thrown our way and translating it into simple solutions is at the core of 85SIXTY. We are looking for team members who fit that ethos.

  


85SIXTY is seeking a Senior CRM Marketing Strategist to lead the strategy, execution, and optimization of lifecycle marketing programs across email, SMS, and emerging customer engagement channels, specifically supporting projects in the Insider One platform. This role bridges strategic planning with hands\-on execution, helping clients transform customer data into meaningful, personalized marketing experiences.



The ideal candidate brings deep expertise in lifecycle marketing, customer segmentation, and cross\-channel engagement platforms. They will guide client relationships, translate data into actionable strategies, and collaborate across internal teams to deliver high\-performing campaigns that drive acquisition, retention, and long\-term customer value.



This role requires a balance of strategic thinking, campaign execution, client leadership, and platform expertise to ensure successful lifecycle marketing programs for our clients. The role also plays a key part in helping clients operationalize their customer data through lifecycle marketing programs powered by modern engagement and Customer Data Platform technologies.


**Responsibilities:**


**Client \& Relationship Management**



* Build and maintain strong client relationships by establishing trust, setting clear expectations, and serving as a strategic advisor on lifecycle marketing initiatives.
* Lead regular client meetings to present marketing strategies, campaign performance, insights, and optimization recommendations using presentation tools such as Google Slides or PowerPoint.
* Serve as a key point of contact for clients on CRM, lifecycle marketing, and customer engagement strategy.
* Proactively identify and propose opportunities to expand lifecycle programs through specific campaigns, automations, improved segmentation, personalization, and cross\-channel engagement.


**Lifecycle Strategy \& Customer Engagement**


* Develop and execute lifecycle marketing strategies that support customer acquisition, engagement, retention, and loyalty.
* Translate customer data and behavioral insights into targeted messaging strategies across email, SMS, and other digital engagement channels.
* Design personalized customer journeys that leverage behavioral triggers, lifecycle milestones, and predictive segmentation.
* Partner with clients to operationalize first\-party data strategies that support scalable lifecycle marketing programs.
* Continuously identify opportunities to improve lifecycle performance through testing, segmentation refinement, and campaign optimization.
* Identify opportunities to expand lifecycle programs through new channels, journey orchestration, and deeper customer data activation across platforms such as Insider One.


**Cross\-Channel Campaign Execution**



* Plan, build, deploy, and optimize email/SMS/RCS marketing campaigns across multiple clients and industries.
* Manage campaign calendars, including campaign briefs, messaging coordination, scheduling, and deployment.
* Occasionally develop or edit email templates and campaigns directly within marketing platforms, including responsive email development using HTML and CSS when necessary.
* Collaborate with creative, content, and analytics teams to ensure campaigns align with broader marketing strategies and brand standards.


**Customer Data, CDP \& Journey Orchestration**


* Manage lifecycle marketing programs within marketing automation and customer engagement platforms such as Insider One, Salesforce Marketing Cloud, Klaviyo, HubSpot, and similar technologies.
* Leverage Customer Data Platform capabilities to build unified audience segments using behavioral, transactional, and engagement data.
* Design and manage automated lifecycle journeys using real\-time triggers, behavioral events, and advanced segmentation.
* Partner with internal analytics, engineering, and client teams to ensure proper data integration, event tracking, and identity resolution for effective campaign targeting.
* Utilize predictive insights and platform capabilities to identify high\-value audiences and deliver personalized messaging at scale.


**Performance Analysis \& Optimization**


* Monitor and analyze campaign performance across lifecycle programs, evaluating results on a weekly, monthly, and quarterly basis.
* Develop reporting and insights that communicate campaign performance, customer engagement trends, and return on investment.
* Translate performance data into actionable recommendations that improve engagement, conversion rates, and customer lifetime value.
* Lead testing strategies including A/B testing, segmentation experiments, and journey optimization.


**Team Collaboration \& Agency Contribution**


* Collaborate with internal teams including analytics, paid media, strategy, and creative to ensure lifecycle programs align with broader marketing initiatives.
* Contribute to the growth and evolution of the CRM and lifecycle marketing practice within the agency.
* Share best practices, industry trends, and platform insights with internal teams to support innovation and continuous improvement.

  


**Desired Qualifications:**


* 6\+ years of experience in lifecycle marketing, email marketing, marketing automation, marketing operations, or related digital marketing roles.
* Direct hands\-on experience using Insider One to build customer segments, activate campaigns, and manage cross\-channel lifecycle journeys is required.
* Strong experience with enterprise and mid\-market marketing automation and customer engagement platforms such as Salesforce Marketing Cloud, Klaviyo, HubSpot, and Insider One.
* Experience working within Customer Data Platforms and journey orchestration tools to build advanced audience segments and activate personalized campaigns across email, SMS, web, and mobile channels.
* Hands\-on experience building, editing, or troubleshooting email campaigns including responsive email development using HTML and CSS.
* Proven expertise in audience segmentation, testing strategies, and lifecycle marketing optimization.
* Strong data analysis skills with the ability to interpret campaign results, identify trends, and develop testing recommendations.
* Experience working directly with clients and presenting strategic recommendations and performance insights.
* Strong communication and collaboration skills with the ability to work cross\-functionally in a fast\-paced agency environment.
* Understanding of marketing regulations and compliance standards including CAN\-SPAM, GDPR, CCPA, and email industry best practices.
* Strong organizational and prioritization skills with the ability to manage multiple clients and projects simultaneously.
* Self\-motivated and comfortable working both independently and within a collaborative team structure.


**Benefits:**


* Competitive salary and benefits package.
	+ Medical, Dental, Vision
	+ Life/AD\&D
	+ FSA \- Health/Dependent Care
	+ Voluntary Life/AD\&D
	+ Retirement Plan
* FTO \- Flexible Time Off
* Paid Holidays Annually: 13
* Opportunities for professional development and growth.
* Collaborative and dynamic work environment.
* Contribution to impactful and innovative projects in the digital space.


\#LI\-Hybrid


**Compensation Disclaimer:**



Compensation: $92,000 \- $102,000 annually. Compensation is based on multiple factors that are unique to each candidate, including and not limited to job\-related knowledge, level of relevant experience, education, skills and geographic location (to account for comparative cost of living). 85SIXTY reserves the right to modify this pay range at any time.


**EEO Statement:**



85SIXTY is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, marital status, age, national origin, ancestry, physical or mental disability, medical condition, pregnancy, genetic information, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, veteran status, or any other status protected under federal, state, or local law.


**Staffing / Recruiting Agencies:**



To all Staffing and Recruiting Agencies: Our Careers Site is only for individuals seeking a job at 85SIXTY. Staffing and recruiting agencies and individuals being represented by an agency are not authorized to use this site or to submit profiles, applications or resumes, and any such submissions will be considered unsolicited. 85SIXTY does not accept unsolicited resumes or applications from agencies. Please do not forward resumes to our jobs alias, 85SIXTY employees or any other company location. 85SIXTY is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes/applications.


**Fake Job Postings Statement:**



Please be aware of fictitious job postings, consulting engagements, solicitations, or employment offers from suspicious sources. These engagements may be an attempt to obtain private information, or to induce you to pay a fee for services related to recruitment or training. 85SIXTY does NOT charge any application, processing, or training fee at any stage of the recruitment or hiring process. If you have any doubts about the authenticity of any messaging behalf of 85SIXTY, please send us an email at HR@85SIXTY.com before taking any further action in relation to the correspondence, recruitment or hiring process. All genuine job openings will be posted on our careers page at https://85sixty.rippling\-ats.com/.

jobs.title: Director of Marketing Operations
jobs.description:
**1\. Overview**  
We are seeking a highly motivated and hands\-on Marketing \& Growth Operator to manage and scale our paid acquisition and marketing systems. This role will focus on running and optimizing Meta (Facebook/Instagram) and Google Ads campaigns, building high\-converting funnels and landing pages, and implementing AI\-driven automation workflows to generate high\-quality B2B leads. The ideal candidate will oversee the full marketing process—from campaign creation and testing to attribution tracking and lead quality optimization—while working closely with the sales team to ensure strong ROI and funded deals.

**2\. Responsibilities**

* Build, launch, and scale Meta (Facebook/Instagram) advertising campaigns for business funding and capital solutions
* Manage and optimize Google Ads campaigns, including search, retargeting, and high\-intent keyword targeting
* Create and test different ad creatives including static ads, carousel ads, UGC\-style videos, and AI\-generated video ads
* Design and optimize landing pages, lead forms, and multi\-step qualification funnels to increase conversions
* Implement and manage tracking systems including pixels, conversion APIs, and server\-side tracking
* Monitor the marketing funnel from click → lead → contact → approval → funding and improve performance
* Build and manage AI\-powered automation systems, including automated email sequences, SMS follow\-ups, and social media messaging automation
* Implement CRM automations, lead routing, and tagging systems
* Track performance metrics such as cost per lead (CPL), cost per qualified lead (CPQL), and cost per funded deal (CPA)
* Continuously optimize campaigns, scale high\-performing strategies, and pause underperforming campaigns
* Work closely with the sales team to improve lead quality and conversion rates

**3\. Skills \& Qualifications**

* 3\+ years of experience managing Meta Ads and Google Ads campaigns
* Proven success generating high\-intent B2B leads
* Strong understanding of marketing funnels, conversion optimization (CRO), and attribution tracking
* Experience working with AI automations and marketing workflow tools
* Familiarity with CRM systems and marketing automation platforms
* Strong analytical and data\-driven decision\-making skills
* Ability to work independently, move quickly, and manage campaigns end\-to\-end
* Experience in business lending, alternative finance, or B2B financial services is a plus.

Pay: $80,000\.00 \- $140,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Flexible schedule
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person

jobs.title: Director of Marketing Operations
jobs.description:
**Overview**  
We are seeking a dynamic and strategic **Director of Marketing Operations** to lead and optimize our marketing initiatives across multiple channels. The Director of Marketing is responsible for developing, executing, and optimizing the company’s marketing strategy to generate consistent, qualified leads and increase brand awareness across all markets. This role requires strong strategic leadership and technical expertise in digital marketing platforms.

The Director of Marketing will also lead and manage the internal marketing team including the **Videographer, Social Media Manager, and Virtual Assistants (VA’s)**. This role ensures all marketing activities across the team remain aligned with Brandon J Roofing’s standards of **Relentless Excellence** and support company growth goals.

**Duties**

* • Lead and manage the marketing team including Videographer, Social Media Manager, and Virtual Assistants.
* • Provide clear direction, priorities, and accountability for marketing initiatives.
* • Ensure marketing team members execute their roles effectively and consistently
* . • Coordinate collaboration between marketing, sales, and leadership.
* • Maintain quality control over all marketing outputs to protect the company brand.
* • Develop quarterly and annual marketing strategies aligned with company growth goals.
* • Identify the highest\-performing lead generation channels.
* • Coordinate campaigns across Google, Meta, website, and CRM systems.
* • Align marketing initiatives with recruiting, branding, and revenue growth goals.
* • Manage and optimize Google PPC campaigns.
* • Manage and maximize lead generation through Local Service Ads (LSA).
* • Oversee SEO strategy and organic search visibility.
* • Manage and optimize Google Business Profiles (GMB) for all company locations.
* • Analyze traffic sources and campaign performance using Google Analytics.
* Build and optimize high\-converting funnels for storm leads, service leads, recruiting, and promotions.
* • Design automated SMS and email lead nurturing workflows.
* • Ensure proper pipeline tracking and lead attribution.
* • Build landing pages integrated with advertising campaigns.
* • Continuously optimize funnel performance and conversion rates.
* Build and manage Meta advertising campaigns (Facebook \& Instagram).
* • Install and manage Meta pixels and conversion tracking.
* • Develop retargeting campaigns to capture missed leads.
* • Create lookalike audiences and advanced targeting strategies.
* • Test ad creatives and messaging to improve campaign performance.

**Skills**

* • Strong expertise in Google Analytics, PPC, LSA, SEO, and GMB optimization
* . • Advanced proficiency in Go High Level CRM including funnel creation and automation workflows. • Experience managing Meta advertising campaigns, retargeting, and pixel installation.
* • Proven ability to lead and manage a marketing team.
* • Ability to present examples of previous funnels, automation systems, and campaigns.

Pay: $100,000\.00 \- $150,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Flexible spending account
* Health insurance
* Health savings account
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Education:

* Bachelor's (Required)

Experience:

* marketing: 5 years (Required)
* Management: 4 years (Required)
* Go High Level CRM: 2 years (Required)

Location:

* Ellisville, MO 63011 (Required)

Work Location: In person

jobs.title: Client Retention & Marketing Operations Specialist (Remote)
jobs.description:
**The Opportunity**

220 Marketing supports over 1,000 mortgage, real estate, and insurance professionals nationwide. Our clients rely on us not only for marketing tools, but for consistent execution, accountability, and long\-term partnership.

This role plays a direct part in protecting and strengthening those client relationships.

You will manage client communication, operate within structured systems, resolve account issues, and contribute directly to client retention and revenue stability. This position combines client service, technical execution, and measurable performance standards within a clearly defined operational framework.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working within structured systems, solving client challenges logically, and taking ownership of consistent, measurable outcomes.

**Core Areas of Responsibility**

**·** Client Retention \& Account Management

* Respond to inbound client calls and emails.
* Manage cancellation conversations using defined retention frameworks.
* Diagnose service gaps and recommend next steps.
* Maintain established retention performance standards.
* Identify appropriate upsell opportunities aligned with client goals.
* Marketing Operations Execution
* Update WordPress\-based websites and marketing assets.
* Manage and submit internal work tickets.
* Assist clients in navigating their dashboards and marketing tools.
* Coordinate scheduling and proactive follow\-ups.

**Workflow \& Performance Accountability**

* Maintain QA standards and communication quality benchmarks.
* Meet monthly retention and activity targets through consistent execution.
* Maintain accurate and detailed CRM documentation.
* Resolve all assigned tickets within defined timelines.
* Operate effectively within structured systems and repeatable workflows.

**What Success Looks Like in the First 90 Days**

By the end of your first 90 days, successful team members:

* Comfortably manage client concerns and cancellation conversations.
* Navigate backend systems independently and efficiently.
* Consistently meet quality and retention benchmarks through steady execution.
* Communicate clearly and professionally, even during challenging conversations.
* Contribute meaningfully to overall team retention goals.

**Traits That Matter More Than Experience**

You will likely thrive in this role if you:

* Remain calm and steady during difficult conversations.
* Approach problems logically and prefer structured systems.
* Are comfortable working with measurable performance goals.
* Communicate clearly and professionally in writing.
* Learn new systems quickly and independently.
* Demonstrate emotional maturity and composure under pressure.
* Take ownership of your work and follow through consistently.

Job Type: Full\-time

Benefits:

* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* On\-the\-job training
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance
* Work from home

Work Location: Remote

Pay: $39,000\.00 \- $45,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Health insurance

Work Location: Remote

jobs.title: Director of Marketing Operations
jobs.description:
Who We Are

Tobacco Junction is a family\-owned tobacco and cigar retail chain with 12 locations across East Texas, established in 1996\. We're passionate about our products, our customers, and our community — and we're looking for a creative, driven Marketing \& Merchandising Director to lead our brand forward.

The Role

The Marketing \& Merchandising Director owns all marketing and in\-store merchandising functions for Tobacco Junction and its family of brands. This is a role that demands equal parts organization and creativity. The right candidate is just as comfortable managing a content calendar and running our loyalty program from their desk as they are driving to a store location to execute a merchandising display. You'll think strategically, stay highly organized, and execute independently across a wide range of creative, operational, and physical retail disciplines.

What You'll Do

* Develop and execute marketing strategies across digital, social, print, radio, and in\-store channels
* Manage and evolve branding and visual identity for Tobacco Junction's house brand portfolio
* Design, print, and distribute in\-house produced marketing materials including shelf signage, promotional literature, and point\-of\-sale displays
* Plan and coordinate special events, including the Annual East Texas Cigar Festival
* Create and manage promotional materials, advertising campaigns, and customer communications
* Oversee email/direct mail campaigns and customer re\-engagement initiatives
* Assist with hands\-on in\-store merchandising across retail locations
* Manage the company's product database and support retail operations with marketing assets
* Coordinate with vendors, distributors, and promotional partners
* Maintain the company's web presence and support SEO strategy
* Manage social media accounts and content scheduling across platforms
* Work cross\-functionally with store managers and corporate staff

What We're Looking For

* Proven experience in a marketing leadership or management role
* Highly organized with strong attention to detail — able to manage multiple campaigns, deadlines, and priorities simultaneously
* Comfortable with consistent desk\-based work — managing social media accounts, loyalty program communications, and digital campaigns is a significant part of this role
* Experience with digital marketing, social media management, and email/direct mail campaigns
* Hands\-on approach to in\-store merchandising — willing to travel to store locations and physically execute displays, signage, and promotional setups
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills
* Ability to manage multiple projects independently in a fast\-paced retail environment
* Knowledge of or passion for tobacco, cigars, or specialty retail a plus

Why Tobacco Junction?

This isn't your average marketing job where you're executing someone else's vision. At Tobacco Junction, you ARE the marketing department. You'll have a direct line to ownership, real creative freedom, and the opportunity to put your stamp on one of East Texas' most recognized retail brands. If you're a self\-starter who thrives with autonomy and wants a role where your work is seen and valued — this is it.

* Full ownership of marketing and merchandising across 12 East Texas locations
* Direct access and visibility to the owner/executive team — your voice matters here
* Be at the center of exciting community events including the Annual East Texas Cigar Festival
* A dynamic role with variety — from digital campaigns to in\-store displays, no two days are the same
* Represent an established, community\-rooted brand with nearly 30 years in East Texas

**To Apply**

*Apply online through Indeed or send your resume and portfolio (if applicable) to Meghan@tobaccojunction.com.*

Pay: $60,000\.00 \- $70,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Paid time off
* Retirement plan

Work Location: In person

jobs.title: Manager, Operations Analytics (Marketing Automation and Digital Asset Management)
jobs.description:
**Position Summary...**
-----------------------

**What you'll do...**
---------------------

***Immigration sponsorship is not available for this role. Applicants must be authorized to work for any employer in the United States without current or future visa sponsorship.***  

  

As marketing evolves through AI\-powered workflows, intelligent automation, and rapidly advancing marketing technologies, Sam’s Club is focused on translating innovation into measurable business outcomes. The Manager, Marketing Automation \& AI Enablement plays a critical role in operationalizing this transformation.  

  

This role sits at the intersection of Marketing Strategy, Marketing Operations, Channel teams (Ecom, Paid, Non\-Paid), and Product/Technology—ensuring automation and AI capabilities are aligned to strategic priorities, operational workflows, channel performance goals, and technical feasibility.  

  

You will bring clarity and structure to complex, cross\-functional initiatives—translating business requirements into actionable execution plans and ensuring alignment between marketing objectives and technical delivery.  

  

*Success in this role means:*
* Marketing, Channel, and Product/Tech teams are aligned on AI and automation priorities.
* Initiatives are delivered on time with clear ownership and minimal friction.
* Channel teams adopt new capabilities with clarity and confidence.
* Leadership has transparent visibility into roadmap progress and impact.
* AI and automation capabilities are embedded into channel execution workflows.


**What You'll Do:**  

*Marketing, Channel \& Product/Tech Alignment*
* Partner closely with Marketing, Ecom, Paid, Non\-Paid teams to identify automation and AI opportunities that improve performance and efficiency.
* Translate channel business requirements into clearly defined use cases and technical inputs for Product and Technology teams.
* Ensure automation and AI initiatives are aligned with platform capabilities, data architecture, and enterprise technology standards.
* Act as a connector across business and technical stakeholders to reduce friction and accelerate execution.


  

*Marketing Automation Execution \& Digital Asset Management (DAM) Execution*
* Support advancement of enterprise marketing automation and orchestration capabilities to improve personalization, lifecycle marketing, and campaign effectiveness.
* Identify workflow inefficiencies across content production, asset management, and distribution, and campaign activation.
* Partner with the Marketing Automation \& AI Acceleration Lead and Product/Tech to: Enhance integration between DAM and activation platforms (email, paid media, onsite, etc.); enable dynamic content assembly and asset reuse across channels; automate asset tagging, metadata enrichment, and governance workflows.
* Ensure DAM workflows are scalable, documented, and compliant with brand, legal and governance standards.
* Support asset lifecycle management processes including ingestion, versioning, and archiving, and expiration control.


  

*Digital Asset Management (DAM) Governance \& Optimization*
* Support governance frameworks for asset taxonomy, metadata standards, naming conventions, and access controls.
* Partner with Creative, Brand, Legal, and Compliance teams to ensure asset rights management and usage guidelines are operationalized within DAM workflows.
* Monitor DAM adoption and asset utilization metrics to identify optimization opportunities.
* Drive continuous improvement in asset discoverability, reuse rates, and time\-to\-market for campaigns.
* Coordinate vendor engagement related to DAM platform enhancements and roadmap prioritization.


  

*AI Initiative Planning \& Support*
* Support intake, documentation, and refinement of business requirements across Marketing and Channels.
* Assist in evaluating initiatives based on impact, readiness, technical dependencies, and capacity.
* Translate prioritized initiatives into structured project plans with clear milestones and ownership.
* Coordinate rollout planning in partnership with Product/Tech to ensure delivery feasibility and sequencing.


  

*Rollout Execution \& Adoption*
* Execute rollout plans for AI and automation initiatives across marketing channels.
* Coordinate pilots, phased releases, and scaling plans with Product/Tech and channel stakeholders.
* Support structured adoption pathways including training, enablement materials, documentation, and workflow integration.
* Monitor adoption metrics and provide feedback loops to Product/Tech and leadership.


  

*Stakeholder Communication*
* Develop clear updates and dashboards to provide visibility into initiative progress, risks, and outcomes.
* Translate technical and operational updates into concise business\-facing narratives.
* Maintain consistent communication cadence across Marketing, Channels, and Product/Tech teams.


  

*Cross\-Functional Coordination*
* Track timelines, dependencies, and deliverables across Marketing, Channel, and Product/Tech workstreams.
* Identify misalignment early and proactively escalate risks or blockers.
* Ensure consistent implementation of automation and AI capabilities across channels.


  

*Change Management \& Enablement*
* Support change management efforts for AI and automation rollouts.
* Clearly communicate what is changing; why it matters; how teams should engage; what success looks like.
* Contribute to development of training materials, documentation, and use case libraries.
* Monitor adoption signals and recommend adjustments to improve sustained engagement.


*Governance \& Measurement*
* Track KPIs including adoption rates, efficiency gains, time\-to\-impact, and campaign performance lift.
* Maintain project documentation, milestone tracking, and dependency management.
* Support vendor coordination and ensure execution aligns with established governance frameworks.


  

**What You'll Bring:**
* 5–8 years of experience in Marketing Operations, Marketing Automation, MarTech, Digital Marketing, or related roles.
* Experience working cross\-functionally with both business and technical stakeholders.
* Familiarity with enterprise marketing technology ecosystems and automation platforms.
* Exposure to AI\-enabled marketing tools or intelligent automation use cases.
* Strong project management and stakeholder alignment skills.
* Ability to translate business needs into structured technical requirements.
* Experience operating in a matrixed organization.


  

**About Sam's Club:**
  

Sam Walton opened the first Sam's Club in 1983 to meet a growing need among customers who wanted to buy merchandise in bulk. Since then, Sam's Club has grown rapidly, opening more than 600 clubs in the U.S. and 100 clubs internationally. By offering affordable, wholesale merchandise to members, Sam's Club helps make saving simple for families and small business owners. Sam's Club employs about 110,000 associates in the U.S. The average club is 134,000 square feet and offers bulk groceries and general merchandise. Most clubs also have specialty services, such as a pharmacy, an optical department, a photo center, or a tire and battery center.
  

  

**Future Ways of Working:**
  

Our company's success can be attributed to our employees. While technology has allowed us to be effective while working remotely, there is no substitute for being in the office together; it helps to shape our culture, collaborate, innovate, build relationships, and move more quickly. We strive to provide flexibility in order to promote a healthy work\-life balance but recognize that in\-person interactions are important to our culture and shared success. We'll meet in person on a regular and purposeful basis.
  

  

**Benefits:**
  

Beyond our great compensation package, you can receive incentive awards for your performance. Other great perks include 401(k) match, stock purchase plan, paid maternity and parental leave, PTO, multiple health plans, and much more.
  

  

**Equal Opportunity Employer:**
  

Sam’s Club is an Equal Opportunity Employer – by choice. We believe we are best equipped to help our associates, customers and the communities we serve live better when we really know them. That means understanding, respecting, and valuing unique styles, experiences, identities, ideas and opinions while being welcoming of all people.  

  

*The above information has been designed to indicate the general nature and level of work performed in the role. It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all responsibilities and qualifications required of employees assigned to this job. The full Job Description can be made available as part of the hiring process.*
  

  

\#LI\-onsite At Sam's Club, we offer competitive pay as well as performance\-based bonus awards and other great benefits for a happier mind, body, and wallet!
  

**\-Health benefits** include medical, vision and dental coverage  

  

**\-Financial benefits** include 401(k), stock purchase and company\-paid life insurance  

  

**\-Paid time off benefits** include PTO, parental leave, family care leave, bereavement, jury duty, and voting. You will also receive PTO and/or PPTO that can be used for vacation, sick leave, holidays, or other purposes. The amount you receive depends on your job classification and length of employment. It will meet or exceed the requirements of paid sick leave laws, where applicable.
  

  

For information about PTO, see https://one.walmart.com/notices.  

  

**\- Other benefits** include short\-term and long\-term disability, company discounts, Military Leave Pay, adoption and surrogacy expense reimbursement, and more.
  

Live Better U is a company paid education benefit program for full\-time and part\-time associates in Walmart and Sam's Club facilities. Programs range from high school completion to bachelor's degrees, including English Language Learning and short\-form certificates. Tuition, books, and fees are completely paid for by Walmart.
  

Eligibility requirements apply to some benefits and may depend on your job classification and length of employment. Benefits are subject to change and may be subject to a specific plan or program terms.
  

  

For information about benefits and eligibility, see One.Walmart.
  

The annual salary range for this position is $80,000\.00 \- $155,000\.00 Additional compensation includes annual or quarterly performance bonuses. Additional compensation for certain positions may also include Regional Pay Zone (RPZ) (based on location).

**Minimum Qualifications...**
-----------------------------

*Outlined below are the required minimum qualifications for this position. If none are listed, there are no minimum qualifications.*

Option 1: Bachelor's degree in Business, Engineering, Statistics, Economics, Analytics, Mathematics, Arts, Finance or related field and 2 years' experience in data analysis, data science, statistics, or related field. Option 2: Master's degree in Business, Engineering, Statistics, Economics, Analytics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Information Technology or related field. Option 3: 4 years' experience in data analysis, data science, statistics, or related field.**Preferred Qualifications...**
-------------------------------

*Outlined below are the optional preferred qualifications for this position. If none are listed, there are no preferred qualifications.*

jobs.title: Senior Marketing Data Analyst
jobs.description:
**It Starts With Our Employees**

FourLeaf's ultimate goal is to provide the best\-in\-class member and employee experience, and it all begins with two things: hiring incredible people and giving them a great place to work.


**What You’ll Do**



The Senior Marketing Data Analyst will lead the Marketing department in audience development and data analysis. They will assist in examining market and organization statistics to provide optimization recommendations where applicable. Responsible for using analytics and visualizations to create compelling narratives that will drive decision making in accordance to strategic goals.



This opportunity is open to flexible work options including a remote set up.


**Core Contributions**


* Evaluate and analyze data for Marketing department and credit union. Create hypotheses around data for optimization. Analyze Marketing campaign performance against forecasts and operational metrics.
* Manage FourLeaf’s marketing database including maintaining data and system updates and monitoring overall database capabilities, limitations, and upgrades. Assist in developing strategy based on database.
* Lead on product line reporting and visualizations; including development of performance dashboards, custom visualizations, ad hoc analysis on large data sets, and the overall dissemination of analysis with team, business units, and executives. Clearly communicate processes and results to technical and non\-technical audiences; lead critical discussions of results.
* Lead audience development and optimization across product lines. Manage partner agencies to develop propensity models and audience segments that are in line with organizational goals. Works with Marketing FLOD and FourLeaf Compliance team to ensure that all models/audience outcomes are within regulatory guidelines.
* Document processes and procedures for collecting and disseminating data, including reporting, audience lists, and suppression files. Identify opportunities to enhance the efficiency of existing processes, and propose improvements to streamline the collection, use, and presentation of data.
* Work closely with Digital Marketing Manager and Marketing Data Specialist to identify opportunities with household appends, Claritas P$YCLE segments, and other third\-party vendors.
* Mentor and lead quality assurance for the Marketing Data Team by guiding, advising, and supporting team members to build analytical and technical skills, and uphold accurate, insightful analytics.
* Special projects as assigned by AVP Marketing Segmentation Strategy, and FVP Marketing.


**Assets You Will Bring**


* Bachelor’s Degree in Marketing, Business, Data Science/Analytics.
* 8–10 years of experience in business data science, with prior marketing experience strongly preferred.
* Experience in a financial institution preferred.
* Expert in data analytics, management, and visualization with experience working with Snowflake, Altair Monarch Data Prep Studio or Sigma, and PowerBI.
* Quick study and motivated.


*The estimated salary for this role is $94,570\.00 to $113,484\.00 annually. (**FourLeaf Federal Credit Union offers a comprehensive benefits package, including medical, dental, and vision coverage; life and disability insurance; voluntary benefit programs; a 401(k) plan with employer match; reimbursement and wellness programs; and an annual performance\-based bonus.**)*


**What Sets Us Apart?**



In addition to our comprehensive benefits, we invest in employee connection and well\-being through:


* Competitive 401(k)
* Tuition and fitness reimbursement programs
* Flexible work options
* Volunteer opportunities
* Executive “Water Cooler Chats”
* Clubs, sports, and social events
* Food truck days


….and more!


**Who is FourLeaf?**



FourLeaf Federal Credit Union (FourLeaf) has been committed to the financial well\-being of our members for over 80 years. Through our full range of competitive savings and loan products, you can trust us in every step of your financial journey. From applying for a credit card to closing on your mortgage to opening your child’s first savings account, FourLeaf is here to help you reach your financial goals.



We are proud to be an award\-winning place to work! Some of our recent recognitions include Certified Great Place to Work 2024\-2025, America’s Greatest Midsize Workplaces 2025, Quantum Workplace Employee Voice Award 2024, and Fortune’s Best Workplaces in Financial Services \& Insurance 2024\.



As a credit union, our vision is to enrich the lives of our members, employees, and communities. Since 2002, we have been an integral community partner through our charitable giving and community development programs in New York and beyond.

Job Summary:


Senior Software Engineer, Marketing Automation (Live Nation Worldwide, Inc.; Beverly Hills, California): Write clean, well designed, testable and efficient code to enable marketing campaign creation. Contribute to all phases of the application development life cycle. Build out and support all the necessary infrastructure in Amazon Web Services (AWS). Design database schemas and tables. Develop, maintain and optimize Structured Query Language (SQL) queries. Maintain and evolve existing real\-time processing systems. Identify bottlenecks and bugs and propose solutions to identified problems. Ensure the overall system health through automated testing and monitoring. Collaborate with engineers and stakeholders to provide efficient automated processes. Integrate with third party applications and vendors. Support continuous improvement by investigating alternative approaches to existing solutions. Telecommuting permitted from anywhere in the U.S.
  

  

Salary: $185,000 \- 195,000 per year
  

  

Education Requirements: Bachelor’s degree or foreign equivalent in Computer Science or a related field.
  

  

Experience Requirements: Five (5\) years of experience in the position offered, as a Software Engineer, Programmer Analyst, or related occupation.
  

  

Must have experience with the following: Java; development of available and reliable distributed systems; relational databases; AWS stack: Elastic Beanstalk, Lambdas and S3; containerized applications; Kafka and Kafka Streams; Spring MVC; and Data Engineering: ETL.
  

  

To Apply: Any interested applicant may click on the APPLY NOW button above to apply for this position.


Live Nation Entertainment will never request payment or equipment purchases as part of the hiring process. Recruiters will only contact candidates from official Live Nation or affiliated brand email domains.

jobs.description:
**Additional Information**Zone 3 pay: $126,900\-$206,000 annuallyRemote pay: $118,900\-$192,800 annually  

**Job Number**26030178  

**Job Category**Information Technology  

**Location**Marriott International HQ, 7750 Wisconsin Ave, Bethesda, Maryland, United States, 20814  

**Schedule**Full Time  

**Located Remotely?**Y  

**Position Type** Management  

**Bonus Eligible:** Y  

**Stock Package:** Y  

**Expiration Date:** 04/09/2026
**Job Summary**


We are looking for a **Director, Data Operations – Paid Marketing** to join the Data, Analytics \& AI (D\&A) organization and lead our data products for paid marketing across a diverse range of paid media channels, including digital advertising, search, sponsored content, pay\-per\-click campaigns, paid social, and more.


The role is responsible for devising, implementing, and assessing strategies related to data product operations—such as data collection, curation, and analytics/AI enablement—in the paid marketing space to support lead generation, brand awareness, and revenue growth.


This position involves close collaboration with business leaders, campaign managers, data engineers and analysts, and external agencies to support:

* Data platform enhancement
* Marketing performance insights
* Media planning \& strategy
* Proactive, data\-driven business decisions


The role includes direct interaction with leadership teams across the D\&A organization and the Marketing discipline.

  

**Candidate Profile**

**Education and Experience Required**

* **Option 1:**
	+ 4\-year degree from an accredited university in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Engineering, Information Systems, or a similar quantitative discipline
	+ Eight or more years of progressive experience in data management, analytics, data warehousing, or marketing technology

**OR**

* **Option 2:**
	+ Six or more years of experience in data management, analytics, or data warehousing
	+ Master’s degree

**Required Experience \& Skills**

* Design and engineering of complex data flows and systems in an omnichannel environment
* Defining and managing transformational strategies for digital marketing and paid media data (search, display, video, affiliates, open web publishers and walled gardens such as Facebook and Google)
* Knowledge of advertising technology (ad tech), real\-time data processing, clean rooms, database technologies, and distributed data stores (SQL, NoSQL)
* Ability to deliver results in complex and ambiguous environments

  

**Core Work Activities**

**Primary Leadership Activities**

* Provide leadership, consulting, and information expertise to achieve Data Products goals in a cross\-functional product pod
* Partner with business and technology disciplines to develop and deploy paid marketing data and AI enablement infrastructure
* Contribute to data strategy, data management, and enhanced data products for Marketing
* Oversee media data strategy and prioritize implementation tactics aligned to long\-term business goals
* Identify KPIs and data requirements to optimize media mix strategies and ROI
* Manage vendors and technical staff supporting AWS, Snowflake, Python, and related tools
* Reduce manual processes and data quality issues related to data capture
* Build Data Product, Analytics, and Reporting roadmaps
* Partner with leadership to secure funding for critical Data Products and IT programs

  

**Managing Responsibilities with Stakeholders**

* Build and maintain effective internal and external stakeholder relationships
* Foster a collaborative and goal\-oriented team environment
* Communicate initiative wins, risks, and opportunities
* Solicit feedback and develop action plans to address gaps
* Support direct reports in building business partnerships

  

**Managing Profitability**

* Analyze service issues and trends
* Develop and execute operational strategies aligned with brand business objectives
* Review and audit expenses

  

**Managing Revenue Goals**

* Monitor performance against budget
* Review financial statements and operational reports
* Coach teams on managing controllable expenses

  

**Managing and Conducting Human Resources Activities**

* Interview and hire employees
* Promote fair and equitable treatment of associates
* Facilitate regular team communication (e.g., staff meetings)
* Model service behaviors and foster engagement
* Set goals, manage performance, and conduct reviews
* Solicit employee feedback and address concerns
* Enforce policies and disciplinary procedures
* Champion change and support talent development

 *At Marriott International, we are dedicated to being an equal opportunity employer, welcoming all and providing access to opportunity. We actively foster an environment where the unique backgrounds of our associates are valued and celebrated. Our greatest strength lies in the rich blend of culture, talent, and experiences of our associates. We are committed to non\-discrimination on any protected basis, including disability, veteran status, or other basis protected by applicable law.*


All positions offer a 401(k) plan, stock purchase plan, discounts at Marriott properties, commuter benefits, employee assistance plan, and childcare discounts. Benefits are subject to terms and conditions, which may include rules regarding eligibility, enrollment, waiting period, contribution, benefit limits, election changes, benefit exclusions, and others. Click here to learn more.  

  

Full\-time positions also offer coverage for medical, dental, vision, health care flexible spending account, dependent care flexible spending account, life insurance, disability insurance, accident insurance, adoption expense reimbursements, paid parental leave and educational assistance.  

  

**Washington Applicants Only**: Employees will accrue paid sick leave, 0\.077 PTO balance for every hour worked and be eligible to receive a minimum of 9 holidays annually.  

  

Marriott HQ is committed to a hybrid work environment that enables associates to Be connected. Headquarters\-based positions are considered hybrid, for candidates within a commuting distance to Bethesda, MD; candidates outside of commuting distance to Bethesda, MD will be considered for Remote positions.
Marriott International is the world’s largest hotel company, with more brands, more hotels and more opportunities for associates to grow and succeed. **Be** where you can do your best work, **begin** your purpose, **belong** to an amazing global team, and **become** the best version of you.

We are looking for a **Director, Data O

jobs.title: Vice President, Technical Program Management, Commercial, MarTech, and Data
jobs.description:
Responsibilities:
**Who We Are**


SiriusXM and its brands (Pandora, SiriusXM Media, AdsWizz, Simplecast, and SiriusXM Connect) are leading a new era of audio entertainment and services by delivering the most compelling subscription and ad\-supported audio entertainment experience for listeners \- in the car, at home, and anywhere on the go with connected devices. Our vision is to shape the future of audio, where everyone can be effortlessly connected to the voices, stories and music they love wherever they are.


This is the place where a diverse group of emerging talent and legends alike come to share authentic and purposeful songs, stories, sounds and insights through some of the best programming and technology in the world. Our critically\-acclaimed, industry\-leading audio entertainment encompasses music, sports, comedy, news, talk, live events, and podcasting. No matter their individual role, each of our employees plays a vital part in bringing SiriusXM’s vision to life every day.

**SiriusXM** is the leading audio entertainment company in North America, and the premier programmer and platform for subscription and digital advertising\-supported audio products. SiriusXM’s platforms collectively reach approximately 150 million listeners, the largest digital audio audience across paid and free tiers in North America, and deliver music, sports, talk, news, comedy, entertainment and podcasts. Pandora, a subsidiary of SiriusXM, is the largest ad\-supported audio entertainment streaming service in the U.S. SiriusXM's subsidiaries Simplecast and AdsWizz make it a leader in podcast hosting, production, distribution, analytics and monetization. The Company’s advertising sales organization, which operates as SiriusXM Media, leverages its scale, cross\-platform sales organization and ad tech capabilities to deliver results for audio creators and advertisers. SiriusXM, through SiriusXM Canada Holdings, Inc., also offers satellite radio and audio entertainment in Canada. In addition to its audio entertainment businesses, SiriusXM offers connected vehicle services to automakers.

**How You’ll Make an Impact**


The Vice President, Technical Program Management, Commercial, MarTech, and Data is a senior technology leader responsible for driving execution excellence and Go\-To\-Market readiness across some of SiriusXM’s most complex and commercially critical initiatives.


This role owns the predictability, orchestration, and delivery confidence of enterprise\-scale programs that power customer growth, subscription management, marketing technology, analytics, and data platforms. The VP establishes the operating rhythms, governance frameworks, and execution standards that ensure strategic initiatives translate into reliable delivery and measurable business outcomes.


In parallel, this leader builds and develops a high\-performing Technical Program Management organization responsible for coordinating large\-scale cross\-domain initiatives spanning engineering, product, marketing, and business functions.


Operating as a trusted advisor to executive leadership, this role provides visibility into delivery health, sequencing risk, cross\-platform dependencies, and Go\-To\-Market readiness, ensuring that technical execution consistently aligns with commercial priorities, revenue commitments, and customer experience goals.

**What You’ll Do**


Technical Program Management Leadership

* Lead and develop a high\-performing Technical Program Management organization of approximately 45 FTEs and consultants, including Domain TPMs, Workstream TPMs, and Program Leads.
* Drive hiring, coaching, performance management, career development, and succession planning to build a world\-class TPM capability.
* Establish strong operating rhythms and program governance structures that enable reliable execution across complex, cross\-functional initiatives.
* Translate complex technical realities into executive\-level insights that enable confident decision\- making and organizational accountability.
* Serve as a senior escalation point to resolve cross\-functional conflicts, unblock execution, and drive alignment when priorities compete.
* Cultivate a culture of ownership, accountability, operational rigor, and disciplined innovation.
* Champion continuous improvement practices that increase delivery predictability, execution velocity, and transparency across the organization.


Enterprise Program Delivery \& Cross\-Domain Execution

* Own the delivery orchestration of large\-scale enterprise initiatives spanning multiple engineering organizations, product teams, and technology platforms.
* Establish disciplined program structures that align business strategy, product direction, and technical execution across dependent workstreams.
* Ensure programs launch with clear ownership, success criteria, governance structures, and scalable delivery practices.
* Maintain enterprise visibility into program risks, technical dependencies, delivery confidence, and sequencing constraints across the portfolio.
* Lead cross\-functional mitigation efforts and executive\-level decision forums to support informed trade\-offs and timely resolution of risks.
* Provide concise, credible reporting on program health, readiness, risks, and delivery confidence to SVP and C\-suite audiences.


Go\-To\-Market \& Commercial Delivery Alignment

* Partner closely with Product, Marketing, Business, Ads, Content, and Sales leaders to ensure Go\-To\-Market readiness is embedded throughout the delivery lifecycle.
* Translate technical milestones, dependencies, and execution risks into business\-relevant timelines tied to product launches, revenue events, partner commitments, and customer experience outcomes.
* Ensure alignment between engineering execution and commercial initiatives impacting subscription management, customer growth, marketing engagement, and platform adoption.
* Support cross\-functional coordination required to launch major initiatives that directly influence revenue, customer acquisition, and retention.


Requirements, Delivery Governance \& Tooling

* Ensure best practices and repeatable processes for requirements definition, planning, and delivery governance.
* Drive the use of Jira and related tooling to ensure traceability from strategic roadmap initiatives to engineering execution.
* Guide the appropriate funneling of requirements into engineering backlogs through collaboration across Product, Engineering, Finance, Legal, Marketing, and business stakeholders.
* Lead program reviews and cross\-domain planning forums to maintain alignment on priorities, delivery health, and execution expectations.
* Ensure TPM\-led governance practices enable engineering teams to deliver efficiently while maintaining enterprise\-level visibility and accountability.

**What You’ll Need**

* Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business, or related field preferred; equivalent experience accepted.
* 15\+ years of experience leading large\-scale technical programs and cross\-functional delivery across complex technology organizations.
* 8\-10\+ years of senior leadership experience building and leading program management or technical program management teams.
* Demonstrated success delivering complex, enterprise\-scale initiatives spanning multiple engineering domains and business stakeholders.
* Strong understanding of software development lifecycles, platform architectures, and cross\-team engineering execution models.
* Experience partnering closely with Product, Engineering, Marketing, and commercial leadership teams to deliver customer\-facing technology initiatives.
* Deep experience managing cross\-functional dependencies, program risk, sequencing, and large\-scale delivery governance.
* Strong technical fluency across modern software platforms, data systems, and enterprise architecture.
* Exceptional executive communication skills and the ability to translate complex technical topics into clear business insights.
* Proven ability to lead in fast\-paced, highly cross\-functional environments with competing priorities.
* Must have legal right to work in the U.S.


At SiriusXM, we carefully consider a wide range of factors when determining compensation, including your background and experience. These considerations can cause your compensation to vary. We expect the base salary for this position to be in the range of $228,000 to $335,000 and will depend on your skills, qualifications, and experience. Additionally, this role might be eligible for discretionary short\-term and long\-term incentives. We encourage all interested candidates to apply.

**Our goal at SiriusXM is to provide and maintain a work environment that fosters mutual respect, professionalism and cooperation. SiriusXM is an equal opportunity employer that does not discriminate on the basis of actual or perceived race, creed, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, alienage or citizenship status, age, disability or handicap, sex, gender identity, marital status, familial status, veteran status, sexual orientation or any other characteristic protected by applicable federal, state or local laws.**

*The requirements and duties described above may be modified or waived by the Company in its sole discretion without notice.*

jobs.title: Marketing Automation Specialist
jobs.description:
**About HealthChannels**  

HealthChannels is a premier point\-of\-care workforce solutions partner dedicated to solving the most pressing challenges in modern healthcare. As the parent organization to a specialized family of brands—including **ScribeAmerica**, **Enabli Health**, **Speke AI**, and **HealthCareU**—we leverage a unique combination of a highly\-skilled pre\-professional workforce and next\-generation AI technology to augment care teams. With over 20 years of industry excellence and a presence in all 50 states,


**About the Role**  

The Marketing Automation Specialist is responsible for the strategic configuration and tactical execution of the company’s marketing automation platform (HubSpot). This role focuses on campaign execution, lead generation and nurturing, workflow optimization, and CRM data integrity to drive demand generation and support the organization’s growth strategy. This role will serve as the internal technical expert for automation, ensuring that all digital campaigns are tracked, measured, and optimized for maximum conversion


**What You’ll Do**


* Manage the day\-to\-day operations of the HubSpot marketing automation platform, including workflow creation, lead scoring, and list segmentation.
* Design and implement end\-to\-end lead nurturing programs, automated drip campaigns, and trigger\-based communications.
* Partner with the Sales Development Representatives to coordinate marketing campaigns with SDR outbound campaigns to maximize impact and prospect touchpoints.
* Create and manage high\-converting landing pages and lead capture forms that align with broader marketing campaigns.
* Monitor and maintain data hygiene within the marketing database, including segmenting audiences based on behavior and ICP (Ideal Customer Profile).
* Execute A/B testing on email subject lines, content, and CTA layouts to continually improve engagement metrics.
* Develop and maintain dashboards to report on campaign performance, ROI, and lead\-to\-opportunity conversion rates.
* Collaborate with the Content Marketing team to distribute white papers, blogs, and case studies through automated channels.
* Ensure all marketing tactics are captured and tracked in Hubspot to evaluate the effectiveness of each channel and report on the respective ROI.
* Stay current with marketing automation trends and provide recommendations for tech stack improvements.


**What You’ll Need**


* Bachelor’s degree in marketing, business, communications, or a related field.
* 3–5 years of experience in digital marketing with a heavy focus on marketing automation.
* HubSpot Certification is highly preferred.
* Demonstrated experience building complex automation workflows and creating omnichannel marketing campaigns inside Hubspot.
* Experience working in a growth\-minded environment where marketing success is measured by revenue generated.
* Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate data into actionable insights.
* Excellent attention to detail and project management skills.
* Experience in the healthcare or health\-tech industry is a plus.


**The Benefits**


* Competitive pay with bonus/commission structure
	+ Compensation range: Our base compensation for this position is between $70,000 \- $80,000 and bonus/commission eligible, depending on qualifications, experience, and geographic location
* Monthly Mobile Reimbursement
* MyFlexPay: Get paid when you need it most
* Health, Dental, Vision, PTO and 401k
* A Diverse, Equitable, and Inclusive culture


**Want to know more?**


* **Our DEI Mission:**

* **What is our culture like?**
	+ **https://www.scribeamerica.com/mission\-vision\-values/**
* **HealthChannels Blogs:**

In addition to federal law requirements, HealthChannels complies with applicable state and local laws governing nondiscrimination in employment in every location in which the company has facilities. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation, and training.*  

  

*HealthChannels expressly prohibits any form of workplace harassment based on race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, national origin, age, disability, genetics, protected veteran status, or any other legally protected group status. Improper interference with the ability of HealthChannels employees to perform their job duties may result in discipline up to and including discharge.*

jobs.title: Marketing Operations Manager
jobs.description:
**About LangChain**
-------------------


At LangChain, our mission is to make intelligent agents ubiquitous. We provide the agent engineering platform and open source frameworks developers need to ship reliable agents fast.


Our open source frameworks, LangChain and LangGraph, see over 90\+ million downloads per month and help developers build agents with speed and granular control. LangSmith offers observability, evaluation, and deployment for rapid iteration, enabling teams to transform LLM systems into dependable production experiences.


LangChain is trusted by millions of developers worldwide and powers AI teams at companies like Replit, Clay, Cloudflare, Harvey, Rippling, Vanta, Workday, and more.

**About the role:**
-------------------


We’re looking for a **Marketing Operations Manager** to help scale the systems and data infrastructure behind our GTM engine.


This role owns supporting marketing in annual planning, managing the tech stack, attribution, data pipelines, reporting, automation, and campaign operations that enable the marketing and sales teams to operate effectively. You’ll work closely with Marketing, Sales, RevOps, and Data to ensure our GTM organization runs on clean data and reliable systems.


This is a highly cross\-functional role ideal for someone who enjoys building systems, solving data problems, and turning complex funnels into actionable insights.

**What you will do:**
---------------------


Strategy \& Planning

* Partner with GTM leadership to define how marketing performance is measured across the funnel
* Help develop the metrics framework for marketing impact, including pipeline generation, opportunity influence, and deal acceleration, etc.
* Support annual planning and forecasting by providing data\-driven insights into channel performance and pipeline contribution
* Translate business questions from leadership into analytical frameworks and measurable KPIs

**Campaign Operations**

* Support the execution of marketing programs across events, webinars, lifecycle, paid media, and field marketing
* Manage campaign setup, tracking, and attribution across systems including HubSpot, Salesforce, Bizzabo, Goldcast, Paid Ads, etc.
* Ensure campaigns are properly structured to capture performance data across the full funnel
* Maintain campaign taxonomy and naming conventions to ensure clean reporting
* Partner with the marketing team to ensure campaigns are properly instrumented and measurable

**Data, Analytics \& Attribution**

* Build and maintain dashboards that measure marketing performance across the funnel
* Develop and improve attribution models that measure how marketing influences pipeline and revenue
* Analyze campaign performance, pipeline creation, and conversion metrics to identify opportunities for improvement
* Partner with the data team to maintain marketing datasets and reporting infrastructure
* Help leadership understand the drivers of pipeline growth through clear reporting and insights

**Tooling \& Automation**

* Own the entire martech stack including HubSpot, Salesforce, Segment, among others.
* Maintain integrations between marketing, sales, and product data systems
* Improve lead routing, lifecycle management, and data enrichment processes
* Identify opportunities to automate manual workflows across marketing and sales operations
* Evaluate and implement new tools that improve the efficiency of the GTM organization

**How to be successful in this role:**

* 5\+ years in Marketing Operations
* Experience supporting annual planning, pipeline forecasting, and performance analysis to inform GTM strategy
* Deep expertise with HubSpot and Salesforce
* Strong SQL skills and experience working with modern data stacks (BigQuery, dbt, Reverse ETL, etc.)
* Familiarity with web data and event tracking tools (Segment, Google Tag Manager, server\-side tracking, etc.)
* Proficiency using AI tools and LLM\-powered workflows to improve productivity and automate GTM operations
* Experience operating in a warehouse\-first analytics environment, where the data warehouse serves as the source of truth for marketing and revenue reporting
* Ability to build dashboards and reporting in BI tools (Looker, Tableau, Hex, etc.)
* Experience with automation and integration platforms (Zapier, n8n, Workato, Make)
* Experience owning and scaling a marketing technology stack, including vendor evaluation, system optimization, and identifying tool redundancies
* Deep understanding of B2B SaaS funnels and gtm metrics
* Ability to diagnose and improve complex marketing and GTM systems
* Comfortable collaborating cross\-functionally with marketing, sales, data, and engineering teams

**Compensation \& Benefits**

* Compensation: We offer competitive compensation that includes base salary, meaningful equity, and benefits such as health and dental coverage, flexible vacation, a 401(k) plan, and life insurance. Actual compensation will vary based on role, level, and location. For team members in the EU and UK, we provide locally competitive benefits aligned with regional norms and regulations.
* Annual salary range: $160,000\- $240,000\.00

jobs.title: Martech – Adobe Experience Manager (AEM) Sites and Assets - Senior Manager
jobs.description:
At PwC, our people in data and analytics focus on leveraging data to drive insights and make informed business decisions. They utilise advanced analytics techniques to help clients optimise their operations and achieve their strategic goals. In data analysis at PwC, you will focus on utilising advanced analytical techniques to extract insights from large datasets and drive data\-driven decision\-making. You will leverage skills in data manipulation, visualisation, and statistical modelling to support clients in solving complex business problems.  

Growing as a strategic advisor, you leverage your influence, expertise, and network to deliver quality results. You motivate and coach others, coming together to solve complex problems. As you increase in autonomy, you apply sound judgment, recognising when to take action and when to escalate. You are expected to solve through complexity, ask thoughtful questions, and clearly communicate how things fit together. Your ability to develop and sustain high performing, diverse, and inclusive teams, and your commitment to excellence, contributes to the success of our Firm.


Examples of the skills, knowledge, and experiences you need to lead and deliver value at this level include but are not limited to:

* Craft and convey clear, impactful and engaging messages that tell a holistic story.
* Apply systems thinking to identify underlying problems and/or opportunities.
* Validate outcomes with clients, share alternative perspectives, and act on client feedback.
* Direct the team through complexity, demonstrating composure through ambiguous, challenging and uncertain situations.
* Deepen and evolve your expertise with a focus on staying relevant.
* Initiate open and honest coaching conversations at all levels.
* Make difficult decisions and take action to resolve issues hindering team effectiveness.
* Model and reinforce professional and technical standards (e.g. refer to specific PwC tax and audit guidance), the Firm's code of conduct, and independence requirements.

  

The Opportunity


As part of the Data and Analytics team you will lead transformative projects in Adobe Experience Manager (AEM) Sites and Assets. As a Senior Manager, you will serve as a strategic advisor, leveraging your knowledge to drive results and secure operational excellence while guiding top\-performing teams through complex challenges.


Responsibilities

* Foster collaboration and open communication among team members
* Implement innovative solutions to enhance project outcomes
* Analyze project performance and identify areas for enhancement
* Maintain adherence to professional standards and ethical guidelines


What You Must Have

* Bachelor's Degree
* At least 10 years of experience


What Sets You Apart

* Certification(s) preferred: Adobe Experience Manager Sites Architect Master, Agile certification (Scrum or SAFe), Project Management certification (PMP), or Cloud certification (AWS, Azure, or GCP)
* Leading complex CMS and DAM transformations
* Shaping and delivering AEM modernization programs
* Establishing governance, decision rights, and operating cadence with senior stakeholders and leading executive steering and escalation forums
* Developing cross\-functional teams and business development
* Driving adoption of content supply chain practices
* Identifying and managing delivery challenges
* Leading change management and communications to support platform adoption and sustainable ways of working

  

The salary range for this position is: $124,000 \- $280,000\. Actual compensation within the range will be dependent upon the individual's skills, experience, qualifications and location, and applicable employment laws. All hired individuals are eligible for an annual discretionary bonus. PwC offers a wide range of benefits, including medical, dental, vision, 401k, holiday pay, vacation, personal and family sick leave, and more. To view our benefits at a glance, please visit the following link: https://pwc.to/benefits\-at\-a\-glance


As PwC is an equal opportunity employer, all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment at PwC without regard to race; color; religion; national origin; sex (including pregnancy, sexual orientation, and gender identity); age; disability; genetic information (including family medical history); veteran, marital, or citizenship status; or, any other status protected by law.


PwC does not intend to hire experienced or entry level job seekers who will need, now or in the future, PwC sponsorship through the H\-1B lottery, except as set forth within the following policy: https://pwc.to/H\-1B\-Lottery\-Policy.


Learn more about how we work: https://pwc.to/how\-we\-work


For only those qualified applicants that are impacted by the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers, the Los Angeles' Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance, the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, San Diego County Fair Chance Ordinance, and the California Fair Chance Act, where applicable, arrest or conviction records will be considered for Employment in accordance with these laws. At PwC, we recognize that conviction records may have a direct, adverse, and negative relationship to responsibilities such as accessing sensitive company or customer information, handling proprietary assets, or collaborating closely with team members. We evaluate these factors thoughtfully to establish a secure and trusted workplace for all.
