**WORK AND PLAY IN THE MOUNTAINS**  

**Join our team of big mountain adventurers at the largest ski resort in Washington!** Here at Crystal, it's more than our work. It's our passion for the guest experience, the outdoors, and the community that bring us together at the end of the tree\-lined road. We come from near and far to be up on the mountain because it's an experience you just don't get anywhere else. Events are always on the calendar and friends are always down to play

jobs.title: Event Marketing Operations Associate
jobs.description:
**About Outreach**  

Outreach, founded in 2014, is the only complete **agentic AI** platform for revenue teams. Outreach infuses agentic AI, conversation intelligence, and assistive AI to power hundreds of use cases across revenue motions. From new logo prospecting to expansions, deal acceleration, driving retention, and forecasting, Outreach AI automates workflows and frees sellers to focus on more strategic conversations and actions. Revenue leaders benefit from connected account visibility, performance insights, and higher forecasting accuracy across every GTM team. World leading enterprise organizations use Outreach to power their revenue teams, including Databricks, SAP, Siemens, and Verizon to name a few.  

  

**The Role**
Outreach is seeking an Event Marketing Operations Associate to power the operational infrastructure behind our global event portfolio. This role will help scale and streamline the systems, processes, and financial operations that support more than 75\+ annual programs, including third\-party conferences, field marketing activations, and proprietary events.

Working closely with the Director of Global Events, Marketing Operations, Finance, and cross\-functional marketing teams, this role will ensure event programs run smoothly behind the scenes by managing procurement workflows, vendor onboarding, contract administration, marketing systems coordination, and project management infrastructure.

This position is ideal for someone who enjoys building structure, improving processes, and ensuring complex programs run efficiently at scale.

**Location:** We’re open to remote within the US or hybrid at one of our office locations (Seattle, Atlanta).

**Key Responsibilities**
### **Event Program Operations \& Project Infrastructure**

* Build and maintain Asana project frameworks for third\-party events, field marketing programs, and proprietary events to ensure consistent execution across the portfolio.
* Create scalable project templates, timelines, and task structures that support multiple concurrent events.
* Coordinate cross\-functional workflows across marketing, creative, brand, demand generation, and sales teams to ensure milestones and dependencies stay on track.
* Serve as a central operational partner for event program managers, ensuring visibility into deliverables, deadlines, and ownership.
* Maintain documentation and operational playbooks for event execution processes.
* Maintain the master event calendar and program tracker to ensure visibility across marketing leadership and cross\-functional teams.
### **Procurement, Vendor Management and Contracts**

* Support the procurement lifecycle for event vendors including vendor onboarding, contract routing, and internal approvals.
* Partner with legal, procurement, and finance teams to ensure contracts, SOWs, and vendor agreements are processed efficiently.
* Track contract status, payment terms, and key deadlines to ensure vendor obligations are met.
* Coordinate vendor setup within internal procurement and finance systems.
* Maintain vendor documentation and assist with RFP coordination and vendor evaluation processes when needed.
### **Budget, Finance and Invoice Management**

* Manage invoice processing, PO tracking, and payment coordination with finance and vendors.
* Maintain accurate financial documentation for event programs including contracts, invoices, and reconciliation records.
* Assist with post\-event financial reconciliation and reporting.
* Help ensure event spend aligns with approved budgets and procurement guidelines.
### **Marketing Operations and Event Technology**

* Partner with Marketing Operations to support event registration workflows, campaign tracking, and attendee data management.
* Ensure accurate attendee data capture, lead tracking, and post\-event data flow into marketing and sales systems.
* Support reporting workflows by helping maintain consistent event data and performance tracking.
### **Who You Are**

* 3\+ years experience in event operations, marketing operations, project management, or event marketing, preferably within B2B SaaS, technology, or high\-growth organizations.
* Highly operational and process\-oriented, with strong attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple workflows simultaneously.
* Experience coordinating vendor contracts, procurement processes, and invoice management.
* Comfortable working with project management platforms such as Asana or similar tools.
* Familiarity with marketing and event technologies such as Salesforce, Marketo, Cvent, or Bizzabo preferred.
* Strong communicator who enjoys working cross\-functionally across marketing, finance, legal, and sales teams.
* Ability to thrive in a fast\-paced environment supporting a high volume of events.
* Limited travel required, with occasional travel to support major flagship events.
$75,000 \- $95,000 a year
The annual base salary range for this role is **$75,000\-$95,000 USD**. You may also be offered incentive compensation, bonus, restricted stock units, and benefits. Actual compensation is based on factors such as the candidate's skills, qualifications, and experience. We also have a location\-based compensation structure; there may be a different range for candidates in other locations
\#LI\-AK1 **Why You’ll Love It Here*** Flexible time off
* 401k to help you save for the future
* Generous medical, dental, and vision coverage for full\-time employees and their dependents
* A parental leave program that includes options for a paid night nurse, and a gradual return to work
* Infertility/ assisted reproductive services benefit
* Employee referral bonuses to encourage the addition of great new people to the team
* Snacks and beverages in the Office, along with fun events to celebrate
* Diversity and inclusion programs that promote employee resource groups like Outreach Women's Network, Latinx community, Outreach Black Connection, AAPI community, Pride/LGBTQIA\+, Gender\+, Disability Community, and Veterans/Military

  

*Outreach is an equal opportunity employer. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran or disability status.* ***Our success is reliant on building teams that include people from different backgrounds and experiences who can elevate assumptions and ideas with fresh perspectives. We're dedicated to hiring the whole human, not just a resume. To that end, we look for a diverse pool of applicants\-including those from historically marginalized groups. We would like to invite you to apply even if you don't think you meet all of the requirements listed below. We don't want a few lines in a job description to get between us and the opportunity to meet you.***
We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

jobs.title: Director, Marketing Technology & CRM
jobs.description:
Description:
**The Why Behind Wellvana:**


Wellvana partners with primary care providers, hospitals and health systems to make the transition from fee\-for\-service to value\-based care life\-changing for patients and providers.


With more than a dozen ACOs, as well as Medicare Advantage contracts from coast to coast, Wellvana matches its partners with personalized paths in their value\-based care journey.


By offering multiple high\-touch delivery models, including guided physician enablement as well as embedded support featuring Foundational Care™ management, Wellvana layers in supporting services that help reduce the administrative load while delivering resources to patients that drive positive outcomes.


Founded in 2018 and based in Nashville, Tenn., Wellvana works with partners in over 40 states, caring for nearly a million lives across multiple payors.

**Clarity on the Role:**


The Director, Marketing Technology \& CRM will manage and continuously improve Wellvana’s marketing technology platforms—optimizing the systems that power personalized, compliant, and measurable engagement across partner and patient audiences.


You’ll live in our ESP and CRM platforms day\-to\-day—building campaigns, optimizing workflows, and ensuring our marketing technology stack runs like a well\-oiled machine. You’ll lead platform implementations when it’s time to stand up new tools, working hand\-in\-hand with engineering and IT to get integrations right. And you’ll bring a genuine curiosity about AI, piloting new features and workflows that make our systems smarter without overcomplicating them.


You’re equal parts operator and optimizer. You can build a multi\-step lifecycle journey in the morning and present campaign performance to leadership in the afternoon. You know what good data hygiene looks like, you obsess over deliverability, and you understand that the best technology in the world means nothing if nobody adopts it.


Whether you’ve managed martech platforms at a health system, a growing startup, or an agency—if you’ve driven real results through CRM and marketing automation, we want to talk. Candidates with healthcare experience have a head start, but strong platform operators from any industry should apply.


This position is hybrid and ideally works from Nashville headquarters at least 3 days per week. Some travel may be required.


The role lives on the Experience Team and reports to the Chief Experience Officer, working closely with Editorial, Growth, Product, Technology, Partner Success, Operations, Analytics, Engineering, and Executive Leadership. This is a people\-leader position.

**What’s Expected:**

* *The Platform Operator:* You manage and optimize Wellvana’s CRM and ESP platforms (Salesforce, Braze, or similar) on a daily basis: building campaigns, configuring automations, managing data fields, troubleshooting deliverability, and ensuring the team gets maximum value from every tool in our stack.
* *The Implementation Lead:* When it’s time to stand up a new platform or migrate off an old one, you lead the charge. You manage vendor evaluation, project timelines, data migration, configuration, testing, and go\-live. You work with engineering and IT to ensure integrations are properly built and maintained.
* *The Journey Builder:* You build and optimize partner and patient lifecycle journeys—onboarding sequences, activation campaigns, retention workflows, and re\-engagement triggers. You manage cross\-channel execution (email, SMS, direct mail) and continuously test to improve engagement and conversion.
* *The AI Explorer:* You have a genuine interest in AI and actively identify opportunities to pilot AI\-powered features within our platforms—predictive send times, AI\-generated subject lines, smart segmentation, dynamic content. You test, measure, and scale what works, championing responsible AI adoption across the marketing team.
* *The Data Steward:* You ensure CRM data quality, list hygiene, and compliance (CAN\-SPAM, HIPAA). You manage segmentation, reporting dashboards, and campaign attribution. You work with analytics to connect lifecycle activity to business outcomes and present performance to leadership.
* *The Collaborator:* You’re the go\-to person for marketing technology questions across the organization. You partner with Editorial, Growth, Partner Success, and Sales to ensure campaigns are properly built, audiences are properly segmented, and reporting is properly attributed. You train teammates on platform best practices.


Requirements:
**What’s Required:**

**Live Out the Wellvana Way**

* Find a way. Be accountable, see the work through, make it life\-changing.
* Ask the why question. The conventional wisdom is often wrong. Be curious and always connect your work back to the goal and audience.
* Move on what matters: Prioritize the work and be laser focused on what moves the needle.
* Expect to win: Confidence is contagious. Bring solutions.
* You can’t do it alone. And you’re not expected to. We work best when we’re a team. Silos slow us down. Collaboration and communication are the fast way... and the fun way.

**Characteristics of the Right Candidate**

* High\-energy, results\-driven marketing technologist with a desire to disrupt the current healthcare system and make it better for providers and patients.
* 5–7\+ years of hands\-on experience in CRM management, marketing automation, email marketing, or marketing operations.
* 3\+ years of experience leading or mentoring a team in a marketing technology or operations function.
* Deep, hands\-on proficiency in at least one major ESP/CRM platform (Salesforce Marketing Cloud, Braze, HubSpot, Marketo, or similar). You can build campaigns, troubleshoot issues, and train others.
* Proven experience leading platform implementations—vendor evaluation, project management, data migration, configuration, and go\-live.
* Working knowledge of how APIs and integrations connect CRM platforms to data warehouses, BI tools, and other systems. You don’t need to write the code, but you need to speak the language and partner effectively with engineering.
* Strong interest in AI\-powered marketing tools and a track record of piloting new platform features. Experience with AI\-driven personalization, predictive analytics, or generative AI tools is a plus—curiosity and willingness to learn is a must.
* Experience building lifecycle and journey programs—onboarding, nurture, retention, and re\-engagement—across email, SMS, and other channels.
* Familiarity with partner\-focused or B2B lifecycle marketing is a plus, especially in healthcare or value\-based care settings.
* Solid understanding of email deliverability, data hygiene, segmentation best practices, and compliance (CAN\-SPAM, HIPAA awareness).
* Comfortable with reporting and analytics—you can build dashboards, track KPIs, and present campaign performance to leadership.
* HTML email template knowledge required. SQL familiarity a plus.
* Bachelor’s degree in marketing, communications, business, information systems, or similar.
* Strong communicator who can translate platform capabilities into business terms and make recommendations that stick.
* An individual who sees ambiguity as an opportunity, not an obstacle.
* Healthcare\-related experience a plus. But hey, we’re all learning here. Varied perspectives make us stronger.

jobs.title: Manager, Marketing Operations & Agency Partnerships
jobs.description:
Welcome to Haleon. We’re a purpose\-driven, world\-class consumer company putting everyday health in the hands of millions. In just three years since our launch, we’ve grown, evolved and are now entering an exciting new chapter – one filled with bold ambitions and enormous opportunity.  

  

Our trusted portfolio of brands – including Sensodyne®, Panadol®, Advil®, Voltaren®, Theraflu®, Otrivin®, and Centrum® – lead in resilient and growing categories. What sets us apart is our unique blend of deep human understanding and trusted science.  

  

Now it’s time to fully realise the full potential of our business and our people. We do this through our Win as One strategy. It puts our purpose – to deliver better everyday health with humanity – at the heart of everything we do. It unites us, inspires us, and challenges us to be better every day, driven by our agile, performance\-focused culture.
The Manager, Marketing Operations \& Agency Partnerships leads our marketing and business planning processes supporting the US CMO team and critical US enterprise marketing initiatives. The ideal candidate will have a deep understanding of marketing and business planning, data analytics, and process optimization, and will be responsible for ensuring the efficiency and effectiveness of our marketing programs. This role is critical in driving the strategic direction and operational execution of our marketing initiatives.
This role will provide YOU the opportunity to lead key activities to progress YOUR career. These responsibilities include some of the following:* Lead and Manage Marketing Operations: Oversee the day\-to\-day operations of the marketing team, ensuring alignment with the company’s strategic objectives.
* Business planning and portfolio management: Partner with Categories and key cross functional team members (finance, sales, portfolio strategy) to coordinate key planning, tracking, and business plan optimization activities including MBR, Marketing Leadership Team across CMO, and annual brand planning (Commercial and comms planning)
* Data Analytics Capabilities: Develop and maintain common approach and processes across category teams to ensure consistent business performance analysis and action\-oriented insights to inform marketing strategies and in\-year decision making. Elevate the role of the Marketing Organization and Tools available to drive consistency across “How to” build superior brand and execution.
* Budget Management: Manage the marketing T\&E budget, ensuring resources are allocated effectively and efficiently across category and enterprise teams. Monitor spending to ensure alignment with in\-year business objectives.
* Process Optimization: Continuously evaluate and refine marketing and E2E business processes (e.g. F4G, Hercules, etc) to improve productivity, reduce costs, and enhance the overall effectiveness of marketing operations.
* Team Leadership and Development: This position has no direct reports, but it is expected to have a high level of influence across the CMO team and all three Categories organization to drive deliverables, and to foster a culture of innovation, collaboration, and continuous improvement.
* Vendor and Stakeholder Management: Manage relationships with external agency partners, ensuring they deliver quality services that meet the company’s needs. Coordinate with internal stakeholders to ensure alignment on marketing strategies and objectives.

*Why you?*
Basic Qualifications:
We are looking for professionals with these required skills to achieve our goals:* Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business Administration, or a related field;
* 5\+ years of experience in marketing operations or a related role, with at least 3 years in a leadership position.
* Strong knowledge of marketing technology platforms, including CRM, marketing automation, and analytics tools.
* Proven track record in managing marketing budgets and optimizing marketing processes.
* Exceptional analytical skills with the ability to interpret complex data and provide actionable insights.
* Excellent project management skills with the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously.
* Strong leadership and team management skills, with a focus on mentoring and developing talent.
* Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work effectively with cross\-functional teams and external partners.
* Strong attention to detail and a commitment to delivering high\-quality work.


Preferred Qualifications:
If you have the following characteristics, it would be a plus:* MBA
* Prior experience working within matrixed organizations on global brands

* The position is based in Warren, NJ, and follows a hybrid model, requiring an in\-office presence of 3 days per week.

*The salary range for this role is $140,596 \- $193,319\.5 USD* *annually \+ bonus*
Benefits
Haleon offers a robust Total Reward package that consists of competitive pay and a comprehensive benefits program. This includes a generous 401(k) plan, tuition reimbursement and time off programs including 6 months paid parental leave. On day one, you are eligible for benefits, including our healthcare programs where the company pays for the majority of your medical coverage for you and your family. We also offer the opportunity to receive a discretionary bonus based on the achievement of key business performance and other incentive/recognition programs as part of the offering.  

Job Posting End Date
2026\-03\-24  

 Equal Opportunities  

Haleon are committed to mobilising our purpose in a way that represents the diverse consumers and communities who rely on our brands every day. It guides us in creating an inclusive culture, where different backgrounds and views are valued and respected – all in support of understanding and best serving the needs of our consumers and unleashing the full potential of our people. It’s important to us that Haleon is a place where all our employees feel they truly belong.  

  

During the application process, we may ask you to share some personal information, which is entirely voluntary. This information ensures we meet certain regulatory and reporting obligations and supports the development, refinement, and execution of our inclusion and belonging programmes that are open to all Haleon employees.  

  

The personal information you provide will be kept confidential, used only for legitimate business purposes, and will never be used in making any employment decisions, including hiring decisions.  

Please note that if you are a US Licensed Healthcare Professional or Healthcare Professional as defined by the laws of the state issuing your license, Haleon may be required to capture and report expenses Haleon incurs, on your behalf, in the event you are afforded an interview for employment. This capture of applicable transfers of value is necessary to ensure Haleon’s compliance to all federal and state US Transparency requirements.  

Accommodation Requests

jobs.title: Senior Marketing Automation Specialist
jobs.description:
Join Triumph!
At Triumph, our vision is a world where freight transactions are accurate and seamless on the most modern and secure freight transaction network. That’s why we’re looking for passionate, innovative, solutions\-oriented people to join our team. We thrive on providing exceptional customer service and we look for team members with an entrepreneurial spirit and a passion to build successful partnerships with our clients. Because at the end of the day our goal is to help our partners businesses run better.
Position Summary: The Senior Marketing Automation Specialist will work with the marketing team and business lines to plan, execute and measure marketing campaigns. The Senior Marketing Automation Specialist will support the VP, Marketing Operations Manager in the ongoing management of the Marketo Automation Platform while supporting campaign execution. This role will serve as technical support for all Marketo campaign issues while building a technical knowledge base to support marketing automation activities. This role will also work with brand and shared services teams to build and implement necessary campaign deliverables.
ESSENTIAL DUTIES \& RESPONSIBILITIES* Work with brand and shared services teams to build and implement necessary campaign deliverables such as email design, graphics, applicable tracking links, etc.
* Uses advanced Marketo knowledge to work with digital marketing manager to develop comprehensive marketing automation campaigns that support brand goals for the year.
* Manage relationships and regularly interface with Marketo customer support and Marketo implementation partner.
* Work with brand lines and stakeholders to define KPIs while working with Marketing Operations manager to build campaign reporting.
* Plan and perform A/B testing to define and execute enhancements to productivity, conversion rates, programs/campaign ROI and sales growth.
* Manages brand assets in Marketo with consistent taxonomy while working with creative team to archive campaign assets in the digital asset management platform.
* Stays aware of new Marketo updates, trends in marketing automation and best practices while educating the marketing team.
* Applies advanced knowledge of Marketo to strategic decision making and campaign direction.
* Work with VP, Marketing Operations Manager to build alignment with Executive team, Business Partners, Shared Services, IT, and Operational Support including the CX and Salesforce teams.
* Understand marketing OKR’s to effectively manage incoming project queue, requested deliverables, and tasks.
* Work with the marketing organization at large to define and manage team processes, hand\-off points, document workflows, and set agreed upon service level agreements.
* Work with brand and compliance teams to adhere to compliance and regulatory standards and make applicable changes to assets when necessary.
* The ideal Senior Marketing Automation Specialist candidate will be a dedicated and disciplined professional who excels in a team environment.


EXPERIENCE \& EDUCATION* Bachelor's Degree in Marketing, Computer Science, IT, or related field, or equivalent work experience.
* 4\+ years’ experience developing and managing marketing automation campaigns via Salesforce or similar CRM.
* 3\+ experience in a banking/financial services/commercial finance environment preferred or equivalent experience in similar environment
* Excellent understanding of digital marketing and social media landscape as well as marketing automation best practices
* Advertising or digital ad agency experience a plus
* Marketo certified expert preferred.


SKILLS \& ABILITIES REQUIRED* Strategic Knowledge and Professional Abilities
	+ Proficiency with HTML, CSS design, cross\-browser and cross\-platform compatibility, firewalls (functionality and maintenance), and Javascript
	+ Working knowledge of Salesforce
	+ Familiarity with Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) and Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG), and the ability to make recommendations for appropriate updates to email and web content.
	+ Strong proficiency with Microsoft Office and Adobe environment.

* Partnership and Relationship Building Skills
	+ Build partnerships to proactively collaborate on goals, objectives, programs and initiatives that promote brand image, community, products \& services and employer recognition. This may include any of the above that cut across the organization to keep team members informed.
	+ Team player with a strong work ethic. Demonstrates considerable tact, diplomacy, discretion and judgment with ability to build/maintain effective interpersonal relationships
	+ High level of maturity and stability with an "optimistic" approach
	+ A self\-starter able to be both entrepreneurial and strategic in approach with hands\-on experience


Work Environment
The work environment characteristics described here maybe encountered while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.* Moderate noise (i.e. business office with computers, phone, and printers, light traffic).
* Ability to work in a confined area.
* Ability to sit at a computer terminal for an extended period of time. Occasional stooping or kneeling may be necessary.
* While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to stand, sit, talk, hear and use hands and fingers to operate a computer keyboard and telephone.
* Specific vision abilities are required by this job due to computer work.
* Light to moderate lifting is required.
* Regular, predictable attendance is required.
* Add any travel or other physical requirements, as needed

*Triumph Financial, Inc. and its subsidiaries, provide equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, national origin, age, disability, genetic information, marital status, or status as a covered veteran in accordance with applicable federal, state and local laws.*
We offer Medical, Dental, Vision, Paid Time Off, 401k and much more.
Go on. Do it. Apply Today!

jobs.title: Marketing Operations Manager
jobs.description:
**Hiring Manager:** Head of Brand Marketing

**Job Title:** Senior Marketing Operations Manager

**Job Type** FT W2

**Location**: Remote




**Who We Are**


Join our team and help transform lives by delivering life\-changing treatments and exceptional care. We offer a collaborative environment with competitive compensation, professional growth opportunities, and a mission\-driven focus on maximizing value for both our business and customers. With a goal\-centric culture that encourages risk\-taking, self\-empowerment, and transparency, you'll thrive in a fast\-paced setting where making an impact is key.




**Our Mission**


We are on a mission to tackle America's metabolic health crisis by educating the public on its challenges and offering safe, science\-backed treatments. With over 80 million Americans affected and $600 billion in annual medical costs attributed to metabolic disorders, we provide accessible solutions that empower people to improve their health and maintain lasting results. Our goal is simple: transform lives through education and effective, outcomes\-driven care.  

  

**About the Role**  

We’re hiring a Growth Marketing Manager who launches and keeps cross\-channel campaigns moving fast and clean. This is a growth role with heavy marketing project management. You will drive initiatives from brief to live, partner with channel owners, and make sure everything goes out on time, on brand, and compliant. You will also support planning and optimization by using data to recommend what to test next.

**Your Responsibilities**  

* Own marketing project management for cross\-channel campaigns and digital initiatives
* Build timelines, briefs, dependencies, and clear deliverables
* Coordinate assets, approvals, tracking, offers, disclaimers, and QA
* Ensure launch readiness across paid, lifecycle, web, partnerships, and CRO
* Review performance signals (CAC/CPA, CVR, retention) and recommend tests
* Use AI tools to speed up planning, QA, documentation, and execution



**What You Bring to Eden**  

* You like running point on projects and keeping teams aligned
* You move fast, stay organized, and do not drop details
* You can push timelines and unblock work without drama
* You care about doing things the right way in regulated marketing
* You enjoy learning, testing, and improving what ships
**Requirements**  

* 3\-5 years in growth marketing, digital marketing, or campaign operations
* MUST have marketing project management experience across campaigns and digital launches
* Strong understanding of the full\-funnel journey and why small errors (tracking, links, disclaimers) can break performance
* Comfortable owning the execution “pipes” of marketing: lifecycle handoffs, web execution, tracking links, and launch readiness
* Strong in project management tools; Figma familiarity is a must
* AI\-savvy and uses automation to reduce manual work and improve speed and QA
* High attention to detail; experience in regulated or compliance\-heavy environments is a strong plus
* Comfortable working across teams and channels (paid, lifecycle, web, creative, CRO)
* EST work overlap required
* Ideal background on telehealth, healthcare, supplements, or wellness; agency and/or startups
**This Role Is Not For**  

* Someone looking to own paid media budgets, channel strategy, or P\&L
* Someone who wants to focus only on creative or brand strategy
* Someone who needs slow decision cycles and heavy structure
**Salary**  

$70k–$90k

**KPIs**  

* Launch 5 cross\-channel campaigns per month with greater than or equal to 90% on\-time delivery
* Maintain greater than or equal to 98% QA accuracy on launches (tracking, links, offers, compliance)
* Reduce brief\-to\-live cycle time by 20% within the first 90 days

  

**Housekeeping**
Eden is proud to be an equal\-opportunity employer. We provide employment opportunities regardless of age, race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, disability, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, veteran status, or any other protected class. Each individual at Eden brings their own perspectives, work experiences, lifestyles, and cultures with them, and we believe that a more diverse team creates more innovative products, provides better services to customers, and helps us all grow and learn.




Compensation at Eden will be determined by skills, experience, and geographic location.




At the launch of each position, we benchmark compensation to the appropriate role and level utilizing competitive compensation data from various data sources as references. At the offer stage, we use the signal we received from our interviews coupled with your experience, location, and other job\-related factors to determine final compensation.




California Consumer Privacy Act Notice

jobs.title: Manager of MarTech
jobs.description:
**EōS Fitness is bold, upbeat, and motivating. Not only in our fitness routines, but in our work environment as well. Like our members, our employees know how to rise to a challenge and handle it head on. EōS Fitness' Core Purpose is to create loyal, lifelong fans and exercise practitioners. To achieve this purpose, our Core Values are: Take Personal Responsibility, Be Optimistic \& Have Fun, Learn \& Grow, Be A Team Player, and Fight Hard To Win.**


**Core Purpose: To create loyal, lifelong fans and exercise practitioners.**


Sound like you? We're in search of a talented Manager of MarTech to join our Marketing Department to play a critical role in advancing our marketing technology ecosystem and ensuring data integrity across the organization. Reporting directly to the Senior Director of Marketing Technology, this role combines technical expertise with strategic collaboration—acting as a bridge between Marketing, IT, and external partners. This position is based out of Addison/Farmers Branch, TX (across the street from the Galleria Mall) and would join a team to support 225\+ gym locations that are currently open or coming soon across nationwide. The position is a hybrid full\-time, Monday thru Friday, with Mondays and Fridays being in\-office days and Tue\-Thu as optional to work from home or be in the office.



If you have a passion for health, fitness and marketing, and you enjoy working in a fast\-paced, challenging environment, we'd love to get to know you!


**Responsibilities**:


**Data Governance \& Quality Management**


* Lead data governance standards across marketing systems, ensuring data accuracy, consistency, and compliance.
* Monitor and maintain data hygiene, including deduplication, identity resolution, and system integrations within marketing systems.


**Analytics \& Business Intelligence**


* Support marketing analytics initiatives (BI experience is a plus, but not required)
* Work closely with the analytics team to develop BI dashboards.
* Translate marketing data into actionable insights and recommendations.


**Marketing Technology Project Support**


* Participate in the rollout of new marketing technology solutions through rigorous quality assurance testing.
* Document test plans, identify defects, validate functionality, and ensure systems meet requirements prior to launch.
* Support company initiatives, including documentation, training materials, and user enablement.
* Support audience segmentation within CDP platform for ad hoc initiatives and campaigns


**Platform Operations \& Optimization**


* Oversee daily operations, monitoring, and troubleshooting for key marketing platforms, including: **Klaviyo** (email/SMS marketing automation), **Monday.com** (project/workflow management/service tool), **Amperity** (customer data platform)
* Identify opportunities to improve workflows, integrations, and platform performance.

  


**We're Looking for a candidate that has**:


* 5\+ years of experience in marketing technology, analytics, or related technical marketing operations role.
* Some experience with business intelligence platforms (Power BI, Tableau, Looker, etc.).
* Hands‑on experience with MarTech systems such as email/SMS platforms, CDPs, workflow tools, or CRM systems.
* Solid understanding of data structures, data governance principles, and system integrations.
* Experience participating in or leading QA processes for technology rollouts.
* Ability to communicate effectively with both technical and non‑technical stakeholders.
* Strong analytical, problem‑solving, and project‑management skills.
* Familiarity with APIs, ETL processes, customer data pipelines, and data mapping.
* Knowledge of marketing attribution modeling or advanced analytics practices.
* Background in working within multi‑location or franchise business models (if relevant).
* Experience collaborating directly with IT, data engineering, or enterprise architecture teams.

**Benefits and Perks:**


* A highly energetic and collaborative team.
* A management team that cares about your professional development.
* Free membership for you and plus one family member.
* Discounted Personal Training, and merchandise – including supplements.
* Employee referral program.
* Daily Pay offered \- access your funds before payday.
* Competitive pay plus vacation, holiday, and sick pay.\*
* Group Health, dental, vision, Supplemental Life, and Employee Assistant Program are offered!\*
* 401(k) \+ Company matching!\*
* Personal education growth options with Sophia Learning.


* Based on eligibility of tenure and full\-time vs. part\-time employment.



EōS Fitness is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. Employment decisions are based on qualifications, merit, and business needs, without regard to race, color, religion, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, age, disability, or any other legally protected status.



To view more information on our CCPA policy, click HERE.


*EEO is The Law \- click here for more information*  

*Equal Opportunity Employer Minorities/Women/Protected Veterans/Disabled*

  


EōS Fitness participates in the government eVerify program. *Please review the details of this program by clicking here*.



We endeavor to make this site accessible to any and all users. If you would like to contact us regarding the accessibility of our website or need assistance completing the application process, please contact the Recruiting Help Line at recruitinghelpline@eosfitness.com or by calling 949\.309\.4182\. To check on the status of your application, please visit the Greenhouse Candidate Portal at my.greenhouse.com for updates.

jobs.title: Marketing Operations Manager
jobs.description:
**Treasure Data:**


At Treasure Data, we’re on a mission to radically simplify how companies use data and AI to create connected customer experiences. Our intelligent customer data platform (CDP) drives revenue growth and operational efficiency across the enterprise to deliver powerful business outcomes.  

  



We are thrilled that Forrester has recognized Treasure Data as a Leader in The Forrester Wave: Customer Data Platforms For B2C. It's an honor to be acknowledged for our efforts in advancing the CDP industry with cutting\-edge AI and real\-time capabilities.

  

Furthermore, Treasure Data employees are enthusiastic, data\-driven, and customer\-obsessed. We are a team of drivers—self\-starters who take initiative, anticipate needs, and proactively jump in to solve problems. Our actions reflect our values of honesty, reliability, openness, and humility.

 **Your Role:**


We are seeking a Marketing Operations Manager to build, own, and optimize our marketing systems and processes with HubSpot Marketing Hub and our own proprietary AI\-native CDP at the center of our demand engine. This role is responsible for turning strategy into scalable, data\-driven execution across the full lead lifecycle.


You will serve as the system owner for HubSpot Marketing Hub, and other integrated tools, designing the architecture, governance, and operating model that Marketing, Sales, and RevOps rely on every day. You will translate business requirements into AI\-native workflows, automation, and data structures that enable predictable pipeline generation and clear performance insights.

**Responsibilities:**

* Own day\-to\-day administration and long\-term roadmap for all marketing systems and tools, centered on HubSpot’s Marketing Hub and our own proprietary AI\-native customer data platform
* Design and maintain lifecycle stages, lead and account scoring models, and routing rules that align with our go\-to\-market strategy
* Build, optimize, and document AI\-driven workflows for lead capture, enrichment, nurture, handoff to Sales, and post\-sales engagement
* Manage the infrastructure for emails, landing pages, forms, and subscriptions, ensuring brand consistency, compliance, and deliverability
* Define and enforce data standards, perform regular data quality audits, and partner with RevOps and IT on integrations
* Develop and maintain dashboards and reporting for funnel health, campaign performance, and attribution, enabling data\-driven decisions across go\-to\-market teams
* Evaluate, implement, and manage complementary martech tools and integrations that extend the value of HubSpot
* Enable and train stakeholders on best practices, processes, and usage of all marketing systems, tools and reporting

 **Job Requirements:**

* 4\+ years in Marketing Operations or related role within an Enterprise SaaS B2B environment
* Deep, hands\-on expertise with HubSpot Marketing Hub (admin\-level configuration, workflows, and reporting) and a proven record of successfully implementing and operating new tools, systems and reporting more generally
* Strong understanding of demand generation, lead lifecycle management, and funnel metrics in the context of managing a connected marketing tech stack
* Analytical mindset with the ability to translate insights into system, process and reporting improvements
* Clear communicator who can collaborate effectively with Marketing, Sales, and technical teams, and who documents processes thoroughly
* Possesses strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to effectively collaborate and align with cross\-functional teams across multiple regions

**Physical Requirements:**


Must be in the office at least 3x per week.

**Travel Requirements:**


1\-2 in\-person team meetings per quarter in one of our major regions: Americas, Japan or EMEA.  

  

**Perks and Benefits (US):**


Our benefit package showcases our culture of care and empathy with

* Comprehensive medical, dental, vision plans and Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
* Competitive compensation packages
* Company paid life insurance 3x salary
* Company paid short\- and long\-term disability coverage
* Retirement planning (401K) with 4% company match
* Restricted Stock Units (RSU)
* Flexible Time Off (FTO)
* Up to 26 weeks paid parental leave including a post\-partum night nurse
* Comprehensive support and access to care for everyone, everywhere through Carrot \- our global reproductive health and family\-building benefit.

**Our Dedication to You:**


We value and promote diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging in all aspects of our business and at all levels. Success comes from acknowledging, welcoming, and incorporating diverse perspectives.


Diverse representation alone is not the desired outcome. We also strive to create an inclusive culture that encourages growth, ownership of your role, and achieving innovation in new and unique ways. Your voice will be heard, and we will help amplify it.  

  



**Agencies and Recruiters:**


We cannot consider your candidate(s) without a contract in place. Any resumes received without having an active agreement will be considered gratis referrals to us. Thank you for your understanding and cooperation!

jobs.title: Marketing Operations Associate (contract)
jobs.description:
At BNY, our culture allows us to run our company better and enables you to grow and succeed. As a leading global financial services company at the heart of the global financial system, we influence nearly 20% of the world’s investible assets. Every day, our teams harness cutting\-edge AI and breakthrough technologies to collaborate with clients, driving transformative solutions that redefine industries and uplift communities worldwide.


Recognized as a top destination for innovators and champions of inclusion, BNY is where bold ideas meet advanced technology and exceptional talent. Together, we power the future of finance – and this is what \#LifeAtBNY is all about. Join us and be part of something extraordinary.

**Job Summary:**  

The Marketing Operations Associate supports the execution of digital marketing campaigns and webinar\-based events across the marketing technology ecosystem. This role helps ensure marketing initiatives are launched efficiently, event platforms are utilized effectively, and performance insights are captured to inform future strategies.


The position focuses heavily on supporting webinar and event technologies such as ON24 and Cvent, assisting with campaign execution, managing registration and communications workflows, and ensuring data from marketing activities flows correctly into reporting and CRM systems.

**Key Responsibilities:**

* **Event Platform Support:** Support the setup and execution of webinars and digital events using platforms such as ON24 and Cvent, including configuring registration pages, audience engagement tools, polls, chat functionality, and post\-event follow\-ups. Assist with event creation, communications, and ensuring events align with established templates and governance standards.
* **Campaign Operations:** Assist with marketing campaign execution by supporting audience list creation, email communications, and event\-related marketing workflows within marketing automation platforms. Partner with marketing teams to ensure alignment between webinars, email outreach, and broader marketing initiatives.
* **Marketing Technology \& Data Flow:** Ensure event and campaign data flows correctly between marketing platforms and connected systems. Identify and troubleshoot potential sync or data flow issues and assist with maintaining platform configurations and documentation.
* **Reporting \& Insights:** Produce reports on event and campaign performance including registrations, attendance, and engagement metrics. Support the development of performance insights to help improve future marketing programs and ensure data accuracy for reporting and analytics.
* **Cross\-Functional Collaboration:** Work closely with marketing teams, campaign managers, and operations stakeholders to support the successful delivery of marketing initiatives. Provide support during event preparation, dry runs, and live events to ensure programs run smoothly.

**Qualifications:**

* 2–4 years of experience in marketing operations, digital marketing, or event marketing
* Experience supporting webinar or event platforms such as ON24, Cvent, Zoom, Webex, or similar technologies
* Familiarity with marketing automation platforms such as Marketo, HubSpot, or similar tools
* Strong attention to detail with the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously
* Analytical mindset with experience generating performance reports and insights
* Bachelor’s degree in marketing, communications, business, or a related field (or equivalent experience)

**Preferred Experience:**

* Experience working with CRM platforms such as Salesforce or Microsoft Dynamics 365
* Understanding of campaign operations, lead management, and marketing analytics
* Experience working within integrated marketing technology environments
* Ability to identify opportunities to improve processes, platform usage, and operational efficiency

**Why Join Us:**  

This role provides the opportunity to work within a global marketing organization supporting high\-impact digital campaigns and events. The Marketing Operations Associate will gain hands\-on experience with modern marketing technologies, webinar platforms, and campaign execution while helping deliver engaging marketing programs and measurable performance outcomes.


At BNY, our culture speaks for itself, check out the latest BNY news at:


BNY Newsroom


BNY LinkedIn


Here’s a few of our recent awards:


America’s Most Innovative Companies, Fortune, 2025


World’s Most Admired Companies, Fortune 2025


“Most Just Companies”, Just Capital and CNBC, 2025


**Pay Rate Range**


58 \- 72\.5 USD hourly


**Additional Notes**


*Applications will be accepted on an ongoing basis.*

 *This posting is for a contract assignment with Tundra Technical Solutions to provide services to Bank of New York (BNY). Please note that this is not a full\-time employment opportunity. Candidates selected for this role will be engaged as contractors for the specified duration of the project. For any inquiries regarding the terms of the contract or engagement, please contact Tundra Technical Solutions directly.*


**Benefits Information**


*Optional benefits offering include medical, dental, vision and retirement benefits via Tundra Technical Solutions.*
jobs.description_snippet:
At BNY, our culture allows us to run our company better and enables you to grow and succeed. As a leading global financial services company at the heart of the global financial system, we influence nearly 20% of the world’s investible assets. jobs.title: Marketing Automation Campaign Manager
jobs.description:
**Requisition Number:** 221794

**Job Description**
-------------------


Cintas is looking for a highly skilled and experienced candidate to become a Campaign Manager who will work on our Digital Technology team and be responsible for campaign strategy and execution through our marketing automation platform. This role is responsible for planning, executing, and optimizing demand generation campaigns and ensuring campaigns are accurately built, targeted, tested, and delivered on time, while maintaining data integrity and operational excellence. This role will oversee campaign workflows, segmentation, lead routing, and performance tracking, translating marketing strategy into scalable, measurable execution that drives pipeline and revenue impact.  

Responsibilities:

* Build, execute, and maintain demand generation campaigns within the marketing automation platform, including email, nurture, events, and integrated digital programs.
* Translate campaign strategy and requirements into accurate technical execution, ensuring proper setup of workflows, triggers, scoring, and lead lifecycle stages.
* Manage audience segmentation and targeting to ensure campaigns reach the right buyers at the right time, leveraging firmographic, behavioral, and intent data.
* Ensure accurate lead capture, routing, and handoff to sales by maintaining alignment with CRM systems and sales operations processes.
* Perform quality assurance and testing on all campaigns prior to launch to validate logic, personalization, tracking, and data flow.
* Monitor campaign performance and operational health, troubleshooting issues related to delivery, data integrity, or system errors.
* Maintain campaign documentation, naming conventions, and operational standards to support scalability and consistency across programs.
* Advise internal teams and stakeholders on best practices for marketing automation, campaign design, and audience segmentation to maximize campaign impact.
* Collaborate with marketing managers, content and creative teams to align campaign objectives and target audiences.
* Continuously evaluate and refine campaign processes, leveraging data and industry benchmarks to drive innovation and performance.
* Stay up\-to\-date with the latest industry trends, tools, and best practices in digital marketing to ensure objectives align with business goals.
* Prepare detailed campaign performance reports and presentations for stakeholders, highlighting key findings and recommendations.
* Work collaboratively and partner with cross functional leaders within marketing, IT, and other functional departments to ensure alignment with overarching marketing and business goals.
**Skills/Qualifications**
-------------------------


Required

* 6\+ years of experience in digital campaign operations or digital marketing, with hands‑on experience executing demand generation campaigns within a marketing automation platform.
* High School Diploma or GED required
  

Preferred

* Strong proficiency with enterprise marketing automation platforms (e.g., Marketo, HubSpot, Eloqua, Pardot, Emarsys), including campaign builds, workflows, and troubleshooting.
* Solid understanding of B2B demand generation concepts, including lead management, nurturing, scoring, and pipeline attribution.
* Exceptional project management skills with the capability to manage multiple high\-stakes projects simultaneously and ensure their successful completion within scope, time, and budget constraints.
* Strong analytical prowess, with the ability to interpret complex data sets and translate them into actionable marketing strategies.
  

Benefits  

Cintas offers comprehensive and competitive medical, dental and vision benefits, with premiums below the national average. We offer flexibility with four different medical plan options; one plan is offered at zero cost.

  

Additionally, our employee\-partners enjoy:

* Competitive Pay
* 401(k) with Company Match/Profit Sharing/Employee Stock Ownership Plan (ESOP)
* Disability, Life and AD\&D Insurance, 100% Company Paid
* Paid Time Off and Holidays
* Skills Development, Training and Career Advancement Opportunities
  

Company Information  

Cintas Corporation helps more than one million businesses of all types and sizes get Ready™ to open their doors with confidence every day by providing products and services that help keep their customers’ facilities and employees clean, safe, and looking their best. With offerings including uniforms, mats, mops, towels, restroom supplies, workplace water services, first aid and safety products, eye\-wash stations, safety training, fire extinguishers, sprinkler systems and alarm service, Cintas helps customers get Ready for the Workday®. Headquartered in the U.S., Cincinnati, OH, Cintas is a publicly held Fortune 500 company traded over the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the symbol CTAS and is a component of both the Standard \& Poor’s 500 Index and Nasdaq\-100 Index.

  

Cintas Corporation is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, age, genetic information, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic or category protected by local, state, or federal law.

  

This job posting will remain open for at least five (5\) days.

 **Job Category:** Information Technology  

**Organization:** Corporate  

**Employee Status:** Regular  

**Schedule:** Full Time  

**Shift:** 1st Shift

jobs.title: Marketing Operations Associate
jobs.description:
**Laerdal Medical Benefits Include:**

* Generous PTO \& Holidays
* Comprehensive Medical, Dental, and Vision
* HSA \& FSA Account Options, with employer HSA contribution
* 401k with Employer Match \& Profit Sharing
* Tuition Reimbursement
* Wellness Reimbursement Program
* Professional Development Opportunities
* Pet Insurance
* Subsidized Cafeteria in NY based office


Position Overview:


The Marketing Operations Manager is responsible for enabling and executing marketing programs across multiple business segments by managing campaigns, workflows, advertising operations, and reporting infrastructure. The role serves as a central operational partner to marketing, sales, and internal teams, ensuring initiatives are delivered efficiently, accurately, and with strong cross‑functional coordination.  

  

This position translates marketing plans into executable workstreams, manages timelines and dependencies, supports campaign data integrity and reporting, and provides operational support to internal stakeholders and external ad partners. Success in this role is defined by execution reliability, process discipline, and the ability to support multiple teams and priorities simultaneously.


Responsibilities:

**Marketing Program \& Campaign Operations (Cross‑Segment)**

  

* Support execution of marketing campaigns across multiple segments and audiences, including email, social, paid media, webinars, and promotional programs.
* Develop target lists for all campaigns, ensuring alignment to segment, portfolio, and campaign objectives.
* Manage lead cleansing and upload from events, conferences, webinars, and SUNs to ensure data accuracy and usability.
* Coordinate campaign logistics such as timelines, approvals, asset readiness, segmentation, and distribution.
* Contribute to cross‑functional campaign collaboration, supporting coordination between marketing, sales, and other internal stakeholders.
* Manage and coordinate virtual marketing events such as webinars and Product Showcases, including scheduling, arranging resources and speakers, and managing end‑to‑end process flows, supporting each segment team.

**Workflow \& Project Management**

  

* Manage marketing tasks and workstreams within project management systems, ensuring clear scope, deadlines, and accountability.
* Manage the Marketing Connections Meeting, including setting agendas, finalizing monthly presentations, incorporating engagement elements, and distributing meeting minutes.
* Coordinate reviews and approvals across marketing, sales, and leadership stakeholders.
* Track progress, flag risks, and provide regular execution status updates to marketing leadership.
* Manage weekly Marketing Minutes meeting.
* Manage agendas for team meetings.

 **Sales \& Internal Enablement**

  

* Support sales enablement across segments.
* Function as an operational point of contact for sales and internal teams regarding marketing timelines, materials, and execution details.

**Ad Partner \& Stakeholder Coordination (Operational Support)**

  

* Manage advertising operations, including spend tracking, calendar management, placement coordination, and preparation of advertising materials by portfolio.
* Provide operational support for marketing activities involving external ad partners, associations, and vendors across segments.
* Organize and facilitate product photo shoots and video shoots, both inhouse and at customer or client sites as needed.
* Coordinate asset delivery, approvals, and communications to ensure ad partners and internal teams have what is needed for execution.
* Maintain organized documentation of ad partner‑related marketing activities and deliverables.

**Reporting, Tracking \& Documentation**

  

* Track executional status of marketing initiatives across segments, maintaining visibility into what is completed, in review, or in progress.
* Support analytic reporting development, including contributions to LMM‑related reporting.
* Maintain organized records of assets, communications, workflows, marketing process documentation, and templates.
* Support marketing projects as assigned and assist the team as needed during peak periods or special initiatives.
* Provide operational marketing support to internal teams as needed, including Customer Success, Government, National
* Accounts, GPO's, including Sourcewell and Kinetic, and the Networking team.


Requirements:

* Ideal candidate must reside in East Coast or Central Time Zones
* Bachelor’s degree in an applicable discipline required, master’s degree preferred
* 3–6 years in marketing operations, campaign operations, or marketing coordination
* Direct experience supporting campaigns through email, digital advertising, webinars, and promotional programs
* Experience developing target lists, managing segmentation, and supporting campaign deployment
* Practical exposure to lead management, including cleansing, validation, uploads, and handoff after events and campaigns
* Approximately 25% travel expectation


Salary Range: $80,000 \- $90,000, eligible for a 9% annual bonus


Please note that this range does not include any other compensation or benefits that an individual may be eligible for. The salary offered depends on a variety of factors, which may include, but not limited to; the qualifications of the individual applicant for the position, location, level of education attained, and/or professional certifications, years of relevant experience, special skills, etc.

*Laerdal Medical Corporation is committed to provide equal employment opportunities to all applicants and employees without regard to race, color, citizenship status, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, martial status, age, genetic information, status as an individual with a disability, status as a protected veteran or any other legally protected status and to affirmatively seek to advance the principles of equal employment opportunity.*

jobs.title: Manager, MarTech Enablement
jobs.description:
The MarTech Enablement Manager is responsible for executing, delivering, and measuring MarTech enablement across North America. Reporting to the MarTech Operations \& Enablement Lead, this role ensures MarTech enablement initiatives are delivered seamlessly, experienced clearly by marketers and agencies, and measured consistently to inform leadership decision\-making‑.

**What You Will Do for Us**

**MarTech Enablement \& Operations**

* Serve as a core execution partner to the MarTech Leadership Team, flexing focus based on evolving priorities across MarTech capabilities, agency needs, and planning to ensure enablement initiatives move from intent to execution.
* Own the delivery of MarTech enablement initiatives, including planning, coordinating, and delivering large\-scale training and enablement moments (e.g., brief training, process and workflow enablement) in partnership with MarTech Leadership Team and subject matter experts.
* Handle the day\-to\-day execution required for flawless delivery, including coordinating logistics, timelines, communications, and materials; scheduling sessions and speakers; booking rooms or virtual set‑ups; managing registrations and attendance; and ensuring enablement moments are delivered smoothly and on time.
* Supports continuous improvement efforts on the ways of working for receiving, managing, and delivering marketing assets across the System
* Lead special projects supporting strategic initiatives within MarTech

**MarTech Hub Orchestration**

* Own and orchestrate Spark as the central MarTech enablement hub, ensuring tools, learning, playbooks, and resources are organized, current, and aligned to MarTech priorities.
* Support MarTech capability programming, assisting with the planning, coordination, and execution of training, enablement, and adoption initiatives across tools and ways of working.
* Plan, manage, and deliver Focus Friday with a consistent cadence, coordinating topics, speakers, and communications to reinforce priority MarTech capabilities, tools, and ways of working.
* Ensure Spark and Focus Friday operate as a connected enablement system, using Focus Friday to drive awareness and traffic to Spark and Spark as the destination for deeper learning, tools, and on ‑demand resources.

**MarTech Adoption \& Enablement Reporting**

* Pull, compile, and maintain reporting across core MarTech enablement channels and systems (Thrive participation for capability programs and trainings; Workfront usage and engagement; Spark site traffic and content engagement; Focus Friday engagement metrics) to create a consistent view of adoption and enablement performance.
* Aggregate and synthesize reporting into defined metrics and insights used as direct inputs into the MarTech scorecard, highlighting adoption trends, usage patterns, engagement gaps, and friction points across initiatives and tools.
* Partner with the MarTech Leadership Team and Change Management Lead to share scorecard inputs, usage insights, and engagement trends that inform enablement priorities, change plans, and leadership recommendations.

**Qualifications \& Requirements**

* Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, Technology, Analytics, or related field.
* 2\-5 years of experience in marketing enablement, MarTech, operations, or capability development within a marketing or agency environment.
* Experience executing cross functional‑ initiatives in a matrixed organization.
* Hands\-‑on experience pulling and working with engagement, adoption, and usage data from learning platforms, workflow tools, or internal hubs.
* Strong understanding of marketing workflows, tools, and agency ecosystems.
* Proven ability to manage multiple priorities with strong attention to detail and follow\-through‑.

**Preferred Skills**

* **Enablement Execution \& Operations:** Strong operational rigor; ability to manage logistics, coordination, communications, and timelines; comfortable owning execution end‑to‑end.
* **MarTech Enablement \& Platforms:** Experience managing enablement hubs or learning platforms; familiarity with tools such as Spark, Thrive, Workfront, or similar systems.
* **Reporting \& Measurement:** Ability to pull, compile, and synthesize engagement and usage data; comfort translating data into clear inputs for scorecards and leadership discussions.
* **Communication \& Coordination:** Clear, concise communicator; effective at coordinating across stakeholders, speakers, and partners.
* **Ways of Working:** Highly organized, proactive, and adaptable; able to flex across priorities with a bias toward action and continuous improvement.
  

  

  

**Skills**  

  

Media Planning; Data Literacy; Stakeholder Management; Brand Strategy; Creative Development; Digital Marketing; Integrated Marketing Campaigns; Campaign Development; Artificial Intelligence (AI); Brand Management; Cross\-Functional Collaboration; Consumer Insights Research
All persons hired will be required to verify identity and eligibility to work in the United States and to complete the required employment eligibility verification form (Form I\-9\) upon hire.

  

Pay Range:$106,800 \- $138,500  

Base pay offered may vary depending on geography, job\-related knowledge, skills, and experience. A full range of medical, financial, and/or other benefits, dependent on the position, is offered.  

  

Annual Incentive Reference Value Percentage:15  

Annual Incentive reference value is a market\-based competitive value for your role. It falls in the middle of the range for your role, indicating performance at target.  

  

Long\-term Incentive Reference Value Percentage:  

Long\-term Incentive reference value is a market\-based competitive value for your role.

jobs.title: Director, Sales and Marketing Analytics
jobs.description:
The Director, Sales \& Marketing Analytics, is key member of the team providing analytical support to the Sales \& Marketing function (Brand Marketing, Field Sales Leadership) of a growing commercial organization with four approved products and a portfolio of pipeline product candidates. This position will be the primary analytical business partner for the Rare Disease – Sleep Medicine Business Unit Marketing Team and will provide analytical support to Sales Leadership as well. In addition, this position will work with other functions in Data, Analytics \& Insights and with Commercial Operations, IT, Finance, Program Management and Compliance on cross functional solutions. This position will work a weekly hybrid schedule in our Waltham office.


This position will report to the Sr Director, Sales \& Marketing Analytics.


  
**Why join Team Alkermes?**

Alkermes applies its decades of deep neuroscience expertise to develop medicines designed to help people living with complex and difficult\-to\-treat psychiatric and neurological disorders. A global biopharmaceutical company, headquartered in Ireland with U.S. locations in Massachusetts and Ohio, we seek to make a meaningful difference in the way people manage their diseases. We have a portfolio of proprietary commercial products for the treatment of alcohol dependence, opioid dependence, schizophrenia, bipolar I disorder and narcolepsy, and a pipeline of clinical and preclinical candidates in development for various psychiatric and neurological disorders.


We are proud to have been recognized as an employer of choice by many national organizations. In 2024 and 2025, we were certified as a Great Place to Work in the U.S. and named one of Massachusetts’ Top Places to Work by the Boston Globe, a Best Place to Work in Greater Cincinnati by the Cincinnati Business Courier and recognized as a Best Place to Work in BioPharma by Fortune Magazine.


Alkermes, Inc. is an equal employment opportunity employer and does not discriminate against any qualified applicant or employee because of race, creed, color, age, national origin, ancestry, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender expression and identity, disability, genetic information, veteran status, military status, application for military service or any other characteristic protected by local, state or federal law. Alkermes also complies with all work authorization and employment eligibility verification requirements of the Immigration and Nationality Act and IRCA. Alkermes is an E\-Verify employer.


  
Degree LevelBachelor's Degree

jobs.title: VP/D Direct Mail Marketing Analytics
jobs.description:
We are seeking a Director of Direct Mail Marketing Strategy and Analytics to join our marketing leadership team. OneMain Financial is the nation’s leading direct mailer for sub\-prime and near\-prime personal loans. As such, our direct mail (“DM”) marketing program is highly analytical and requires a large amount of strategic oversight. The candidate will lead a team of analysts and will be responsible for leveraging advanced analytics to resiliently grow our Direct Mail channel. This includes DM strategy development, analysis, mail selection optimization, performance reporting, and development of robust learning agenda to drive continued innovation.

**In the Role**


The successful candidate will combine the skills of a strategic thinker with the ability to run a complex execution engine. On the strategy front, this person will continuously look out for opportunities to optimize and grow the channel and present insights and recommendations up to C\-level executives. On the execution side, they will be able to manage multiple workstreams with a high attention to detail. Ability to partner well with peers and senior leaders outside of analytics within our marketing team is a must as well.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Lead and develop an analytics team whose primary function is to manage a monthly DM investment decision process that maximizes both response and NPV.
* Build a deep understanding of our end\-to\-end mail execution process that involves multiple teams (Campaign Execution, Credit, Marketing, etc.) and how we capture DM response data within our prospect database that feeds future insights, and execute this process on a monthly basis with minimum errors.
* Build a strategic understanding of the key levers that can be used to improve and/or expand the program, and build/execute a learning agenda to help accomplish these strategic objectives. Examples include develop tests around mail cadence, segmentation of prospects, incorporation of new data sources, and ongoing A/B creative testing process.
* Re\-evaluate and improve our holdout strategies that allow for measurement of the mail channel’s incremental contribution to the overall business and marginal incrementality
* Analyze cross\-channel interactions i.e., how DM influences other marketing channels and other channels’ impact to DM.
* Build deep expertise in our DM Targeting models, partner with Data Science team to continuously improve our targeting efficiency.
* Understand the reporting needs of the business and enhance / streamline reports where necessary.
* Monitor and report on the broader macro\-environment for personal lending, specifically other lenders behavior in Direct Mail through competitive intelligence. Use those insights to inform our own DM decision making.

**Requirements**

* 10\+ years of experience driving informative decisions through analyses; Master’s or PHD degrees may offset experience
* 5\+ years of analytics leadership experience, building and prioritizing teams and objectives based on company strategy
* Deep knowledge of analytical tools and techniques to drive business outcomes. Advanced analytical skillset using tools such as SQL, SAS, R, or Python. Experience leveraging data visualization and digital marketing tools such as Power BI
* Innovative and capable of developing a highly analytical approach to solving problems; strong data and insights intuition, intellectual curiosity
* Strong cross\-functional communication· Bachelors degree in a quantitative discipline such as Engineering, Statistics, Economics, Business Management, or Computer Science (advanced degree desired) and influencing skills, including experience presenting to and getting buy\-in on strategy from senior executives
* Experience in marketing and in particular direct mail marketing is preferred
* Lending or consumer finance industry experience preferred
* Preferred but not required: Thorough understanding of how DM marketing campaigns are designed and executed, from identifying prospects to be mailed in our prospect database, culminating in sending out marketing solicitations through our print vendors. Experience using bureau data and non\-bureau data within a prospect database solution (i.e., Acxiom)

**Location: \[HYBRID] Wilmington, DE // Anchor Days: Tues. \- Thurs.**

**Who we Are**


OneMain Financial (NYSE: OMF) is the leader in offering nonprime customers responsible access to credit and is dedicated to improving the financial well\-being of hardworking Americans. Since 1912, we’ve looked beyond credit scores to help people get the money they need today and reach their goals for tomorrow. Our growing suite of personal loans, credit cards and other products help people borrow better and work toward a brighter future.


Driven collaborators and innovators, our team thrives on transformative digital thinking, customer\-first energy and flexible work arrangements that grow lives, careers and our company. At every level, we’re committed to an inclusive culture, career development and impacting the communities where we live and work. Getting people to a better place has made us a better company for over a century. There’s never been a better time to shine with OneMain.


Because team members at their best means OneMain at our best, we provide opportunities and benefits that make their health and careers a priority. That’s why we’ve packed our comprehensive benefits package for full\- and some part\-timers with:

* Health and wellbeing options including medical, prescription, dental, vision, hearing, accident, hospital indemnity, and life insurances
* Up to 4% matching 401(k)
* Employee Stock Purchase Plan (10% share discount)
* Tuition reimbursement
* Paid time off (15 days’ vacation per year, prorated based on start date)
* Paid sick leave as determined by state or local ordinance (prorated based on start date)
* 11 Paid holidays (4 floating holidays, prorated based on start date)
* Paid volunteer time (3 days per year, prorated based on start date)
