jobs.title: Manager, Integrated Marketing Operations
jobs.description:
Overview:

The Manager, Integrated Marketing Operations is a senior operational leader within the Integrated Marketing and Creative Marketing organization. This role owns how marketing and creative work gets done — translating brand and business priorities into clear plans, disciplined workflows, and high\-quality execution across all marketing initiatives.  

This leader is accountable for the end\-to\-end marketing operations ecosystem, ensuring that everything requiring creative resources — from day\-to\-day PDP production to major brand campaigns, shoots, video, and printed materials — is prioritized, resourced, and delivered efficiently. The Manager operates as the central owner of intake, prioritization, and delivery, enabling creative teams to focus on producing their best work while ensuring the business moves at speed.


Responsibilities:

Marketing Operations Ownership  

* Own the end\-to\-end intake, prioritization, and workflow management for all marketing and creative initiatives.
* Serve as the operational lead for all work requiring creative resources, including PDPs, seasonal brand campaigns, video, photography, print, and special projects.
* Translate brand, marketing, and business objectives into clear scopes, briefs, timelines, and delivery plans.
* Balance competing priorities, capacity, and deadlines, making informed tradeoffs to protect quality and delivery.


Integrated Campaign \& Production Leadership  

* Oversee planning and execution of 360 marketing initiatives from concept through delivery.
* Partner with Brand Strategy, Integrated Marketing, Merchandising, Digital, and Channel teams to align priorities and timelines.
* Provide operational leadership for campaign shoots, asset development, and production calendars.
* Identify risks, dependencies, and resourcing gaps early and drive solutions.


Process, Tools \& Ways of Working  

* Design, implement, and continuously improve marketing operations processes that increase efficiency, clarity, and speed\-to\-market.
* Own project management tools, documentation, and workflows supporting creative and marketing execution.
* Establish standards for briefs, approvals, timelines, feedback, and handoffs.
* Drive consistency and discipline across teams while remaining flexible to business needs.


Cross\-Functional Leadership  

* Act as the primary operational bridge between creative, marketing, product, merchandising, and channel teams.
* Set clear expectations and decision frameworks for stakeholders engaging creative resources.
* Facilitate alignment and accountability across cross\-functional partners and external agencies.


Reporting \& Performance Management  

* Own visibility into workload, capacity, timelines, and delivery performance across the creative organization.
* Provide regular updates to marketing and creative leadership on priorities, risks, and progress.
* Lead post\-campaign and post\-project retrospectives to drive continuous improvement.


Team Leadership  

* Lead, mentor, and develop project management and operations talent within the marketing organization.
* Foster a culture of ownership, clarity, collaboration, and high standards.

Qualifications:
* 6–9\+ years of experience in marketing operations, creative operations, integrated marketing, or project management within a marketing or creative organization.
* Proven experience owning complex, multi\-channel marketing workflows end\-to\-end.
* Strong understanding of creative production across digital, video, photography, print, and PDP content.
* Demonstrated ability to prioritize, make decisions, and manage competing demands.
* Exceptional organizational, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
* Experience with project management, DAM, and workflow tools (e.g., monday.com).

  

Success Profile  

The ideal candidate is a decisive, systems\-minded operator who brings structure to complexity and momentum to teams. They are trusted to run marketing operations with minimal oversight, anticipate issues before they arise, and ensure that brand and creative excellence are delivered consistently and on time.


Pay Range: USD $75,900\.00 \- USD $138,000\.00 /Yr.

jobs.title: VP of Sales — Profitable Video MarTech SaaS
jobs.description:
This job is for you if…

1 – You want to win more than anything… by being part of an entrepreneurial team where winning, ownership, and execution matter, and leadership means rolling up your sleeves and setting the pace.

2 – You don’t just want to close deals — you want to shape how the entire organization acquires revenue from pipeline to scaling wild experiments, and being accountable for revenue results..

3 – You can – and have already – build and scale a high performance sales team. Refining how sales actually happen day to day, and leading from the front as the team grows and evolves.

We’re Vidalytics – a video hosting and marketing platform – and our product is great. When we’re demoing to our ICPs, they typically say “holy sh\*\*, this is awesome.”

The reason is simple: we sell money at a discount, because we’re the most advanced, best converting video player for marketers that costs less than the competitors. Period. We have dozens of split tests proving this with 7, 8 and 9 figure brands.

***What You’ll Be Doing (Job Tasks)*** 

* Own the sales function end\-to\-end, including pipeline creation, revenue targets, forecasting, pipeline health, and execution.
* Personally contribute to pipeline creation and deal execution, especially early on or in high\-impact scenarios, while coaching Account Executives through discovery, demos, objections, and negotiations.
* Lead, coach, and performance\-manage a team of Account Executives, including hiring, onboarding, ramping, and ongoing development.
* Own and continuously improve how we generate demand through outbound, inside sales, partnerships, and events.
* Approach events as a system — including pre\-event planning, attendee strategy, meetings booked, success criteria, post\-event analysis, pipeline attribution, and time\-to\-ROI measurement.
* Define, enforce, and iterate on sales strategy, deal structure, pricing discipline, qualification standards, and messaging.
* Design, test, and refine prospecting campaigns and sequences across email, LinkedIn, calling, and other outbound channels.
* Write and review high\-quality sales emails, outreach copy, proposals, contracts, and presentations.
* Define, track, and report on key sales KPIs and metrics, ensuring clear visibility into pipeline, conversion, velocity, and forecast accuracy.
* Roll up accurate monthly and quarterly forecasts and deliver on revenue commitments to the business.
* Own and optimize CRM usage, pipeline stages, reporting, and sales execution tools (HubSpot and related systems).
* Partner cross\-functionally with Marketing, Product, Engineering, and Customer Success to align revenue strategy and execution, while maintaining clear ownership boundaries.
* Support smooth handoffs to Customer Success and Account Management, without owning post\-sale retention directly.
* Represent Vidalytics at industry events, conferences, and trade shows — developing new business, strengthening relationships, and increasing brand presence.
* Continuously improve sales processes, documentation, and internal playbooks as patterns emerge and the team scales.
* Grow and refine your skills as a sales leader while building a high\-performance, accountable sales organization.

***Must Have*** 

* At least 2\+ years of experience in Sales Leadership, or Business Development roles, ideally within B2B SaaS and Enterprise roles. . With a track record of winning!
* This means Experience leading and developing sales teams to perform at a high level from A to Z: , including hiring, coaching, performance management, firing and everything else to drive results.
* Proven experience generating pipeline without relying solely on marketing or other teams and closing deals, with the ability and willingness to personally execute when needed.
* Strong understanding of outbound and inside sales motions, including prospecting, objection handling, and deal progression.
* Experience with a variety of GTM Strategies, especially events as that is our main effort now, but also online paid ad lead gen, affiliates, etc.
* Experience using events as a revenue channel, with an understanding that ROI is measurable over time.
* Comfort with sales metrics, pipeline math, forecasting, analysis and revenue discipline.
* Strong communication skills, written and verbal, with prospects, customers, and internal stakeholders.
* An infectious personality that inspires ppl to want to follow you to the gates of hell.
* Autonomy and ownership — you move fast, meet deadlines, and take responsibility for outcomes without waiting to be told what to do. We need someone who’s been there / done that. At this comp and opportunity this is NOT on the job training.
* Proficiency in CRM and sales execution tools such as HubSpot, Salesloft, Outreach, respond.io or similar platforms. But ofc we’ll get you the rev ops support you need.
* Willingness to travel approximately 25–50% for key events and meetings. As you scale the team and perform as a unit… this can possibly scale down. 90% of travel is domestic USA, with some intl.

***Nice to Have*** 

* Prior experience in the **Direct Response Marketing** niche.
* Basic understanding of technical concepts (we’re a SaaS after all!).
* Background in **non\-traditional sales methods**, outbound, or relationship\-driven sales environments that get results.
* Prior experience working in a **high\-growth, remote\-first** company.

***What You’ll Love About Us (Benefits and Perks)*** 

* **Fully Remote**
* **Health care benefits.**
* **Guaranteed Ramp\-Up Period.**
* **Professional Development:** Want to continue your education? Vidalytics can cover $3k annually for classes, conferences, and more related to becoming the best professional version of yourself.
* **Stay Fresh.** 20 Days PTO for you to use whenever you want (no pre\-selected National Holidays). 5 sick days.
* **High\-performance company culture.** You won’t be a cog in a machine. Rather you’ll be a key member of a high\-performance team that is getting things done. This is a place to grow and realize your potential, no matter what level you’re at in your career.
* **No office politics.** We’re small and flat. There is no bs of trying to dance around sensitive topics or divert resources from other division’s projects. We’re direct, we’re honest, we’re here to accomplish big audacious goals.
* **We value your ideas**. At Vidalytics, our door is always open. Need help? Let’s talk! Have a vision for the future of the company? We want to hear it! Think we suck at our job? Help us grow!
* **This is a resume\-making position**, where you’ll learn a lot and have a lot of responsibility. You don’t have to wait for someone to die or retire to move up, like in a mega\-corp.
* **Entrepreneurial to our core** – Not only are we a startup, but we need intrapreneurs who will help us scale.

**Vidalytics’ Values** 

**(Yes, We’re Actually Serious About These)** 

* **Play to Win** – We’re competitors. We do what it takes to win. This means that we will fail, we will lose, but we want to do these things fast… so we can win. Because winning is on the other side of mistakes.
* **Scrappy** – We’re a startup, it’s chaotic. This isn’t the government. You need to be able to learn fast, move fast and handle it. You need to be able to survive in a scrappy entrepreneur environment. If you want an easy job, go work at the government.
* **Meritocracy** – The best idea wins, not who it comes from… This means you can be direct \& transparent with anyone. Test your ideas starting with analysis, form hypotheses, test small, measure results, iterate, and scale big with data. We can’t have perfect data. So when a decision is made, get on board.
* **Own Yourself** – We do what we say, and own what we’ve done. Mistakes happen. But the worst thing that can happen is when someone doesn’t own and learn from them. You trust us. We trust you.
* **Self Actualization** – This is a place for hyper learning. We want to scale. You need to scale with us, this doesn't always mean climbing some ladder, but upleveling skill – closing gaps, growing and being self actualized.
* **Conscious Teamwork** – No politics. No bs. Rather integrity, emotional intelligence, transparency and curiosity. Attack ideas, not people.

Cheers,

Patrick Stiles

Founder / CEO @ Vidalytics

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: $150,000\.00 \- $250,000\.00 per year

Location:

* United States (Required)

Work Location: Remote

Previous experience working with Microsoft will be an added advantage

Legal \& Financial Assistance: Legal Assistance, 401K Plan, Performance Bonus, Colleg e Fund, Student Loan Refinancing.

jobs.title: Director - MarTech & AdTech Platforms
jobs.description:
Costco IT is responsible for the **technical future of Costco Wholesale**, the third largest retailer in the world with wholesale operations in fourteen countries. Despite our size and explosive international expansion, we continue to provide a family, employee centric atmosphere in which our employees thrive and succeed.


This is an environment **unlike anything in the high\-tech world** and the secret of Costco’s success is its culture. The value Costco puts on its employees is well documented in articles from a variety of publishers including Bloomberg and Forbes. Our employees and our members come FIRST. Costco is well known for its generosity and community service and has won many awards for its philanthropy. The company joins with its employees to take an active role in volunteering by sponsoring many opportunities to help others.


Come join the **Costco Wholesale IT family**. Costco IT is a dynamic, fast\-paced environment, working through exciting transformation efforts. We are building the next generation retail environment where you will be surrounded by dedicated and highly professional employees.


 At Costco Wholesale, we’re not just looking for a manager; we’re looking for a leader to help us bridge the gap between our world\-class warehouse experience and our digital future. As the**Director of MarTech \& AdTech Platforms**, you’ll be the strategic lead for the technology that powers our member engagement. From overseeing our Adobe Experience Platform (AEP) suite to scaling our Retail Media Network (RMN), your work will directly influence how we use data to drive growth and deliver a more personalized experience for millions of members worldwide.


This role is a unique blend of high\-level strategy and deep engineering execution. You will lead a global, multi\-disciplinary team—including software engineers, platform engineers, and product owners—to build and maintain the platforms that handle everything from audience management to omni\-channel measurement. We compete for talent with the biggest names in tech because the scale of our data and the complexity of our "omni\-channel" challenge are just as significant. You’ll be responsible for a prioritized roadmap that doesn’t just keep the lights on, but actively unlocks new value for the business.


Reporting to the **AVP of Data \& Marketing Platforms**, you’ll act as a vital link between technical teams and executive leadership. We value a "player\-coach" mentality—someone who can dive into technical architecture one hour and discuss long\-term business strategy the next. Success here means navigating a fast\-moving, collaborative environment where your ability to foster a culture of trust and open communication is just as important as your technical acumen.


Ultimately, your goal is to ensure our technology is as reliable and member\-focused as the warehouses themselves. As a steward of the Costco culture, you will develop a strategic vision that aligns with our core mission: providing high\-quality products and services at the best possible value. If you’re ready to lead an essential part of a global IT transformation while staying grounded in a culture that puts people first, we’d love to talk.


As the primary conduit between your employees and upper leadership, your role in communicating and modeling the values and guiding principles of our company culture is of vital importance. All members of IT Management should strive to consciously and consistently foster a culture of engagement, trust, and “open door” communication.
If you want to be a part of one of the worldwide **BEST companies “to work for**”, simply apply and let your career be reimagined.

**ROLE**

* **INTEGRITY:** **When achieving benchmarks and goals, use methods/strategies that are consistent with the Code of Ethics and the Standard of Ethics for Managers and Supervisors. Always leads by example. Appropriately handles employee concerns and follows through to resolution.**
* **ADMINISTRATION:** **Ensures proper department coverage (writing schedule and break aids if needed). Understands department budget, able to research and explain budget variances.**
* **MANAGING PERFORMANCE:** **Coaches and mentors’ employees to provide support and guidance. Has regular open and honest conversations with employees to discuss work performance and career development. Identifies learning opportunities to strengthen employee knowledge, skill, and ability.**
* **COMMUNICATION:** **Regularly shares information with employees via meetings and one\-on\-one conversations. Successfully navigates difficult conversations with employees, members, and suppliers. Listens, expresses empathy, and adapts to get points across. Addresses issues immediately to ensure a timely resolution and to avoid escalating the situation. Consistently demonstrates business knowledge during interactions with senior management.**
* **SELF\-MANAGEMENT:** **Demonstrates sound judgment, taking a partner when necessary.**
* **INCLUSION****: Encourages different approaches and ideas to work and to accomplish goals. Seeks employee input. Takes the time to get to know or reach out to candidates who show potential that may not come forward on their own.**
* **COMPLIANCE AND SAFETY:** **Takes measures to ensure employee and member information is kept confidential and adheres to IS security policy.**
* **Leads the development, integration, and optimization of advertising and marketing technologies, delivering seamless and personalized member experiences across ads, web, email, push, and SMS channels.**
* **Defines the marketing technology roadmap, analyzing new Adobe products and use cases to support business goals.**
* **Collaborates with multi\-disciplinary teams including marketing, technology, analytics, and business partners to ensure successful platform implementation and campaign delivery.**
* **This is a full\-time management/leadership position (45\+ hours per week).**

**REQUIRED**

* 5\-7 years’ experience in marketing \& advertising technology to support critical business needs.
* Experience in Retail Technology \- Online Advertising, eCommerce, and Membership \& Marketing.
* Experience building and leading high performing teams including mentorship and career development of team members.
* Experience with cloud data infrastructure and building things in a repeatable, scalable way.
* Extensive knowledge of Marketing technology, specifically Adobe Experience Platform CDP or similar (Salesforce, Braze).
* Experience with digital advertising, DSP, DMP, personalization, and data driven outcomes.
* Software Development or Leading Software Development Teams building core digital experiences or integrations.
* Experience in defining and implementing data strategy for the companies and teams fulfill the transition to being data driven.
* Strong communication skills with the ability to level the conversation to business audiences with different technical backgrounds and build strong relationships across departments.
* Proven track record of active coaching and mentorship of leading teams to further their career and improve their skills.
* Strong familiarity with the Agile development process and proven ability to prepare a project plan, communicate the plan to the team, and break the work down into trackable tasks.
* Proactively partner and identify opportunities, set priorities, and establish goals.
* HIPAA Training and Supervisors Orientation (within 30 days of hire); Leadership Development 101 (within one year); Costco Pay Policies (within 90 days of promotion).

**Recommended**
---------------

* Degree in Computer Science.
* Familiarity with AI, ML, GenAI applications.
* Proficient in Google Workspace applications, including Sheets, Docs, Slides, and Gmail.


**Required Documents**

* Cover Letter
* Resume


California applicants, please review the Costco Applicant Privacy Notice.

 **Pay Range: $160,000 \- $230,000, Bonus and Restricted Stock Unit (RSU) eligible**

**We offer a comprehensive package of benefits including paid time off, health benefits \- medical/dental/vision/hearing aid/pharmacy/behavioral health/employee assistance, health care reimbursement account, dependent care assistance plan, short\-term disability and long\-term disability insurance, AD\&D insurance, life insurance, 401(k), stock purchase plan to eligible employees.**

**Costco is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace. Costco is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard of race, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, protected veteran status, disability, age, or any other legally protected status. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application or the recruiting process, please send a request to IT\-Recruiting@costco.com****If hired, you will be required to provide proof of authorization to work in the United States. Applicants and employees for this position will not be sponsored for work authorization, including, but not limited to H1\-B visas****.**

jobs.title: Global Product Lead, Marketing Technology
jobs.description:
Applicants in San Francisco: Qualified applications with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers and the California Fair Chance Act.  

Note: By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following: **San Francisco, CA, USA; New York, NY, USA**.### **Minimum qualifications:**

* Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience.
* 6 years of experience in product or program management.

  

### **Preferred qualifications:**

* Experience implementing technical solutions and managing the product development lifecycle.
* Experience with data\-driven analysis, reporting, and success measurement.
* Understanding of the architecture of a technical solution, including platforms, data, integrations and constraints.
* Ability to manage conflicting stakeholders and drive conflict resolution.
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to engage with technical and non\-technical audiences.

**About the job**
-----------------


gTech’s Product and Tools Operations team (gPTO) leverages deep user, operational, and technical insights to innovate Google's Ads products into customer experiences that are so intuitive (or automated) that they require no support at all. gPTO partners closely with gTech’s Support, Professional Services, Product Management, and Engineering teams to innovate and simplify our Ads products and build the productivity tools ecosystem for gTech users.



In this role, you will be responsible for understanding how core marketing programs are used globally and identify the capabilities needed to meet customer needs. You will leverage your knowledge of systems design and business processes to advocate the agentification of web workflows, translating operational bottlenecks into automated, technology\-driven solutions. You will act as a subject matter specialist, resolving technical challenges and ensuring integration functions reliably across our marketing ecosystem.


Collaborating closely with ads marketing, product management, and engineering, you will bridge the gap between technical execution and business objectives. You will lead the commercialization of new web solutions and experiences, influence cross\-functional teams to align resources, and manage the operational cadence to ensure the successful delivery of high\-impact marketing capabilities.


Know the user. Know the magic. Connect the two. At its core, marketing at Google starts with technology and ends with the user, bringing both together in unconventional ways. Our job is to demonstrate how Google's products solve the world's problems\-from the everyday to the epic, from the mundane to the monumental. And we approach marketing in a way that only Google can\-changing the game, redefining the medium, making the user the priority, and ultimately, letting the technology speak for itself.


The US base salary range for this full\-time position is $146,000\-$213,000 \+ bonus \+ equity \+ benefits. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job\-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your preferred location during the hiring process.


Please note that the compensation details listed in US role postings reflect the base salary only, and do not include bonus, equity, or benefits. Learn more about benefits at Google.

**Responsibilities**
--------------------

* Drive the product roadmap and strategy for key marketing technology web solutions, gathering business requirements and ensuring alignment with global marketing goals.
* Lead the go\-to\-market strategy and full product lifecycle from pilot to global deployment, communicating changes and driving adoption among product marketing managers.
* Partner with product management and engineering to commercialize new solutions, resolve technical challenges, and manage the operational cadence of the work\-stream.
* Utilize data analysis and user insights to monitor product performance, identify opportunities for improvement, and optimize feature offerings to drive operational efficiency.
* Advocate the agentification of web workflows by applying knowledge of systems design to automate manual processes and translate operational bottlenecks into scalable solutions.
Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form.

jobs.title: Director - Marketing Analytics and Insights
jobs.description:
**Location:** Home\-based, United States **Position Summary:**  

As the Director of Marketing Analytics and Insights at Bentley, you will be the driving force behind our global marketing data strategy. You won't just analyze data; you'll transform it into a strategic asset that fuels our growth. Reporting to the Sr. Director of Marketing Operations, you will lead a talented team to uncover actionable insights, optimize our marketing performance, and partner with executive leadership to shape the future of our customer engagement and operational efficiency. **Responsibilities:**  

* Shape Our Data\-Driven Future: Define and execute a forward\-thinking analytics vision for our marketing organization, ensuring your strategy directly aligns with our core business objectives.
* Measure What Matters: Develop and implement a comprehensive measurement framework to demonstrate the ROI of all marketing initiatives, from campaign effectiveness to customer lifetime value. You will oversee the creation and maintenance of marketing dashboards that provide real\-time, actionable insights.
* Be the Voice of the Data: Translate complex data into compelling stories and actionable recommendations for our executive team and other stakeholders. Your insights will be crucial for strategic decision\-making.
* Champion Data Literacy: Collaborate with leaders in Product, Sales, and Finance to foster a data\-first culture. You will work to establish shared data definitions and empower teams across Bentley with self\-service analytics capabilities.
* Lead and Inspire: Mentor and lead a team of 2\-3 marketing analytics professionals, fostering their growth and guiding them to deliver exceptional results.
* Drive Innovation with Advanced Analytics: Harness the power of machine learning, predictive modeling, and customer segmentation to uncover new opportunities and optimize our marketing strategies.
* Advanced analytics**:** Leverage machine learning, predictive modeling, and customer segmentation to optimize marketing strategies.
* Requires sitting or standing at will while performing work on a computer (or any other physical requirements).

**Qualifications:**  

* 10\+ years of experience in marketing analytics, business intelligence, or a related field.
* Proven track record of partnering with marketing leadership to optimize spend and demonstrate ROI.
* Hands\-on experience with enterprise\-level marketing automation platforms (e.g., Marketo, HubSpot), analytics tools (e.g., Google Analytics), and CRMs (e.g., Salesforce).
* Demonstrated experience in leading and developing a team of analysts.
* Expertise in data analysis and visualization, with proficiency in tools such as SQL, Python, R, or advanced Excel.
* Strong knowledge of statistical analysis, modeling techniques, and A/B testing.
* Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to convey complex data stories to both technical and non\-technical audiences.
* Exceptional problem\-solving skills and a critical\-thinking mindset.
* Experience leading the adoption of AI\-driven analytics tools preferred.
* Experience in a B2B or software industry context preferred.

 **What We Offer:**  

* A great Team and culture – please see our colleague video.
* An exciting career as an integral part of a world\-leading software company providing solutions for architecture, engineering, and construction \- watch this short documentary about how we got our start.
* An attractive salary and benefits package.
* A commitment to inclusion, belonging and colleague wellbeing through global initiatives and resource groups.
* A company committed to making a real difference by advancing the world’s infrastructure for better quality of life, where your contributions help build a more sustainable, connected, and resilient world. Discover our latest user success stories for an insight into our global impact.


**About Bentley Systems**  

Around the world, infrastructure professionals rely on software from Bentley Systems to help them design, build, and operate better and more resilient infrastructure for transportation, water, energy, cities, and more. Founded in 1984 by engineers for engineers, Bentley is the partner of choice for engineering firms and owner\-operators worldwide, with software that spans engineering disciplines, industry sectors, and all phases of the infrastructure lifecycle. Through our digital twin solutions, we help infrastructure professionals unlock the value of their data to transform project delivery and asset performance. www.bentley.com

**Equal Opportunity Employer:**


Bentley is an equal opportunity employer and considers all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability, pregnancy, protected veteran status, religion, national origin, age, genetic information or any other protected characteristic. This commitment extends to all aspects of employment, including, but not limited to, hiring, placement, promotion, compensation, and training. Know Your Rights as an applicant under the law.

  

Bentley Policy on EEO, Affirmative Action and Pay Transparency Non\-Discrimination


Bentley participates in e\-Verify / Bentley participate in e\-Verify / Right to Work Notice

 **Request an Accommodation:**  

As an Equal Opportunity Employer, Bentley is committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities. We encourage you to request a reasonable accommodation if you are not able to fully use or access our online application system. You can make an accommodation request by calling 610\-458\-5000 or sending us an email at disabilityrequest@bentley.com

jobs.title: Senior Analyst, Marketing Technology
jobs.description:
**Position Purpose:**
Serves as a member of the Marketing Technology Operations team and plays an integral role in enabling marketing projects, processes, and driving operational efficiencies through technology implementations and on\-going support. This is accomplished by participating in research/information gathering to understand business requirements, system configuration, and on\-going support through training, new associate on\-boarding, and governance for technological implementations.

 **Key Responsibilities:*** 10% Knowledge of Marketing Operations \- Remains knowledgeable on Marketing Technology systems and capabilities, and trends in Marketing Operations
* 20% On\-Going Support for Systems \- Provide on\-going support for systems that enable marketing project execution by developing and delivering training, new associate on\-boarding, and governance of implementations to ensure configurations and data stay "clean"
* 20% Optimizes Processes \- Use data and insights to optimize processes and support through areas such as automation, incremental enhancements to system configurations, process improvement and modifications to training programs to drive operational efficiencies
* 10% Partner with Third\-Parties \- Partner with third party software providers to understand and influence their capabilities roadmap
* 20% Partnership \- Partner with Marketing Technology product management, Brand Marketing account services, creative, program management office (PMO), and other cross functional partners to understand processes and system requirements to enable marketing project execution and technology services
* 20% Requirement Gathering \- Based on PMO center\-of\-excellence processes and ways of working and requirements gathered, configure technology, on\-board users, and thoughtfully roll\-out solutions that enable marketing project execution and processes

  

**Direct Manager/Direct Reports:*** This position typically reports to Manager/Sr Manager Marketing Operations
* This position has 0 Direct Reports

  

**Travel Requirements:*** Typically requires overnight travel less than 10% of the time.

  

**Physical Requirements:*** Most of the time is spent sitting in a comfortable position and there is frequent opportunity to move about. On rare occasions there may be a need to move or lift light articles.

  

**Working Conditions:*** Located in a comfortable indoor area. Any unpleasant conditions would be infrequent and not objectionable.

  

**Minimum Qualifications:*** Must be eighteen years of age or older.
* Must be legally permitted to work in the United States.

  

**Preferred Qualifications:*** Previous experience in traditional and/or digital marketing, marketing operations, or marketing technology operations
* Self\-motivated and able to work independently and collaboratively within/across team(s); able to lead and deliver projects with little direction
* Strong problem\-solving skills with the ability to manage around ambiguity
* Ability to communicate complex topics to manager and senior leadership
* Experience with Marketing Technology systems and ways of working including but not limited to digital asset management, dynamically assembled creative/content, campaign and offer management, marketing workflow management, and agile marketing
* Excellent verbal and written communication skills and an ability to prepare clean and concise leadership\-ready documents

  

**Minimum Education:*** The knowledge, skills and abilities typically acquired through the completion of a bachelor's degree program or equivalent degree in a field of study related to the job.

  

**Preferred Education:*** No additional education

  

**Minimum Years of Work Experience:*** 4

  

**Preferred Years of Work Experience:*** No additional years of experience

  

**Minimum Leadership Experience:*** None

  

**Preferred Leadership Experience:*** None

  

**Certifications:*** None

  

**Competencies:*** Action Oriented
* Collaborates
* Drives Engagement
* Communicates Effectively
* Customer Focus
* Drives Results
* Manages Conflict

jobs.title: Marketing Operations Manager
jobs.description:
We are seeking a Marketing Operations Manager to help build and scale a growing marketing function. This is a foundational hire on a lean team and will play a key role in improving marketing infrastructure, pipeline generation, and reporting.  
This person will own core marketing operations, improve CRM hygiene and process flow,  
support paid lead generation efforts, and build a more sophisticated lead generation and  
pipeline reporting engine.  
This is not a broad brand marketing role. It is a hands on performance marketing and  
pipeline management position.  
**Key Responsibilities**  
● Own and improve day to day marketing operations  
● Clean up, structure, and optimize the CRM environment  
● Manage and improve HubSpot workflows, automation, and integrations  
● Help implement and manage the broader marketing tech stack  
● Partner with outside agencies supporting lead generation and related initiatives  
● Build, maintain, and improve lead generation reporting and dashboards  
● Evolve pipeline tracking from a basic spreadsheet driven process into a more scalable  
system  
● Support and manage paid lead generation efforts with a focus on measurable  
performance  
● Manage paid LinkedIn campaign execution and optimization  
● Work closely with marketing leadership to generate pipeline and improve overall funnel  
visibility

**Required Experience**  
● Strong hands on experience with HubSpot  
○ CRM management  
○ Campaign automation  
○ Workflow and process setup  
○ Systems integration  
○ Website and platform connectivity  
● Strong experience with paid LinkedIn campaigns  
● Experience in performance marketing, demand generation, or paid lead generation  
● Experience building and managing dashboards, reporting, and pipeline visibility tools  
● Ability to operate as a strong individual contributor in a lean environment  
● Comfort working cross functionally with leadership, sales, and agency partners

**Preferred Background**  
● Experience in B2B technology environments  
● Experience supporting longer sales cycle lead generation motions  
● Financial services experience is helpful, but not required  
● Experience in smaller or leaner go to market teams is especially relevant  
● Candidates from startup or larger company environments can be a fit if they have the  
right hands on execution experience

**Ideal Candidate Profile**  
● Proactive and self directed  
● Collaborative and low ego  
● Comfortable working in a small team environment  
● Able to execute without heavy day to day oversight  
● Strong operator with a process oriented mindset  
● Focused on outcomes, reporting, and continuous improvement

**Team and Environment**  
● Fully remote company  
● Preference for candidates in the Eastern Time Zone  
● Small go to market team  
● High visibility role with direct partnership with marketing leadership

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: $120,000\.00 \- $150,000\.00 per year

Work Location: Remote

jobs.title: Marketing Analytics, Associate - Corporate Affairs
jobs.description:
Blackstone is the world’s largest alternative asset manager. We seek to create positive economic impact and long\-term value for our investors, the companies we invest in, and the communities in which we work. We do this by using extraordinary people and flexible capital to help companies solve problems. Our $1\.1 trillion in assets under management include investment vehicles focused on private equity, real estate, public debt and equity, infrastructure, life sciences, growth equity, opportunistic, non\-investment grade credit, real assets and secondary funds, all on a global basis. Further information is available at www.blackstone.com. Follow @blackstone on LinkedIn, X, and Instagram.
The Marketing Analytics Associate or AVP will support Blackstone’s Corporate Affairs organization, with a primary focus on the Private Wealth business, by delivering data\-driven insights that inform marketing strategy, measure effectiveness, and support broader business decisions. This role partners closely with marketing channel teams, technology, and business stakeholders to build scalable analytics, reporting, and measurement frameworks. The ideal candidate will demonstrate strong analytical rigor and business acumen and effectively translates complex data into clear, actionable insights for senior\-level leadership.
Responsibilities* Generate actionable insights through analysis of marketing initiatives and audience behavior
* Develop learning agendas and measurement frameworks for core marketing programs, including defined KPIs
* Identify client segments and targeting opportunities to inform marketing strategy
* Design, build, and maintain performance dashboards and reporting
* Ensure data accuracy and integrity through ongoing governance and audits
* Drive best practices in data usage and inputs through documentation, training, and cross\-functional collaboration
* Communicate analytical insights through clear, compelling presentations and narratives for leadership and key stakeholders

  

Qualifications* 3\+ years of experience in marketing analytics, business analytics, or data analytics roles, preferably within financial services, asset management, or similar data\-intensive environment
* Strong analytical, quantitative, and problem\-solving skills; effectively navigates complex business questions
* Proven skills translating data into actionable insights that are aligned with marketing and business objectives
* Strong experience performing complex, multi\-variable analyses on large, structured customer, sales, and marketing datasets
* Proficiency in Python, R, SQL, or similar languages for data manipulation, advanced analysis, and automation
* Experience designing, building, and maintaining Tableau dashboards or comparable business intelligence dashboards
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills and strong data storytelling skills
* Strong attention to detail and work ethic; and effectively manages multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment

  

The duties and responsibilities described here are not exhaustive and additional assignments, duties, or responsibilities may be required of this position. Assignments, duties, and responsibilities may be changed at any time, with or without notice, by Blackstone in its sole discretion.
Expected annual base salary range:
$110,000 \- $135,000
Actual base salary within that range will be determined by several components including but not limited to the individual's experience, skills, qualifications and job location. For roles located outside of the US, please disregard the posted salary bands as these roles will follow a separate compensation process based on local market comparables.  

  

Additional compensation and benefits offered in connection with the role consist of comprehensive health benefits, including but not limited to medical, dental, vision, and FSA benefits; paid time off; life insurance; 401(k) plan; and discretionary bonuses. Certain employees may also be eligible for equity and other incentive compensation at Blackstone’s sole discretion.
Blackstone is committed to providing equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, marital or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, disability, genetic predisposition, veteran or military status, status as a victim of domestic violence, a sex offense or stalking, or any other class or status in accordance with applicable federal, state and local laws. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including but not limited to hiring, placement, promotion, termination, transfer, leave of absence, compensation, and training. All Blackstone employees, including but not limited to recruiting personnel and hiring managers, are required to abide by this policy.
If you need a reasonable accommodation to complete your application, please contact Human Resources at 212\-583\-5000 (US), \+44 (0\)20 7451 4000 (EMEA) or \+852 3656 8600 (APAC).
Depending on the position, you may be required to obtain certain securities licenses if you are in a client facing role and/or if you are engaged in the following:* Attending client meetings where you are discussing Blackstone products and/or and client questions;
* Marketing Blackstone funds to new or existing clients;
* Supervising or training securities licensed employees;
* Structuring or creating Blackstone funds/products; and
* Advising on marketing plans prepared by a sales team or developing and/or contributing information for marketing materials.


Note: The above list is not the exhaustive list of activities requiring securities licenses and there may be roles that require review on a case\-by\-case basis. Please speak with your Blackstone Recruiting contact with any questions.  

  

To submit your application please complete the form below. Fields marked with a red asterisk \* must be completed to be considered for employment (although some can be answered "prefer not to say"). Failure to provide this information may compromise the follow\-up of your application. When you have finished click Submit at the bottom of this form.

jobs.title: Senior Marketing Operations Specialist, NA
jobs.description:
**LOCATION:** Fort Lauderdale FL
  

  

**THE ROLE:**  

A highly organized, marketing\-minded professional to support and optimize marketing operations for the North America team. This individual will play a key role in budget oversight, campaign execution, process management, and performance tracking. Strong communication skills, exceptional organization, and keen attention to detail are essential, as this role partners cross\-functionally with Field and Channel Marketing, Sales, Finance, and leadership to ensure alignment, accuracy, and successful program execution. This person will work closely with Field and Channel Marketing Managers (FCMMs) and the VP of Marketing for North America.  



  

**TASKS AND RESPONSIBILITIES:*** Owns and improves NA marketing operations processes
* Manages and tracks NA marketing budget performance
* Reviews Budget and MDF sheets for compliance
* Collects invoices and submits for processing
* Owns and maintains the NA marketing calendar
* Provides operational support for Field and Channel Marketing programs
* Manages send/retrieval of event marketing materials
* Oversees lead management in IGEL Marketing automation platform/CRM
* Analyzes and reports on campaign and event performance
* Maintains and updates the ABM target list
* Manages project timelines for content creation â€“ social assets
* Writes copy for campaigns and webinar promotions
* Engages with graphic design, sales, channel, and cross\-functional teams as needed
* Supports additional marketing initiatives as required

  

  

**EXPERIENCE AND QUALIFICATIONS:*** Associates Degree in business, marketing, etc.
* Proficiency in Excel, PowerPoint, and other applications in Microsoft Office
* Experience with CRM (such as Dynamics) and marketing tools/platform (such as Act\-On)
* Ability to handle logistics for field and channel marketing events
* Basic understanding of budget administration and calendar management

  

**Personal Skills:**  



* Demonstrates flexibility, integrity, and strong problem\-solving skills
* Builds collaborative, responsive working relationships across all levels of the organization
* Highly detail\-oriented with exceptional organizational and follow\-through skills
* Exhibits a proactive, accountable, and results\-driven work ethic
* Self\-starter who takes initiative and drives tasks to completion in fast\-paced environments
* Communicates clearly and consistently, ensuring timely responses and alignment across stakeholders

**Preferred Qualifications:**

* Bachelorâ€™s degree in Marketing, Business, or related field

* Strong analytical mindset with experience building reports and dashboards and translating data into insights
* Hands\-on experience with marketing automation and CRM platforms (e.g., Marketo, Salesforce)
* Experience managing and updating websites (e.g., WordPress or similar CMS platforms)
* Strong copywriting and editing skills for campaigns and promotions
* Solid understanding of the marketing funnel, lead management, and lead scoring methodologies
* Experience supporting budget tracking and MDF processes preferred

  

**WE OFFER:*** Health, dental, vision, and prescription benefits (employee premiums covered by IGEL)
* 11 company\-paid holidays per year
* 18\-22 days of PTO per year (18 days in year 1, 20 days starting in year 2 and 22 days after 5 years of service)
* Sick time of 10 days per year, with rollover of unused days
* 401(k) plan with 100% company match
* Paid maternity and paternity leave
* Monthly home office allowance
* Remote working opportunities and flexible working times, so you can combine your demanding work with your personal goals
* Employee Assistance Program (EAP) and Financial Wellness tool
* Company\-paid life insurance policy, long\-term disability (LTD), and short\-term disability (STD) coverage
* Wellbeing apps, including Rightway, Headspace and Wellhub
* Training and development opportunities to advance your career
* Presidentâ€™s Club for the highest performing salespeople and overachievers
* An amazing culture powered by a workplace run on trust, empowerment, and feedback with a positive, inspiring working atmosphere
* A highly motivated team that is already looking forward to your support in developing strategies and achieving common goals, together with you!

  

IGEL is an equal opportunity employer and makes employment decisions on the basis of merit. We want the best available person in every job. Our policy prohibits unlawful discrimination based on color, creed, sex, religion, marital status, age, national origin or ancestry, physical or mental disability, medical condition, sexual orientation, or any other consideration made unlawful by federal, state or local laws.

jobs.title: Director of Brand Marketing Operations
jobs.description:
Proper Hospitality is seeking a Director of Brand Marketing Operations to join our corporate team in Los Angeles.


**Position Overview**



The Director of Brand Marketing Operations is the leader and central coordinator for all brand marketing at Proper Hospitality. This role demands exceptional people leadership and operational excellence. You will inspire and develop a talented marketing team while ensuring workflows run efficiently, priorities are clear, and campaigns launch flawlessly.



Leading Marketing Managers, Creative Director, and Social Media Managers, you set the vision, build capabilities, and create an environment where your team thrives. You coach through challenges, remove obstacles, and hold everyone to high standards. Your leadership creates the momentum that drives marketing forward.



This role sits at the center of marketing operations, working closely with the Director of Growth and Performance Marketing, Creative Director, Marketing Managers (Collective and Proper), Social Media Managers, Digital Marketing Manager, and external partners to orchestrate seamless campaign execution. Additionally, this role partners closely with the Director of Growth \& Performance Marketing to ensure brand campaigns and performance marketing initiatives are coordinated and mutually reinforcing. The Director ensures projects move through the pipeline smoothly, resources are allocated effectively, bottlenecks are removed, and quality standards are maintained.



The ideal candidate is a natural leader who brings out the best in their team through clear direction and genuine support. You excel at building efficient processes, communicate clearly, and get satisfaction from launching campaigns on time and on brand. You are equally comfortable coaching a team member, solving an operational problem, or providing strategic input. Your team trusts you, and stakeholders count on you to deliver.


***Key Responsibilities***


**Team Leadership \& Development**


* Lead and develop Marketing Manager Collective, Marketing Manager Proper, Social Media Managers, and Creative Director
* Provide clear vision, strategic direction, and coaching that enables each team member to excel
* Set ambitious goals and hold team accountable for delivering excellent results
* Invest in professional development through regular 1:1s, feedback, and growth opportunities
* Build collaborative, results\-driven culture where people feel valued and challenged
* Recruit and retain top talent aligned with company culture
* Lead by example with strong work ethic, creativity, and collaborative spirit


**Marketing Workflow Coordination**


* Coordinate all marketing activities from initial brief through final delivery, keeping stakeholders aligned
* Manage marketing calendar across properties, brands, and campaigns to prevent conflicts and maximize impact
* Identify and remove bottlenecks to keep projects moving forward
* Run project kickoffs, status meetings, and reviews to ensure clarity and accountability
* Track project status and provide leadership visibility on progress and risks
* Coordinate with revenue, operations, membership, partnerships, and property teams
* Partner with Director of Growth \& Performance Marketing to align brand and performance initiatives


**Prioritization \& Resource Management**


* Work with SVP to establish marketing priorities and translate them into actionable plans
* Allocate team resources effectively across competing priorities and deadlines
* Make real\-time decisions on priority shifts and resource reallocation
* Balance strategic initiatives with tactical needs
* Flag resource constraints early before they become problems
* Coordinate workload to optimize output and prevent burnout


**Creative Team Coordination**


* Partner daily with Creative Director to coordinate design and content workload
* Manage creative briefing process with clear briefs and sufficient lead time
* Coordinate creative reviews and approvals between stakeholders and creative team
* Oversee production timelines for photoshoots, video, design, and written content
* Manage relationships with external creative contractors
* Ensure brand consistency across all creative deliverables
* Coordinate asset tracking and final file delivery


**Marketing Manager Support \& Collaboration**


* Support Marketing Managers Collective and Proper in executing their campaigns
* Help Marketing Managers secure resources and navigate dependencies
* Facilitate collaboration between both Marketing Managers on cross\-brand initiatives
* Remove obstacles blocking Marketing Managers from executing effectively
* Ensure Marketing Managers have clear priorities and adequate support


**Process \& Systems Management**


* Design and improve marketing workflows and standard operating procedures
* Manage marketing technology stack and project management tools
* Create templates and frameworks that improve efficiency
* Establish quality control checkpoints
* Identify opportunities to automate repetitive tasks


**Growth \& Performance Marketing Collaboration**


* Maintain regular coordination with Director of Growth \& Performance Marketing
* Align brand campaigns and performance initiatives in messaging, timing, and targeting
* Coordinate shared resources between brand and performance teams
* Share campaign calendars and provide visibility into brand marketing plans
* Collaborate on unified reporting frameworks


**What Success Looks Like**


* **Your team is thriving:** Team members are engaged, growing in their roles, and consistently delivering excellent work; retention is strong
* **People want to work for you:** Top talent is attracted to your team because you're known as a great leader who develops people
* **Things happen on time:** Campaigns launch when planned, deadlines are met consistently, and the team operates with predictable velocity
* **Priorities are clear:** Everyone knows what they should be working on and why it matters; there's no confusion about what's most important
* **Workflows run smoothly:** Projects move through creative, approvals, and production without getting stuck; handoffs are seamless
* **Quality is maintained:** Marketing deliverables consistently meet brand standards; mistakes are caught before launch
* **Team is productive:** Marketing and creative teams work efficiently without excessive overtime or constant fire drills
* **Stakeholders are satisfied:** Leadership, property teams, and partners trust that marketing operations will deliver as promised
* **You're the go\-to person:** When people need to know status, solve a problem, or get something moving, they come to you


***Qualifications***


**Required Experience**


* 7\-10\+ years in marketing with progressive leadership responsibility; must have at least 3\-5 years successfully leading and developing teams
* Demonstrated track record as a dynamic people leader with strong team engagement, development outcomes, and retention
* Proven experience managing complex, multi\-stakeholder marketing projects from concept through execution
* Track record working closely with creative teams, coordinating design and content production workflows
* Experience in luxury hospitality, lifestyle brands, or premium consumer sectors strongly preferred
* Background managing integrated campaigns across digital, social, content, and traditional channels


**Core Competencies \- You Must Excel At:**


* **Leading people:** You're a natural leader who inspires trust, builds genuine relationships, and brings out the best in every team member
* **Developing talent:** You invest in people's growth, provide honest feedback with care, and help team members reach their potential
* **Building culture:** You create an environment where people feel valued, challenged, and excited to do their best work
* **Driving accountability:** You set high standards and hold people (including yourself) accountable while maintaining respect and support
* **Communication:** You're crystal clear in written and verbal communication; you keep people informed without overcommunicating
* **Problem\-solving:** When obstacles arise, you quickly identify solutions and get things unstuck
* **Collaboration:** You build strong working relationships across teams and levels; people enjoy working with you
* **Follow\-through:** You're relentlessly reliable; when you say something will happen, it happens
* **Calm under pressure:** Marketing can be chaotic; you stay composed and keep the team focused when things get intense
* **Attention to detail:** You catch errors others miss and maintain high quality standards without slowing things down
* **Adaptability:** Priorities shift; you adjust quickly and help the team pivot smoothly


**Technical Requirements**


* Expert\-level proficiency with project management tools (Asana, Monday.com, Airtable, Smartsheet, or similar)
* Strong working knowledge of digital marketing channels and tactics (you need to understand what you're coordinating)
* Experience with marketing automation platforms, CRM systems, and digital asset management tools
* Comfortable with data and analytics; able to track performance metrics and report on operational KPIs
* Advanced skills in Microsoft Office Suite, Google Workspace, and presentation development


**Salary**


* $225,000\-250,000


**Why Join Proper Hospitality**



At Proper, we build experiences that move people — and that begins with the team behind them. As a best\-in\-class employer, we’re committed to creating one of the **Best Places to Work** in hospitality by nurturing a culture where creativity, excellence, and humanity thrive together.



Everything we do is grounded in the belief that hospitality is more than a profession \- it’s an opportunity to care for others and make lives better. Guided by the **Pillars of Proper**, we show up with warmth and authenticity (*Care Proper*), strive for excellence in everything we do (*Achieve Proper*), think creatively and resourcefully (*Imagine Proper*), and take pride in the style and culture that make us who we are (*Present Proper*).



We believe our people are our greatest strength, and we invest deeply in their wellbeing, growth, and sense of belonging. From comprehensive benefits to meaningful development programs, Proper is designed to help you build a career, and a life, that feels as inspiring as the experiences we create for our guests.

jobs.title: Marketing Technology Coordinator
jobs.description:
Summary:

The Marketing Technology Coordinator supports the firm’s marketing technology tools and related systems. The Coordinator is responsible for ensuring the integrity of the data within these systems and for executing the processes and procedures related to their use. The incumbent coordinates data quality management procedures and works closely with the appropriate resources to process contact, company, activity and other data. The incumbent contributes to the development and delivery of training programs as well as provides support to internal resources. Along with the rest of the Marketing Technology team, the Coordinator serves as an ambassador for the technology, people and processes related to marketing technology tools by encouraging its use and best practices.


Duties and Responsibilities:
* Ensure the overall quality of contact relationship data by examining contact data changes and encouraging CRM system best practices.
* Monitor and analyze data changes made by system users, including lawyers, secretaries and other operations personnel.
* Test all firm\-wide email campaigns, including client alerts, invitations, newsletters, surveys, holiday cards, etc.,
* Assist with daily data quality procedures, including checking for duplicate contacts, merging duplicate contacts, standardizing address data and ensuring that necessary data fields are populated.
* Research email bounce backs, and update contact information as appropriate.
* Provide day to day user support and trouble shooting assistance.
* Assist with data imports and exports.
* Produce analytical reports to support marketing initiatives.
* Provide varied project support as directed by the Marketing Technology Manager.

jobs.title: Senior Marketing Operations Specialist
jobs.description:
Uplight is creating a new category of energy. We make software that manages energy resources in homes and businesses—including things like smart thermostats, electric vehicles, solar panels, storage batteries, heat pumps, and even people’s behavior—to generate, shift, or save energy to balance the grid, making it more efficient and reliable. This creates clean energy capacity that can be used by the power grid instead of burning more fossil fuels. Our solutions accelerate the transition to clean energy and save money for energy customers.
Our Consumer Marketing Team is seeking a **Senior Marketing Operations Specialist** to join our team and help us achieve our ambitious goals for our business and the planet.**How you will make an impact:**  

Responsibilities include supporting our marketing operations team and utility clients through strategic management of programs and projects, website and campaign configuration, review and approvals management, data management, quality assurance and deployment across our online Marketplace websites and digital marketing channels. You will work as a member of our Agile marketing team to deliver ongoing marketing operations for our Marketplaces, increase on\-site conversions and execute highly targeted marketing campaigns with an emphasis on email marketing.
In this role, you’ll use your extreme attention to detail and your technical aptitude to build and deploy flawless campaigns to utility customers across a number of different accounts. You’ll manage complex clients focusing on client outcomes, Uplight goals, and strong decision\-making. If you’re interested in learning marketing and e\-commerce from the ground up, plus you have a passion for sustainability and teamwork, this job may be for you!**What you get to do:**

* Conduct day\-to day marketing operations tasks, including configuration, customization, quality assurance testing, data/list management and deployment of website content, marketing campaigns and post\-purchase messaging
* Manage and track utility approvals for new products, promotional participation and marketing content
* Support planning, operations and website updates for sales and promos
* Write tickets and project briefs for work to be completed by Marketing, E\-Commerce, Reporting, Engineering and Product squad members
* Support new client integrations with setup work in various professional ecosystems.
* Maintain hygienic email lists and develop customer segments to support a robust targeting and personalization strategy. Support utility\-specific list transfers and opt\-out management.
* Employ and analyze A/B and multivariate testing of different content strategies
* Collaborate with internal team members from Marketing, E\-commerce, Partner Success and Customer Support teams on projects and issues
* Proactively identify opportunities and risks and raise awareness to larger teams and propose potential solutions
* Operate with a high level of independence to maximize outcomes for the company and the client
* Train other employees on new process or onboarding as opportunities arise
* Own complex clients, focusing on client outcomes and Uplight goals
* Drives progress even in ambiguity and can work independently on routine tasks and as part of a team to achieve outcomes.

**What you bring to Uplight:*** You are a master multi\-tasker and can pivot from one task to the next to meet aggressive deadlines and juggle the demands of multiple clients
* You are great at prioritizing your to\-do list based on level of importance
* You have a strong attention to detail
* You are incredibly resourceful and look for answers first and ask questions when you need clarity
* You work well independently and in a group
* You confidentially present to internal and external stakeholders
* You love a good puzzle and can think through all the angles
* You’re committed to learning and adhering to copyright, CAN\-SPAM and other marketing\-related legal requirements and best practices
* You enjoy following (and improving!) operational processes
* You are an active communicator who can keep internal team members informed of new developments and contribute behind the scenes to client satisfaction
* You are a go\-getter. You take responsibility and run with it
* You love to take on new challenges and are not afraid to teach yourself new things
* You actively seek new opportunities within your workload—taking on new projects is something you love
* You thrive in unstructured environments and use that flexibility to your advantage
* Familiarity HTML/CSS, preferred
* Familiarity with outbound email platforms and ecommerce tools, preferred

**Why Join Uplight in Leading the Fight Against Climate Change?**
At Uplight, we're not just offering a job – we're offering a chance to be part of the solution to one of the world's biggest challenges. As a certified B Corporation, we're deeply committed to both social and environmental responsibility. Here's why you should join our team of passionate Uplighters:* **Make a Meaningful Impact:** Your work directly impacts our mission of decarbonization and building a more sustainable future.
* **Grow Your Career:** We offer ample advancement opportunities, robust learning and development programs, and a supportive team environment that fosters collaboration and innovation.
* **Thrive:** We offer comprehensive benefits, including flexible time off, generous parental leave, a wellness stipend, and work flexibility to help you thrive both personally and professionally.
* **Belong to an Inclusive Community:** We celebrate diversity and foster an inclusive workplace where everyone feels respected, empowered, and heard. Our Employee Resource Groups offer opportunities to connect with colleagues who share your interests and backgrounds.
* **Be Part of a Growing Movement:** Join a team of dedicated individuals who are passionate about creating a more sustainable future. We offer a collaborative environment where your ideas are valued and your contributions


Salary Range: $64,000 to $80,000 USD \+ bonus
Application Deadline: April 30, 2026
In accordance with the Colorado Equal Pay for Equal Work Act, the approximate annual base compensation range is listed above. The actual offer, reflecting the total compensation package and benefits, will be determined by a number of factors including the applicant's experience, knowledge, skills, and abilities, as well as internal equity among our team.
Uplight provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race (including hair texture and hairstyles), color, religion (including head coverings), age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws.

jobs.title: Marketing Data Specialist
jobs.description:
Marketing Data Specialist


**MailWell: Data Services**  

masterworks.agency

  

**General Description:**



The Marketing Data Specialist will leverage data management tools to clean, prepare and deliver audiences selected for marketing efforts. This role selects data based on written targeting strategies, works with our project management, client engagement and analytics teams to ensure accuracy, then prepares and delivers marketing files for use by a vendor or an internal marketing team.

  

**Primary Responsibilities:**


* Follow established processes to select audiences and prepare data for marketing efforts, delivering on the project requirements with a very high degree of accuracy.
* Incorporate data from multiple sources using a variety of data software tools which include Google BigQuery, Microsoft Excel, Matillion, and Magnus.
* QC data elements for projects received from clients, transformed data, final output file, and reports.
* Partner with developers, analysts, project management, and client engagement teams to gather documentation and instructions necessary to complete data projects on time.
* Create and update documentation on current processes for the data team and other agency teams, client contacts, and third\-party data partners.
* QC your work and the work of team members for data quality, process and accuracy.
* Troubleshoot data errors and anomalies, and provide recommended solutions.
* Perform ad\-hoc queries as needed to answer questions about the data.
* Create data query logic from non\-technical requests.
* Escalate any data or project issues to the Data Services Manager, as required.
  


**Professional Competencies:**


* An adaptable and resilient mindset, comfortable with a dynamic work environment and evolving technological landscape
* A very high level of attention to detail
* Excellent organizational skills
* Proactive communication
* Ability to multi\-task
* Ability to manage assigned tasks and projects to ensure they are completed on schedule
* Self\-directed and able to work at peak performance in a fast\-paced, production\-oriented environment
* A desire to work as part of a team in a collaborative environment
* Ability to think critically and think outside the box
* A commitment to continuous learning
  


**Technical Competencies:**


* Knowledge of database relationships and architecture
* Demonstrated ability to quickly learn and adapt to new software, tools, and technological advancements
* Proficiency with Google Workspace and Microsoft Office Suite
* Intermediate coding language skills (SQL, etc)
* Proficiency in data management tools appropriate to this position:
* + Relational database tools
	+ Data encryption and transfer technologies (SFTP or use of APIs)
	+ Cloud\-based data lake technologies such as Google Big Query
	+ Knowledge of basic direct mail and digital marketing metrics a plus

**Work Experience:**


* 1\+ years of experience in a related field (data science, database management, analytics, information technology, or marketing analytics)
  


**Educational Requirements:**


* Bachelor’s Degree in a related field (data science, data analytics, data technology, database management, software and data engineering, information technology, applied mathematics, computer science)

jobs.title: Director, Marketing Technology Architect
jobs.description:
Labcorp is hiring a Director, Marketing Technology Architect.

**Work Schedule**


This is a full\-time, exempt (salaried) position assigned to a First Shift schedule, with standard business hours of Monday through Friday, 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. in your local time zone. Business needs may occasionally require flexibility in work hours, including earlier, later, or additional hours, with reasonable notice provided when possible.


Applicants who live within 35 miles of either the Burlington, NC or Durham, NC location will follow a hybrid schedule. This schedule includes a minimum of three in\-office days per week at an assigned location, either Burlington or Durham, supporting both collaboration and flexibility.

**Responsibilities**

* Manage and execute all marketing\-technology related implementations and integration activities.
* Develop a deep understanding of the organization’s marketing technology stack.
* Provide solutions to either expand the use of existing tools or bring new tools to fill the business needs.
* Troubleshoot and resolve technical issues related to marketing technology.
* Support the Director of Marketing Technology in establishing governance and process to improve and roll out marketing technologies.
* Provide training and support to marketing team members on the use of marketing technology platforms.
* Stay current with industry trends, emerging technologies, and best practices in MarTech to recommend continuous improvements and innovations.

**Minimum Qualifications**

* Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Information Technology, Computer Science, or related field.
* 7 or more years of experience in Marketing Technology architecture, implementation, and management.
* 1 or more years of experience in integration of Adobe product suite.
* 1 or mor years of experience leading global teams.

**Preferred Qualifications**

* 1 or more years of experience in the diagnostic or biopharma industry.

**Additional Qualifications**

* Current or previous Labcorp experience.
* Proficiency in implementing and managing marketing technology platforms, including CRM (SalesForce), marketing automation (Eloqua), analytics (Adobe Customer Journey Analytics)
* Solid understanding of data integrations
* Strong analytical, problem\-solving, and organizational skills with keen attention to detail.
* Excellent communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively across diverse teams and stakeholders.
* Self\-motivated with the ability to work independently and manage multiple projects simultaneously.
* A passion for innovation, continuous learning, and staying abreast of industry trends in Marketing Technology and Digital Asset Management.


Labcorp is a global life sciences and healthcare company harnessing science for human good. Every day, our mission is to improve health and improve lives. We are seeking a Marketing Technology Architect to join our dynamic team.


In this role, you will design, implement, and maintain our marketing technology stack to drive business growth and optimize marketing performance. You will collaborate closely with marketing, sales, and IT teams to ensure our technology solutions are effectively utilized and aligned with our business objectives.

**Benefits:** Employees regularly scheduled to work 20 or more hours per week are eligible for comprehensive benefits including: Medical, Dental, Vision, Life, STD/LTD, 401(k), Paid Time Off (PTO) or Flexible Time Off (FTO), Tuition Reimbursement and Employee Stock Purchase Plan. Casual, PRN \& Part Time employees regularly scheduled to work less than 20 hours are eligible to participate in the 401(k) Plan only. Employees who are regularly scheduled to work a 7 on 7 off schedule are eligible to receive all the foregoing benefits except PTO or FTO. For more detailed information, please click here.

**Labcorp is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer:**


Labcorp strives for inclusion and belonging in the workforce and does not tolerate harassment or discrimination of any kind. We make employment decisions based on the needs of our business and the qualifications and merit of the individual. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), family or parental status, marital, civil union or domestic partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, personal appearance, age, veteran status, disability, genetic information, or any other legally protected characteristic. Additionally, all qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with applicable law.

**We encourage all to apply**


If you are an individual with a disability who needs assistance using our online tools to search and apply for jobs, or needs an accommodation, please visit our accessibility site or contact us at Labcorp Accessibility. For more information about how we collect and store your personal data, please see our Privacy Statement.
